BATT7_ARM_MAH.label: Required arming remaining capacity
BATT7_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT7_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT7_ARM_VOLT.label: Required arming voltage
BATT7_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATT7_CAPACITY.label: Battery capacity
BATT7_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATT7_CHANNEL.label: INA3221 channel
BATT7_CHANNEL.description: INA3221 channel to return data for

BATT7_CRT_MAH.label: Battery critical capacity
BATT7_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT7_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT7_CRT_VOLT.label: Critical battery voltage
BATT7_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT7_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT7_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT7_CURR_MULT.label: Scales reported power monitor current
BATT7_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATT7_CURR_PIN.label: Battery Current sensing pin
BATT7_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT7_ESC_INDEX.label: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATT7_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATT7_ESC_MASK.label: ESC mask
BATT7_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATT7_FL_FLTR.label: Fuel level filter frequency
BATT7_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATT7_FL_PIN.label: Fuel level analog pin number
BATT7_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT7_FL_VLT_MIN.label: Empty fuel level voltage
BATT7_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT7_FL_V_MULT.label: Fuel level voltage multiplier
BATT7_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT7_FS_CRT_ACT.label: Critical battery failsafe action
BATT7_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATT7_FS_LOW_ACT.label: Low battery failsafe action
BATT7_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATT7_FS_VOLTSRC.label: Failsafe voltage source
BATT7_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATT7_I2C_ADDR.label: Battery monitor I2C address
BATT7_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATT7_LOW_MAH.label: Low battery capacity
BATT7_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT7_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT7_LOW_TIMER.label: Low voltage timeout
BATT7_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATT7_LOW_VOLT.label: Low battery voltage
BATT7_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT7_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT7_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT7_MAX_AMPS.label: Battery monitor max current
BATT7_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATT7_MAX_VOLT.label: Maximum Battery Voltage
BATT7_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATT7_MONITOR.label: Battery monitoring
BATT7_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATT7_OPTIONS.label: Battery monitor options
BATT7_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

options.label: MPPT reports input voltage and current
Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
Battery is for internal autopilot use only
Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATT7_SERIAL_NUM.label: Battery serial number
BATT7_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATT7_SHUNT.label: Battery monitor shunt resistor
BATT7_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATT7_SUM_MASK.label: Battery Sum mask
BATT7_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATT7_VLT_OFFSET.label: Volage offset
BATT7_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATT7_VOLT_MULT.label: Voltage Multiplier
BATT7_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT7_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATT7_VOLT_PIN.label: Battery Voltage sensing pin on the AD7091R5 Ic
BATT7_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATT8_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATTB_AMP_OFFSET.label: AMP offset
BATTB_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATTB_AMP_PERVLT.label: Amps per volt
BATTB_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATTB_ARM_MAH.label: Required arming remaining capacity
BATTB_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTB_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATTB_ARM_VOLT.label: Required arming voltage
BATTB_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATTB_CAPACITY.label: Battery capacity
BATTB_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATTB_CHANNEL.label: INA3221 channel
BATTB_CHANNEL.description: INA3221 channel to return data for

BATTB_CRT_MAH.label: Battery critical capacity
BATTB_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTB_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTB_CRT_VOLT.label: Critical battery voltage
BATTB_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTB_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTB_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTB_CURR_MULT.label: Scales reported power monitor current
BATTB_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATTB_CURR_PIN.label: Battery Current sensing pin
BATTB_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATTB_ESC_INDEX.label: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATTB_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATTB_ESC_MASK.label: ESC mask
BATTB_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATTB_FL_FLTR.label: Fuel level filter frequency
BATTB_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATTB_FL_PIN.label: Fuel level analog pin number
BATTB_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATTB_FL_VLT_MIN.label: Empty fuel level voltage
BATTB_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTB_FL_V_MULT.label: Fuel level voltage multiplier
BATTB_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTB_FS_CRT_ACT.label: Critical battery failsafe action
BATTB_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATTB_FS_LOW_ACT.label: Low battery failsafe action
BATTB_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATTB_FS_VOLTSRC.label: Failsafe voltage source
BATTB_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATTB_I2C_ADDR.label: Battery monitor I2C address
BATTB_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATTB_LOW_MAH.label: Low battery capacity
BATTB_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTB_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTB_LOW_TIMER.label: Low voltage timeout
BATTB_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATTB_LOW_VOLT.label: Low battery voltage
BATTB_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTB_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTB_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTB_MAX_AMPS.label: Battery monitor max current
BATTB_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATTB_MAX_VOLT.label: Maximum Battery Voltage
BATTB_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATTB_MONITOR.label: Battery monitoring
BATTB_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATTB_OPTIONS.label: Battery monitor options
BATTB_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

options.label: MPPT reports input voltage and current
Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
Battery is for internal autopilot use only
Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATTB_SERIAL_NUM.label: Battery serial number
BATTB_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATTB_SHUNT.label: Battery monitor shunt resistor
BATTB_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATTB_SUM_MASK.label: Battery Sum mask
BATTB_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATTB_VLT_OFFSET.label: Volage offset
BATTB_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATTB_VOLT_MULT.label: Voltage Multiplier
BATTB_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATTB_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATTB_VOLT_PIN.label: Battery Voltage sensing pin on the AD7091R5 Ic
BATTB_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATTE_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATTE_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

BATTE_VOLT_PIN.label: Battery Voltage sensing pin on the AD7091R5 Ic
BATTE_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATTF_AMP_OFFSET.label: AMP offset
BATTF_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

BATTF_AMP_PERVLT.label: Amps per volt
BATTF_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

BATTF_ARM_MAH.label: Required arming remaining capacity
BATTF_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTF_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATTF_ARM_VOLT.label: Required arming voltage
BATTF_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATTF_CAPACITY.label: Battery capacity
BATTF_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATTF_CHANNEL.label: INA3221 channel
BATTF_CHANNEL.description: INA3221 channel to return data for

BATTF_CRT_MAH.label: Battery critical capacity
BATTF_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTF_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTF_CRT_VOLT.label: Critical battery voltage
BATTF_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTF_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTF_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTF_CURR_MULT.label: Scales reported power monitor current
BATTF_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATTF_CURR_PIN.label: Battery Current sensing pin
BATTF_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

BATTF_ESC_INDEX.label: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATTF_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATTF_ESC_MASK.label: ESC mask
BATTF_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATTF_FL_FLTR.label: Fuel level filter frequency
BATTF_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATTF_FL_PIN.label: Fuel level analog pin number
BATTF_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATTF_FL_VLT_MIN.label: Empty fuel level voltage
BATTF_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTF_FL_V_MULT.label: Fuel level voltage multiplier
BATTF_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTF_FS_CRT_ACT.label: Critical battery failsafe action
BATTF_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATTF_FS_LOW_ACT.label: Low battery failsafe action
BATTF_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATTF_FS_VOLTSRC.label: Failsafe voltage source
BATTF_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATTF_I2C_ADDR.label: Battery monitor I2C address
BATTF_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATTF_LOW_MAH.label: Low battery capacity
BATTF_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTF_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTF_LOW_TIMER.label: Low voltage timeout
BATTF_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATTF_LOW_VOLT.label: Low battery voltage
BATTF_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTF_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTF_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTF_MAX_AMPS.label: Battery monitor max current
BATTF_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

BATTF_MAX_VOLT.label: Maximum Battery Voltage
BATTF_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATTF_MONITOR.label: Battery monitoring
BATTF_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATTF_OPTIONS.label: Battery monitor options
BATTF_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

options.label: MPPT reports input voltage and current
Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
Battery is for internal autopilot use only
Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average
Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap

BATTF_SERIAL_NUM.label: Battery serial number
BATTF_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATTF_SHUNT.label: Battery monitor shunt resistor
BATTF_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATTF_SUM_MASK.label: Battery Sum mask
BATTF_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATTF_VLT_OFFSET.label: Volage offset
BATTF_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

BATTF_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

BCN_ALT.label: Beacon origin's altitude above sealevel in meters
BCN_ALT.description: Beacon origin's altitude above sealevel in meters

BCN_ORIENT_YAW.label: Beacon systems rotation from north in degrees
BCN_ORIENT_YAW.description: Beacon systems rotation from north in degrees

BCN_TYPE.label: Beacon based position estimation device type
BCN_TYPE.description: What type of beacon based position estimation device is connected

BRD_ALT_CONFIG.label: Alternative HW config
BRD_ALT_CONFIG.description: Select an alternative hardware configuration. A value of zero selects the default configuration for this board. Other values are board specific. Please see the documentation for your board for details on any alternative configuration values that may be available.

BRD_BOOT_DELAY.label: Boot delay
BRD_BOOT_DELAY.description: This adds a delay in milliseconds to boot to ensure peripherals initialise fully

BRD_HEAT_LOWMGN.label: Board heater temp lower margin
BRD_HEAT_LOWMGN.description: Arming check will fail if temp is lower than this margin below BRD_HEAT_TARG. 0 disables the low temperature check

BRD_HEAT_TARG.label: Board heater temperature target
BRD_HEAT_TARG.description: Board heater target temperature for boards with controllable heating units. Set to -1 to disable the heater, please reboot after setting to -1.

BRD_IDLE_STATS.label: Capture and calculate true CPU load using idle threads
BRD_IDLE_STATS.description: Capture and calculate true CPU load using idle threads

BRD_IO_DSHOT.label: Load DShot FW on IO
BRD_IO_DSHOT.description: This loads the DShot firmware on the IO co-processor

BRD_IO_ENABLE.label: Enable IO co-processor
BRD_IO_ENABLE.description: This allows for the IO co-processor on boards with an IOMCU to be disabled. Setting to 2 will enable the IOMCU but not attempt to update firmware on startup

options.label: Write protect firmware flash on reboot
Write protect bootloader flash on reboot
Disable board arming gpio output change on arm/disarm

BRD_PWM_VOLT_SEL.label: Set PWM Out Voltage
BRD_PWM_VOLT_SEL.description: This sets the voltage max for PWM output pulses. 0 for 3.3V and 1 for 5V output. On boards with an IOMCU that support this parameter this option only affects the 8 main outputs, not the 6 auxiliary outputs. Using 5V output can help to reduce the impact of ESC noise interference corrupting signals to the ESCs.

BRD_RADIO_ABLVL.label: Auto-bind level
BRD_RADIO_ABLVL.description: This sets the minimum RSSI of an auto-bind packet for it to be accepted. This should be set so that auto-bind will only happen at short range to minimise the change of an auto-bind happening accidentially

BRD_RADIO_ABTIME.label: Auto-bind time
BRD_RADIO_ABTIME.description: When non-zero this sets the time with no transmitter packets before we start looking for auto-bind packets.

BRD_RADIO_BZOFS.label: Transmitter buzzer adjustment
BRD_RADIO_BZOFS.description: Set transmitter buzzer note adjustment (adjust frequency up)

BRD_RADIO_FCCTST.label: Put radio into FCC test mode
BRD_RADIO_FCCTST.description: If this is enabled then the radio will continuously transmit as required for FCC testing. The transmit channel is set by the value of the parameter. The radio will not work for RC input while this is enabled

BRD_RADIO_PPSCH.label: Packet rate channel
BRD_RADIO_PPSCH.description: Channel to show received packet-per-second rate, or zero for disabled

BRD_RADIO_SIGCH.label: RSSI signal strength
BRD_RADIO_SIGCH.description: Channel to show receive RSSI signal strength, or zero for disabled

BRD_RADIO_STKMD.label: Stick input mode
BRD_RADIO_STKMD.description: This selects between different stick input modes. The default is mode2, which has throttle on the left stick and pitch on the right stick. You can instead set mode1, which has throttle on the right stick and pitch on the left stick.

BRD_RADIO_TELEM.label: Enable telemetry
BRD_RADIO_TELEM.description: If this is non-zero then telemetry packets will be sent over DSM

BRD_RADIO_TESTCH.label: Set radio to factory test channel
BRD_RADIO_TESTCH.description: This sets the radio to a fixed test channel for factory testing. Using a fixed channel avoids the need for binding in factory testing.

BRD_RADIO_TPPSCH.label: Telemetry PPS channel
BRD_RADIO_TPPSCH.description: Channel to show telemetry packets-per-second value, as received at TX

BRD_RADIO_TSIGCH.label: RSSI value channel for telemetry data on transmitter
BRD_RADIO_TSIGCH.description: Channel to show telemetry RSSI value as received by TX

BRD_RADIO_TXMAX.label: Transmitter transmit power
BRD_RADIO_TXMAX.description: Set transmitter maximum transmit power (from 1 to 8)

BRD_RADIO_TXPOW.label: Telemetry Transmit power
BRD_RADIO_TXPOW.description: Set telemetry transmit power. This is the power level (from 1 to 8) for telemetry packets sent from the RX to the TX

BRD_RADIO_TYPE.label: Set type of direct attached radio
BRD_RADIO_TYPE.description: This enables support for direct attached radio receivers

BRD_RTC_TYPES.label: Allowed sources of RTC time
BRD_RTC_TYPES.description: Specifies which sources of UTC time will be accepted

BRD_RTC_TZ_MIN.label: Timezone offset from UTC
BRD_RTC_TZ_MIN.description: Adds offset in +- minutes from UTC to calculate local time

BRD_SAFETYOPTION.label: Options for safety button behavior
BRD_SAFETYOPTION.description: This controls the activation of the safety button. It allows you to control if the safety button can be used for safety enable and/or disable, and whether the button is only active when disarmed

options.label: Force safety on when the aircraft disarms

BRD_SAFETY_DEFLT.label: Sets default state of the safety switch
BRD_SAFETY_DEFLT.description: This controls the default state of the safety switch at startup. When set to 1 the safety switch will start in the safe state (flashing) at boot. When set to zero the safety switch will start in the unsafe state (solid) at startup. Note that if a safety switch is fitted the user can still control the safety state after startup using the switch. The safety state can also be controlled in software using a MAVLink message.

BRD_SAFETY_MASK.label: Outputs which ignore the safety switch state
BRD_SAFETY_MASK.description: A bitmask which controls what outputs can move while the safety switch has not been pressed

BRD_SBUS_OUT.label: SBUS output rate
BRD_SBUS_OUT.description: This sets the SBUS output frame rate in Hz

BRD_SD_FENCE.label: SDCard Fence size
BRD_SD_FENCE.description: This sets the amount of storage in kilobytes reserved on the microsd card in fence.stg for fence storage.

BRD_SD_MISSION.label: SDCard Mission size
BRD_SD_MISSION.description: This sets the amount of storage in kilobytes reserved on the microsd card in mission.stg for waypoint storage. Each waypoint uses 15 bytes.

BRD_SD_SLOWDOWN.label: microSD slowdown
BRD_SD_SLOWDOWN.description: This is a scaling factor to slow down microSD operation. It can be used on flight board and microSD card combinations where full speed is not reliable. For normal full speed operation a value of 0 should be used.

BRD_SER1_RTSCTS.label: Serial 1 flow control
BRD_SER1_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 1 (telemetry 1). You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup. Note that the PX4v1 does not have hardware flow control pins on this port, so you should leave this disabled.

BRD_SER2_RTSCTS.label: Serial 2 flow control
BRD_SER2_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 2 (telemetry 2). You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER3_RTSCTS.label: Serial 3 flow control
BRD_SER3_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 3. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER4_RTSCTS.label: Serial 4 flow control
BRD_SER4_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 4. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER5_RTSCTS.label: Serial 5 flow control
BRD_SER5_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 5. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER6_RTSCTS.label: Serial 6 flow control
BRD_SER6_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 6. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER7_RTSCTS.label: Serial 7 flow control
BRD_SER7_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 7. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SER8_RTSCTS.label: Serial 8 flow control
BRD_SER8_RTSCTS.description: Enable flow control on serial 8. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

BRD_SERIAL_NUM.label: User-defined serial number
BRD_SERIAL_NUM.description: User-defined serial number of this vehicle, it can be any arbitrary number you want and has no effect on the autopilot

BRD_TYPE.label: Board type
BRD_TYPE.description: This allows selection of a PX4 or VRBRAIN board type. If set to zero then the board type is auto-detected (PX4)

BRD_VBUS_MIN.label: Autopilot board voltage requirement
BRD_VBUS_MIN.description: Minimum voltage on the autopilot power rail to allow the aircraft to arm. 0 to disable the check.

BRD_VSERVO_MIN.label: Servo voltage requirement
BRD_VSERVO_MIN.description: Minimum voltage on the servo rail to allow the aircraft to arm. 0 to disable the check.

BTAG_CUR_CYCLES.label: current battery cycles
BTAG_CUR_CYCLES.description: this is the highest value for battery cycles for all connected batteries

BTN_ENABLE.label: Enable button reporting
BTN_ENABLE.description: This enables the button checking module. When this is disabled the parameters for setting button inputs are not visible

BTN_FUNC1.label: Button Pin 1 RC Channel function
BTN_FUNC1.description: Auxiliary RC Options function executed on pin change

options.label: Test autotuned gains after tune is complete

BTN_FUNC2.label: Button Pin 2 RC Channel function
BTN_FUNC2.description: Auxiliary RC Options function executed on pin change

options.label: Test autotuned gains after tune is complete

BTN_FUNC3.label: Button Pin 3 RC Channel function
BTN_FUNC3.description: Auxiliary RC Options function executed on pin change

options.label: Test autotuned gains after tune is complete

BTN_FUNC4.label: Button Pin 4 RC Channel function
BTN_FUNC4.description: Auxiliary RC Options function executed on pin change

options.label: Test autotuned gains after tune is complete

BTN_OPTIONS1.label: Button Pin 1 Options
BTN_OPTIONS1.description: Options for Pin 1. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_OPTIONS2.label: Button Pin 2 Options
BTN_OPTIONS2.description: Options for Pin 2. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_OPTIONS3.label: Button Pin 3 Options
BTN_OPTIONS3.description: Options for Pin 3. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_OPTIONS4.label: Button Pin 4 Options
BTN_OPTIONS4.description: Options for Pin 4. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

BTN_PIN1.label: First button Pin
BTN_PIN1.description: Digital pin number for first button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_PIN2.label: Second button Pin
BTN_PIN2.description: Digital pin number for second button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_PIN3.label: Third button Pin
BTN_PIN3.description: Digital pin number for third button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_PIN4.label: Fourth button Pin
BTN_PIN4.description: Digital pin number for fourth button input. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

BTN_REPORT_SEND.label: Report send time
BTN_REPORT_SEND.description: The duration in seconds that a BUTTON_CHANGE report is repeatedly sent to the GCS regarding a button changing state. Note that the BUTTON_CHANGE message is MAVLink2 only.

CAM1_DURATION.label: Camera shutter duration held open
CAM1_DURATION.description: Duration in seconds that the camera shutter is held open

CAM1_FEEDBAK_PIN.label: Camera feedback pin
CAM1_FEEDBAK_PIN.description: pin number to use for save accurate camera feedback messages. If set to -1 then don't use a pin flag for this, otherwise this is a pin number which if held high after a picture trigger order, will save camera messages when camera really takes a picture. A universal camera hot shoe is needed. The pin should be held high for at least 2 milliseconds for reliable trigger detection.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot. See also the CAMx_FEEDBCK_POL option.

CAM1_FEEDBAK_POL.label: Camera feedback pin polarity
CAM1_FEEDBAK_POL.description: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the feedback pin should go high on trigger. If set to 0 then it should go low

CAM1_HFOV.label: Camera horizontal field of view
CAM1_HFOV.description: Camera horizontal field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM1_INTRVAL_MIN.label: Camera minimum time interval between photos
CAM1_INTRVAL_MIN.description: Postpone shooting if previous picture was taken less than this many seconds ago

CAM1_MNT_INST.label: Camera Mount instance
CAM1_MNT_INST.description: Mount instance camera is associated with. 0 means camera and mount have identical instance numbers e.g. camera1 and mount1

options.label: Recording Starts at arming and stops at disarming

CAM1_RC_BTN_DELY.label: RunCam button delay before allowing further button presses
CAM1_RC_BTN_DELY.description: Time it takes for the a RunCam button press to be actived in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM1_RC_BT_DELAY.label: RunCam boot delay before allowing updates
CAM1_RC_BT_DELAY.description: Time it takes for the RunCam to become fully ready in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM1_RC_CONTROL.label: RunCam control option
CAM1_RC_CONTROL.description: Specifies the allowed actions required to enter the OSD menu and other option like autorecording

CAM1_RC_FEATURES.label: RunCam features available
CAM1_RC_FEATURES.description: The available features of the attached RunCam device. If 0 then the RunCam device will be queried for the features it supports, otherwise this setting is used.

CAM1_RC_MDE_DELY.label: RunCam mode delay before allowing further button presses
CAM1_RC_MDE_DELY.description: Time it takes for the a RunCam mode button press to be actived in ms. If a mode change first requires a video recording change then double this value is used. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM1_RC_TYPE.label: RunCam device type
CAM1_RC_TYPE.description: RunCam device type used to determine OSD menu structure and shutter options.

CAM1_RELAY_ON.label: Camera relay ON value
CAM1_RELAY_ON.description: This sets whether the relay goes high or low when it triggers. Note that you should also set RELAY_DEFAULT appropriately for your camera

CAM1_SERVO_OFF.label: Camera servo OFF PWM value
CAM1_SERVO_OFF.description: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is deactivated

CAM1_SERVO_ON.label: Camera servo ON PWM value
CAM1_SERVO_ON.description: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is activated

CAM1_TRIGG_DIST.label: Camera trigger distance
CAM1_TRIGG_DIST.description: Distance in meters between camera triggers. If this value is non-zero then the camera will trigger whenever the position changes by this number of meters regardless of what mode the APM is in. Note that this parameter can also be set in an auto mission using the DO_SET_CAM_TRIGG_DIST command, allowing you to enable/disable the triggering of the camera during the flight.

CAM1_TYPE.label: Camera shutter (trigger) type
CAM1_TYPE.description: how to trigger the camera to take a picture

CAM1_VFOV.label: Camera vertical field of view
CAM1_VFOV.description: Camera vertical field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM2_DURATION.label: Camera shutter duration held open
CAM2_DURATION.description: Duration in seconds that the camera shutter is held open

CAM2_FEEDBAK_PIN.label: Camera feedback pin
CAM2_FEEDBAK_PIN.description: pin number to use for save accurate camera feedback messages. If set to -1 then don't use a pin flag for this, otherwise this is a pin number which if held high after a picture trigger order, will save camera messages when camera really takes a picture. A universal camera hot shoe is needed. The pin should be held high for at least 2 milliseconds for reliable trigger detection.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot. See also the CAMx_FEEDBCK_POL option.

CAM2_FEEDBAK_POL.label: Camera feedback pin polarity
CAM2_FEEDBAK_POL.description: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the feedback pin should go high on trigger. If set to 0 then it should go low

CAM2_HFOV.label: Camera horizontal field of view
CAM2_HFOV.description: Camera horizontal field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM2_INTRVAL_MIN.label: Camera minimum time interval between photos
CAM2_INTRVAL_MIN.description: Postpone shooting if previous picture was taken less than this many seconds ago

CAM2_MNT_INST.label: Camera Mount instance
CAM2_MNT_INST.description: Mount instance camera is associated with. 0 means camera and mount have identical instance numbers e.g. camera1 and mount1

options.label: Recording Starts at arming and stops at disarming

CAM2_RC_BTN_DELY.label: RunCam button delay before allowing further button presses
CAM2_RC_BTN_DELY.description: Time it takes for the a RunCam button press to be actived in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM2_RC_BT_DELAY.label: RunCam boot delay before allowing updates

CAM2_RC_CONTROL.label: RunCam control option
CAM2_RC_CONTROL.description: Specifies the allowed actions required to enter the OSD menu and other option like autorecording

CAM2_RC_FEATURES.label: RunCam features available
CAM2_RC_FEATURES.description: The available features of the attached RunCam device. If 0 then the RunCam device will be queried for the features it supports, otherwise this setting is used.

CAM2_RC_MDE_DELY.label: RunCam mode delay before allowing further button presses
CAM2_RC_MDE_DELY.description: Time it takes for the a RunCam mode button press to be actived in ms. If a mode change first requires a video recording change then double this value is used. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.

CAM2_RC_TYPE.label: RunCam device type
CAM2_RC_TYPE.description: RunCam device type used to determine OSD menu structure and shutter options.

CAM2_RELAY_ON.label: Camera relay ON value
CAM2_RELAY_ON.description: This sets whether the relay goes high or low when it triggers. Note that you should also set RELAY_DEFAULT appropriately for your camera

CAM2_SERVO_OFF.label: Camera servo OFF PWM value
CAM2_SERVO_OFF.description: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is deactivated

CAM2_SERVO_ON.label: Camera servo ON PWM value
CAM2_SERVO_ON.description: PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is activated

CAM2_TRIGG_DIST.label: Camera trigger distance
CAM2_TRIGG_DIST.description: Distance in meters between camera triggers. If this value is non-zero then the camera will trigger whenever the position changes by this number of meters regardless of what mode the APM is in. Note that this parameter can also be set in an auto mission using the DO_SET_CAM_TRIGG_DIST command, allowing you to enable/disable the triggering of the camera during the flight.

CAM2_TYPE.label: Camera shutter (trigger) type
CAM2_TYPE.description: how to trigger the camera to take a picture

CAM2_VFOV.label: Camera vertical field of view
CAM2_VFOV.description: Camera vertical field of view. 0 if unknown

CAM_AUTO_ONLY.label: Distance-trigging in AUTO mode only
CAM_AUTO_ONLY.description: When enabled, trigging by distance is done in AUTO mode only.

CAM_MAX_ROLL.label: Maximum photo roll angle.
CAM_MAX_ROLL.description: Postpone shooting if roll is greater than limit. (0=Disable, will shoot regardless of roll).

CAN_D1_PC_ECU_ID.label: ECU Node ID
CAN_D1_PC_ECU_ID.description: Node ID to send ECU throttle messages to. Set to zero to disable ECU throttle messages. Set to 255 to broadcast to all ECUs.

CAN_D1_PC_ECU_RT.label: ECU command output rate
CAN_D1_PC_ECU_RT.description: Output rate of ECU command messages

CAN_D1_PC_ESC_BM.label: ESC channels
CAN_D1_PC_ESC_BM.description: Bitmask defining which ESC (motor) channels are to be transmitted over Piccolo CAN

CAN_D1_PC_ESC_RT.label: ESC output rate
CAN_D1_PC_ESC_RT.description: Output rate of ESC command messages

CAN_D1_PC_SRV_BM.label: Servo channels
CAN_D1_PC_SRV_BM.description: Bitmask defining which servo channels are to be transmitted over Piccolo CAN

CAN_D1_PC_SRV_RT.label: Servo command output rate
CAN_D1_PC_SRV_RT.description: Output rate of servo command messages

CAN_D1_PROTOCOL.label: Enable use of specific protocol over virtual driver
CAN_D1_PROTOCOL.description: Enabling this option starts selected protocol that will use this virtual driver

CAN_D1_PROTOCOL2.label: Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressing
CAN_D1_PROTOCOL2.description: Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressing

CAN_D1_UC_ESC_BM.label: Output channels to be transmitted as ESC over DroneCAN
CAN_D1_UC_ESC_BM.description: Bitmask with one set for channel to be transmitted as a ESC command over DroneCAN

CAN_D1_UC_ESC_OF.label: ESC Output channels offset
CAN_D1_UC_ESC_OF.description: Offset for ESC numbering in DroneCAN ESC RawCommand messages. This allows for more efficient packing of ESC command messages. If your ESCs are on servo outputs 5 to 8 and you set this parameter to 4 then the ESC RawCommand will be sent with the first 4 slots filled. This can be used for more efficient usage of CAN bandwidth

CAN_D1_UC_ESC_RV.label: Bitmask for output channels for reversible ESCs over DroneCAN.
CAN_D1_UC_ESC_RV.description: Bitmask with one set for each output channel that uses a reversible ESC over DroneCAN. Reversible ESCs use both positive and negative values in RawCommands, with positive commanding the forward direction and negative commanding the reverse direction.

CAN_D1_UC_NODE.label: Own node ID
CAN_D1_UC_NODE.description: DroneCAN node ID used by the driver itself on this network

CAN_D1_UC_NTF_RT.label: Notify State rate
CAN_D1_UC_NTF_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for Notify State Message

CAN_D1_UC_POOL.label: CAN pool size
CAN_D1_UC_POOL.description: Amount of memory in bytes to allocate for the DroneCAN memory pool. More memory is needed for higher CAN bus loads

CAN_D1_UC_RLY_RT.label: DroneCAN relay output rate
CAN_D1_UC_RLY_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for relay outputs, note that this rate is per message each message does 1 relay, so if with more relays will take longer to update at the same rate, a extra message will be sent when a relay changes state

CAN_D1_UC_S1_BD.label: DroneCAN Serial default baud rate
CAN_D1_UC_S1_BD.description: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_D1_UC_S1_IDX.label: DroneCAN Serial1 index
CAN_D1_UC_S1_IDX.description: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D1_UC_S1_NOD.label: Serial CAN remote node number
CAN_D1_UC_S1_NOD.description: CAN remote node number for serial port

CAN_D1_UC_S1_PRO.label: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port
CAN_D1_UC_S1_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D1_UC_S2_BD.description: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_D1_UC_S2_NOD.description: CAN remote node number for serial port

CAN_D1_UC_S2_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D1_UC_SRV_BM.description: Bitmask with one set for channel to be transmitted as a servo command over DroneCAN

CAN_D1_UC_SRV_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for servo outputs

This can be used for more efficient usage of CAN bandwidth

CAN_D3_UC_ESC_RV.label: Bitmask for output channels for reversible ESCs over DroneCAN.
CAN_D3_UC_ESC_RV.description: Bitmask with one set for each output channel that uses a reversible ESC over DroneCAN. Reversible ESCs use both positive and negative values in RawCommands, with positive commanding the forward direction and negative commanding the reverse direction.

CAN_D3_UC_NODE.label: Own node ID
CAN_D3_UC_NODE.description: DroneCAN node ID used by the driver itself on this network

CAN_D3_UC_NTF_RT.label: Notify State rate
CAN_D3_UC_NTF_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for Notify State Message

CAN_D3_UC_POOL.label: CAN pool size
CAN_D3_UC_POOL.description: Amount of memory in bytes to allocate for the DroneCAN memory pool. More memory is needed for higher CAN bus loads

CAN_D3_UC_RLY_RT.label: DroneCAN relay output rate
CAN_D3_UC_RLY_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for relay outputs, note that this rate is per message each message does 1 relay, so if with more relays will take longer to update at the same rate, a extra message will be sent when a relay changes state

CAN_D3_UC_S1_BD.label: DroneCAN Serial default baud rate
CAN_D3_UC_S1_BD.description: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S1_IDX.label: DroneCAN Serial1 index
CAN_D3_UC_S1_IDX.description: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S1_NOD.label: Serial CAN remote node number
CAN_D3_UC_S1_NOD.description: CAN remote node number for serial port

CAN_D3_UC_S1_PRO.label: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port
CAN_D3_UC_S1_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D3_UC_S2_BD.label: DroneCAN Serial default baud rate
CAN_D3_UC_S2_BD.description: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S2_IDX.label: Serial port number on remote CAN node
CAN_D3_UC_S2_IDX.description: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S2_NOD.label: Serial CAN remote node number
CAN_D3_UC_S2_NOD.description: CAN remote node number for serial port

CAN_D3_UC_S2_PRO.label: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port
CAN_D3_UC_S2_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D3_UC_S3_BD.label: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node
CAN_D3_UC_S3_BD.description: Serial baud rate on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S3_IDX.label: Serial port number on remote CAN node
CAN_D3_UC_S3_IDX.description: Serial port number on remote CAN node

CAN_D3_UC_S3_NOD.label: Serial CAN remote node number
CAN_D3_UC_S3_NOD.description: CAN node number for serial port

CAN_D3_UC_S3_PRO.label: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port
CAN_D3_UC_S3_PRO.description: Serial protocol of DroneCAN serial port

CAN_D3_UC_SRV_BM.label: Output channels to be transmitted as servo over DroneCAN
CAN_D3_UC_SRV_BM.description: Bitmask with one set for channel to be transmitted as a servo command over DroneCAN

CAN_D3_UC_SRV_RT.label: Servo output rate
CAN_D3_UC_SRV_RT.description: Maximum transmit rate for servo outputs

CAN_LOGLEVEL.label: Loglevel
CAN_LOGLEVEL.description: Loglevel for recording initialisation and debug information from CAN Interface

CAN_P1_BITRATE.label: Bitrate of CAN interface
CAN_P1_BITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 10000 to 1000000

CAN_P1_DRIVER.label: Index of virtual driver to be used with physical CAN interface
CAN_P1_DRIVER.description: Enabling this option enables use of CAN buses.

CAN_P1_FDBITRATE.label: Bitrate of CANFD interface
CAN_P1_FDBITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

CAN_P2_BITRATE.label: Bitrate of CAN interface
CAN_P2_BITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 10000 to 1000000

CAN_P2_DRIVER.label: Index of virtual driver to be used with physical CAN interface
CAN_P2_DRIVER.description: Enabling this option enables use of CAN buses.

CAN_P2_FDBITRATE.label: Bitrate of CANFD interface
CAN_P2_FDBITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

CAN_P3_BITRATE.label: Bitrate of CAN interface
CAN_P3_BITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 10000 to 1000000

CAN_P3_DRIVER.label: Index of virtual driver to be used with physical CAN interface
CAN_P3_DRIVER.description: Enabling this option enables use of CAN buses.

CAN_P3_FDBITRATE.label: Bitrate of CANFD interface
CAN_P3_FDBITRATE.description: Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

CAN_SLCAN_CPORT.label: SLCAN Route
CAN_SLCAN_CPORT.description: CAN Interface ID to be routed to SLCAN, 0 means no routing

CAN_SLCAN_SDELAY.label: SLCAN Start Delay
CAN_SLCAN_SDELAY.description: Duration after which slcan starts after setting SERNUM in seconds.

CAN_SLCAN_SERNUM.label: SLCAN Serial Port
CAN_SLCAN_SERNUM.description: Serial Port ID to be used for temporary SLCAN iface, -1 means no temporary serial. This parameter is automatically reset on reboot or on timeout. See CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT for timeout details

CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT.label: SLCAN Timeout
CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT.description: Duration of inactivity after which SLCAN is switched back to original driver in seconds.

CC2_ANG_PIT_P.label: Pitch axis angle controller P gain
CC2_ANG_PIT_P.description: Pitch axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired pitch angle and actual angle to a desired pitch rate

CC2_ANG_RLL_P.label: Roll axis angle controller P gain
CC2_ANG_RLL_P.description: Roll axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired roll angle and actual angle to a desired roll rate

CC2_ANG_YAW_P.label: Yaw axis angle controller P gain
CC2_ANG_YAW_P.description: Yaw axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired yaw angle and actual angle to a desired yaw rate

CC2_RAT_PIT_D.label: Pitch axis rate controller D gain
CC2_RAT_PIT_D.description: Pitch axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

CC2_RAT_PIT_D_FF.label: Pitch Derivative FeedForward Gain
CC2_RAT_PIT_D_FF.description: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

CC2_RAT_PIT_FF.label: Pitch axis rate controller feed forward
CC2_RAT_PIT_FF.description: Pitch axis rate controller feed forward

CC2_RAT_PIT_FLTD.label: Pitch axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz
CC2_RAT_PIT_FLTD.description: Pitch axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_PIT_FLTE.label: Pitch axis rate controller error frequency in Hz
CC2_RAT_PIT_FLTE.description: Pitch axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_PIT_FLTT.label: Pitch axis rate controller target frequency in Hz
CC2_RAT_PIT_FLTT.description: Pitch axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_PIT_I.label: Pitch axis rate controller I gain
CC2_RAT_PIT_I.description: Pitch axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

CC2_RAT_PIT_IMAX.label: Pitch axis rate controller I gain maximum
CC2_RAT_PIT_IMAX.description: Pitch axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

CC2_RAT_PIT_P.label: Pitch axis rate controller P gain
CC2_RAT_PIT_P.description: Pitch axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

CC2_RAT_PIT_PDMX.label: Pitch axis rate controller PD sum maximum
CC2_RAT_PIT_PDMX.description: Pitch axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

CC2_RAT_PIT_SMAX.label: Pitch slew rate limit
CC2_RAT_PIT_SMAX.description: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

CC2_RAT_RLL_D.label: Roll axis rate controller D gain
CC2_RAT_RLL_D.description: Roll axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

CC2_RAT_RLL_D_FF.label: Roll Derivative FeedForward Gain
CC2_RAT_RLL_D_FF.description: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

CC2_RAT_RLL_FF.label: Roll axis rate controller feed forward
CC2_RAT_RLL_FF.description: Roll axis rate controller feed forward

CC2_RAT_RLL_FLTD.label: Roll axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz
CC2_RAT_RLL_FLTD.description: Roll axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_RLL_FLTE.label: Roll axis rate controller error frequency in Hz
CC2_RAT_RLL_FLTE.description: Roll axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_RLL_FLTT.label: Roll axis rate controller target frequency in Hz
CC2_RAT_RLL_FLTT.description: Roll axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_RLL_I.label: Roll axis rate controller I gain
CC2_RAT_RLL_I.description: Roll axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

CC2_RAT_RLL_IMAX.label: Roll axis rate controller I gain maximum
CC2_RAT_RLL_IMAX.description: Roll axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

CC2_RAT_RLL_P.label: Roll axis rate controller P gain
CC2_RAT_RLL_P.description: Roll axis rate controller P gain. Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

CC2_RAT_RLL_PDMX.label: Roll axis rate controller PD sum maximum
CC2_RAT_RLL_PDMX.description: Roll axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

CC2_RAT_RLL_SMAX.label: Roll slew rate limit
CC2_RAT_RLL_SMAX.description: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

CC2_RAT_YAW_D.label: Yaw axis rate controller D gain
CC2_RAT_YAW_D.description: Yaw axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

CC2_RAT_YAW_D_FF.label: Yaw Derivative FeedForward Gain
CC2_RAT_YAW_D_FF.description: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

CC2_RAT_YAW_FF.label: Yaw axis rate controller feed forward
CC2_RAT_YAW_FF.description: Yaw axis rate controller feed forward

CC2_RAT_YAW_FLTD.label: Yaw axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz
CC2_RAT_YAW_FLTD.description: Yaw axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_YAW_FLTE.label: Yaw axis rate controller error frequency in Hz
CC2_RAT_YAW_FLTE.description: Yaw axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_YAW_FLTT.label: Yaw axis rate controller target frequency in Hz
CC2_RAT_YAW_FLTT.description: Yaw axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

CC2_RAT_YAW_I.label: Yaw axis rate controller I gain
CC2_RAT_YAW_I.description: Yaw axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

CC2_RAT_YAW_IMAX.label: Yaw axis rate controller I gain maximum
CC2_RAT_YAW_IMAX.description: Yaw axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

CC2_RAT_YAW_P.label: Yaw axis rate controller P gain
CC2_RAT_YAW_P.description: Yaw axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

CC2_RAT_YAW_PDMX.label: Yaw axis rate controller PD sum maximum
CC2_RAT_YAW_PDMX.description: Yaw axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

CC2_RAT_YAW_SMAX.label: Yaw slew rate limit
CC2_RAT_YAW_SMAX.description: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

CC_AXIS_MASK.label: Custom Controller bitmask
CC_AXIS_MASK.description: Custom Controller bitmask to chose which axis to run

CC_TYPE.label: Custom control type
CC_TYPE.description: Custom control type to be used

CFACT.label: Measurement correction factor
CFACT.description: This is multiplicative factor to correct the measured flow. Set to <1 if your sensor measures too high.

CGA_RATIO.label: CoG adjustment ratio
CGA_RATIO.description: The ratio between the front and back motor outputs during steady-state hover. Positive when the CoG is in front of the motors midpoint (front motors work harder).

CHUTE_ALT_MIN.label: Parachute min altitude in meters above home
CHUTE_ALT_MIN.description: Parachute min altitude above home.  Parachute will not be released below this altitude.  0 to disable alt check.

CHUTE_CRT_SINK.label: Critical sink speed rate in m/s to trigger emergency parachute
CHUTE_CRT_SINK.description: Release parachute when critical sink rate is reached

CHUTE_DELAY_MS.label: Parachute release delay
CHUTE_DELAY_MS.description: Delay in millseconds between motor stop and chute release

CHUTE_SERVO_OFF.label: Servo OFF PWM value
CHUTE_SERVO_OFF.description: Parachute Servo PWM value in microseconds when parachute is not released

CHUTE_SERVO_ON.label: Parachute Servo ON PWM value
CHUTE_SERVO_ON.description: Parachute Servo PWM value in microseconds when parachute is released

CHUTE_TYPE.label: Parachute release mechanism type (relay or servo)
CHUTE_TYPE.description: Parachute release mechanism type (relay number in versions prior to 4.5, or servo). Values 0-3 all are relay. Relay number used for release is set by RELAYx_FUNCTION in 4.5 or later.

CIRCLE_OPTIONS.label: Circle options
CIRCLE_OPTIONS.description: 0:Enable or disable using the pitch/roll stick control circle mode's radius and rate

options.label: Start at center rather than on perimeter
Make Mount ROI the center of the circle

CIRCLE_RADIUS_M.label: Circle Radius
CIRCLE_RADIUS_M.description: Radius of the circle the vehicle will fly when in Circle flight mode

CIRCLE_RATE.label: Circle rate
CIRCLE_RATE.description: Circle mode's maximum turn rate in deg/sec.  Positive to turn clockwise, negative for counter clockwise. Final circle rate is also limited by speed and acceleration settings.

COMPASS_AUTODEC.label: Auto Declination
COMPASS_AUTODEC.description: Enable or disable the automatic calculation of the declination based on gps location

COMPASS_AUTO_ROT.label: Automatically check orientation
COMPASS_AUTO_ROT.description: When enabled this will automatically check the orientation of compasses on successful completion of compass calibration. If set to 2 then external compasses will have their orientation automatically corrected.

options.label: use same tolerance to auto rotate 45 deg rotations

COMPASS_CAL_FIT.label: Compass calibration fitness
COMPASS_CAL_FIT.description: This controls the fitness level required for a successful compass calibration. A lower value makes for a stricter fit (less likely to pass). This is the value used for the primary magnetometer. Other magnetometers get double the value.

COMPASS_CUS_PIT.label: Custom orientation pitch offset
COMPASS_CUS_PIT.description: Compass mounting position pitch offset. Positive values = pitch up, negative values = pitch down. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

COMPASS_CUS_ROLL.label: Custom orientation roll offset
COMPASS_CUS_ROLL.description: Compass mounting position roll offset. Positive values = roll right, negative values = roll left. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

COMPASS_CUS_YAW.label: Custom orientation yaw offset
COMPASS_CUS_YAW.description: Compass mounting position yaw offset. Positive values = yaw right, negative values = yaw left. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

COMPASS_DEC.label: Compass declination
COMPASS_DEC.description: An angle to compensate between the true north and magnetic north

COMPASS_DEV_ID.label: Compass device id
COMPASS_DEV_ID.description: Compass device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID2.label: Compass2 device id
COMPASS_DEV_ID2.description: Second compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID3.label: Compass3 device id
COMPASS_DEV_ID3.description: Third compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID4.label: Compass4 device id
COMPASS_DEV_ID4.description: Extra 4th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID5.label: Compass5 device id
COMPASS_DEV_ID5.description: Extra 5th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID6.label: Compass6 device id
COMPASS_DEV_ID6.description: Extra 6th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID7.label: Compass7 device id
COMPASS_DEV_ID7.description: Extra 7th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DEV_ID8.label: Compass8 device id
COMPASS_DEV_ID8.description: Extra 8th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

COMPASS_DIA2_X.label: Compass2 soft-iron diagonal X component
COMPASS_DIA2_X.description: DIA_X in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA2_Y.label: Compass2 soft-iron diagonal Y component
COMPASS_DIA2_Y.description: DIA_Y in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA2_Z.label: Compass2 soft-iron diagonal Z component
COMPASS_DIA2_Z.description: DIA_Z in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA3_X.label: Compass3 soft-iron diagonal X component
COMPASS_DIA3_X.description: DIA_X in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA3_Y.label: Compass3 soft-iron diagonal Y component
COMPASS_DIA3_Y.description: DIA_Y in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA3_Z.label: Compass3 soft-iron diagonal Z component
COMPASS_DIA3_Z.description: DIA_Z in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA_X.label: Compass soft-iron diagonal X component
COMPASS_DIA_X.description: DIA_X in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA_Y.label: Compass soft-iron diagonal Y component
COMPASS_DIA_Y.description: DIA_Y in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DIA_Z.label: Compass soft-iron diagonal Z component
COMPASS_DIA_Z.description: DIA_Z in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_DISBLMSK.label: Compass disable driver type mask
COMPASS_DISBLMSK.description: This is a bitmask of driver types to disable. If a driver type is set in this mask then that driver will not try to find a sensor at startup

COMPASS_ENABLE.label: Enable Compass
COMPASS_ENABLE.description: Setting this to Enabled(1) will enable the compass. Setting this to Disabled(0) will disable the compass. Note that this is separate from COMPASS_USE. This will enable the low level senor, and will enable logging of magnetometer data. To use the compass for navigation you must also set COMPASS_USE to 1.

COMPASS_EXTERN2.label: Compass2 is attached via an external cable
COMPASS_EXTERN2.description: Configure second compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

COMPASS_EXTERN3.label: Compass3 is attached via an external cable
COMPASS_EXTERN3.description: Configure third compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

COMPASS_EXTERNAL.label: Compass is attached via an external cable
COMPASS_EXTERNAL.description: Configure compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. Set to 1 if the compass is externally connected. When externally connected the COMPASS_ORIENT option operates independently of the AHRS_ORIENTATION board orientation option. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

COMPASS_FLTR_RNG.label: Range in which sample is accepted
COMPASS_FLTR_RNG.description: This sets the range around the average value that new samples must be within to be accepted. This can help reduce the impact of noise on sensors that are on long I2C cables. The value is a percentage from the average value. A value of zero disables this filter.

COMPASS_LEARN.label: Learn compass offsets automatically
COMPASS_LEARN.description: Enable or disable the automatic learning of compass offsets. You can enable learning either using a compass-only method that is suitable only for fixed wing aircraft or using the offsets learnt by the active EKF state estimator. If this option is enabled then the learnt offsets are saved when you disarm the vehicle. If InFlight learning is enabled then the compass with automatically start learning once a flight starts (must be armed). While InFlight learning is running you cannot use position control modes.

COMPASS_MOT2_X.label: Motor interference compensation to compass2 for body frame X axis
COMPASS_MOT2_X.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT2_Y.label: Motor interference compensation to compass2 for body frame Y axis
COMPASS_MOT2_Y.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT2_Z.label: Motor interference compensation to compass2 for body frame Z axis
COMPASS_MOT2_Z.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT3_X.label: Motor interference compensation to compass3 for body frame X axis
COMPASS_MOT3_X.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT3_Y.label: Motor interference compensation to compass3 for body frame Y axis
COMPASS_MOT3_Y.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT3_Z.label: Motor interference compensation to compass3 for body frame Z axis
COMPASS_MOT3_Z.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOTCT.label: Motor interference compensation type
COMPASS_MOTCT.description: Set motor interference compensation type to disabled, throttle or current.  Do not change manually.

COMPASS_MOT_X.label: Motor interference compensation for body frame X axis
COMPASS_MOT_X.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT_Y.label: Motor interference compensation for body frame Y axis
COMPASS_MOT_Y.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_MOT_Z.label: Motor interference compensation for body frame Z axis
COMPASS_MOT_Z.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

COMPASS_ODI2_X.label: Compass2 soft-iron off-diagonal X component
COMPASS_ODI2_X.description: ODI_X in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI2_Y.label: Compass2 soft-iron off-diagonal Y component
COMPASS_ODI2_Y.description: ODI_Y in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI2_Z.label: Compass2 soft-iron off-diagonal Z component
COMPASS_ODI2_Z.description: ODI_Z in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI3_X.label: Compass3 soft-iron off-diagonal X component
COMPASS_ODI3_X.description: ODI_X in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI3_Y.label: Compass3 soft-iron off-diagonal Y component
COMPASS_ODI3_Y.description: ODI_Y in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI3_Z.label: Compass3 soft-iron off-diagonal Z component
COMPASS_ODI3_Z.description: ODI_Z in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI_X.label: Compass soft-iron off-diagonal X component
COMPASS_ODI_X.description: ODI_X in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI_Y.label: Compass soft-iron off-diagonal Y component
COMPASS_ODI_Y.description: ODI_Y in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_ODI_Z.label: Compass soft-iron off-diagonal Z component
COMPASS_ODI_Z.description: ODI_Z in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

COMPASS_OFFS_MAX.label: Compass maximum offset
COMPASS_OFFS_MAX.description: This sets the maximum allowed compass offset in calibration and arming checks

COMPASS_OFS2_X.label: Compass2 offsets in milligauss on the X axis
COMPASS_OFS2_X.description: Offset to be added to compass2's x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS2_Y.label: Compass2 offsets in milligauss on the Y axis
COMPASS_OFS2_Y.description: Offset to be added to compass2's y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS2_Z.label: Compass2 offsets in milligauss on the Z axis
COMPASS_OFS2_Z.description: Offset to be added to compass2's z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS3_X.label: Compass3 offsets in milligauss on the X axis
COMPASS_OFS3_X.description: Offset to be added to compass3's x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS3_Y.label: Compass3 offsets in milligauss on the Y axis
COMPASS_OFS3_Y.description: Offset to be added to compass3's y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS3_Z.label: Compass3 offsets in milligauss on the Z axis
COMPASS_OFS3_Z.description: Offset to be added to compass3's z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS_X.label: Compass offsets in milligauss on the X axis
COMPASS_OFS_X.description: Offset to be added to the compass x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS_Y.label: Compass offsets in milligauss on the Y axis
COMPASS_OFS_Y.description: Offset to be added to the compass y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OFS_Z.label: Compass offsets in milligauss on the Z axis
COMPASS_OFS_Z.description: Offset to be added to the compass z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

COMPASS_OPTIONS.label: Compass options
COMPASS_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the compass

options.label: Allow missing DroneCAN compasses to be automaticaly replaced (calibration still required)

COMPASS_ORIENT.label: Compass orientation
COMPASS_ORIENT.description: The orientation of the first external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

COMPASS_ORIENT2.label: Compass2 orientation
COMPASS_ORIENT2.description: The orientation of a second external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

COMPASS_ORIENT3.label: Compass3 orientation
COMPASS_ORIENT3.description: The orientation of a third external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation.
