FOLL_ACCEL_NE.label: Acceleration limit for the horizontal kinematic input shaping
FOLL_ACCEL_NE.description: Acceleration limit of the horizontal kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the estimate varies in velocity

FOLL_DIST_MAX.label: Follow distance maximum
FOLL_DIST_MAX.description: Follow distance maximum. If exceeded, the follow estimate will be considered invalid. If zero there is no maximum.

FOLL_JERK_D.label: Jerk limit for the vertical kinematic input shaping
FOLL_JERK_D.description: Jerk limit of the vertical kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the estimate varies in acceleration

FOLL_JERK_H.label: Angular jerk limit for the heading kinematic input shaping
FOLL_JERK_H.description: Angular jerk limit of the heading kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the estimate varies in angular acceleration

FOLL_JERK_NE.label: Jerk limit for the horizontal kinematic input shaping
FOLL_JERK_NE.description: Jerk limit of the horizontal kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the estimate varies in acceleration

FOLL_OFS_X.label: Follow offsets in meters north/forward
FOLL_OFS_X.description: Follow offsets in meters north/forward. If positive, this vehicle fly ahead or north of lead vehicle.  Depends on FOLL_OFS_TYPE

FOLL_OFS_Y.label: Follow offsets in meters east/right
FOLL_OFS_Y.description: Follow offsets in meters east/right. If positive, this vehicle will fly to the right or east of lead vehicle.  Depends on FOLL_OFS_TYPE

FOLL_OFS_Z.label: Follow offsets in meters down
FOLL_OFS_Z.description: Follow offsets in meters down. If positive, this vehicle will fly below the lead vehicle

options.label: Mount Follows lead vehicle on mode enter

FOLL_POS_P.label: Follow position error P gain
FOLL_POS_P.description: Follow position error P gain. Converts the difference between desired vertical speed and actual speed into a desired acceleration that is passed to the throttle acceleration controller

FOLL_TIMEOUT.label: Follow timeout
FOLL_TIMEOUT.description: Follow position update from lead - timeout after x seconds

FOLT_MAV_CHAN.label: Follow Target Send MAVLink Channel
FOLT_MAV_CHAN.description: MAVLink channel to which FOLLOW_TARGET should be sent

FORMAT_VERSION.label: Eeprom format version number
FORMAT_VERSION.description: This value is incremented when changes are made to the eeprom format

FRAME_CLASS.label: Frame Class
FRAME_CLASS.description: Controls major frame class for multicopter component

FRAME_TYPE.label: Frame Type (+, X, V, etc)
FRAME_TYPE.description: Controls motor mixing for multicopters.  Not used for Tri or Traditional Helicopters.

FRSKY_DNLINK1_ID.label: First downlink sensor id
FRSKY_DNLINK1_ID.description: Change the first extra downlink sensor id (SPort only)

FRSKY_DNLINK2_ID.label: Second downlink sensor id
FRSKY_DNLINK2_ID.description: Change the second extra downlink sensor id (SPort only)

FRSKY_DNLINK_ID.label: Default downlink sensor id
FRSKY_DNLINK_ID.description: Change the default downlink sensor id (SPort only)

FRSKY_OPTIONS.label: FRSky Telemetry Options
FRSKY_OPTIONS.description: A bitmask to set some FRSky Telemetry specific options

FRSKY_UPLINK_ID.label: Uplink sensor id
FRSKY_UPLINK_ID.description: Change the uplink sensor id (SPort only)

FSTRATE_DIV.label: Fast rate thread divisor
FSTRATE_DIV.description: Fast rate thread divisor used to control the maximum fast rate update rate. The actual rate is the gyro rate in Hz divided by this value. This value is scaled depending on the configuration of FSTRATE_ENABLE.

FSTRATE_ENABLE.label: Enable the fast Rate thread
FSTRATE_ENABLE.description: Enable the fast Rate thread. In the default case the fast rate divisor, which controls the update frequency of the thread, is dynamically scaled from FSTRATE_DIV to avoid overrun in the gyro sample buffer and main loop slow-downs. Other values can be selected to fix the divisor to FSTRATE_DIV on arming or always.

FS_CRASH_CHECK.label: Crash check enable
FS_CRASH_CHECK.description: This enables automatic crash checking. When enabled the motors will disarm if a crash is detected.

FS_DR_ENABLE.label: DeadReckon Failsafe Action
FS_DR_ENABLE.description: Failsafe action taken immediately as deadreckoning starts. Deadreckoning starts when EKF loses position and velocity source and relies only on wind estimates

FS_DR_TIMEOUT.label: DeadReckon Failsafe Timeout
FS_DR_TIMEOUT.description: DeadReckoning is available for this many seconds after losing position and/or velocity source.  After this timeout elapses the EKF failsafe will trigger in modes requiring a position estimate

FS_EKF_ACTION.label: EKF Failsafe Action
FS_EKF_ACTION.description: Controls the action that will be taken when an EKF failsafe is invoked

options.label: Switch to Land mode if current mode requires position
Switch to AltHold mode if current mode requires position
Switch to Land mode from all modes

FS_EKF_FILT.label: EKF Failsafe filter cutoff
FS_EKF_FILT.description: EKF Failsafe filter cutoff frequency. EKF variances are filtered using this value to avoid spurious failsafes from transient high variances. A higher value means the failsafe is more likely to trigger.

FS_EKF_THRESH.label: EKF failsafe variance threshold
FS_EKF_THRESH.description: Allows setting the maximum acceptable compass, velocity, position and height variances. Used in arming check and EKF failsafe.

FS_GCS_ENABLE.label: Ground Station Failsafe Enable
FS_GCS_ENABLE.description: Controls whether failsafe will be invoked (and what action to take) when connection with Ground station is lost for at least 5 seconds. See FS_OPTIONS param for additional actions, or for cases allowing Mission continuation, when GCS failsafe is enabled.

options.label: RTL or Continue with Mission in Auto Mode (Removed in 4.0+-see FS_OPTIONS)

FS_GCS_TIMEOUT.label: GCS failsafe timeout
FS_GCS_TIMEOUT.description: Timeout before triggering the GCS failsafe

FS_OPTIONS.label: Failsafe options bitmask
FS_OPTIONS.description: Bitmask of additional options for battery, radio, & GCS failsafes. 0 disables all options.

options.label: Continue if in Auto on RC failsafe
Continue if in Auto on GCS failsafe
Continue if in Guided on RC failsafe
Continue if landing on any failsafe
Continue if in pilot controlled modes on GCS failsafe

FS_THR_ENABLE.label: Throttle Failsafe Enable
FS_THR_ENABLE.description: The throttle failsafe allows you to configure a software failsafe activated by a setting on the throttle input channel

options.label: Enabled Continue with Mission in Auto Mode (Removed in 4.0+)

FS_THR_VALUE.label: Throttle Failsafe Value
FS_THR_VALUE.description: The PWM level in microseconds on channel 3 below which throttle failsafe triggers

FS_VIBE_ENABLE.label: Vibration Failsafe enable
FS_VIBE_ENABLE.description: This enables the vibration failsafe which will use modified altitude estimation and control during high vibrations

GCS_PID_MASK.label: GCS PID tuning mask
GCS_PID_MASK.description: bitmask of PIDs to send MAVLink PID_TUNING messages for

GEN_L_IDLE_TEMP.label: Idle Temperature
GEN_L_IDLE_TEMP.description: temperature required for generator to return to idle after having run

GEN_L_IDLE_TH.label: Idle throttle
GEN_L_IDLE_TH.description: throttle value to use when idling

GEN_L_IDLE_TH_H.label: High Idle throttle
GEN_L_IDLE_TH_H.description: throttle value to use when warming up or cooling down

GEN_L_OVER_TEMP.label: Cylinder Head Over Temperature Warning Level
GEN_L_OVER_TEMP.description: threshold temperature for the cylinder head above which the mavlink over temperature message gets sent

GEN_L_RUNTIME.label: Total runtime
GEN_L_RUNTIME.description: Total time this generator has run in seconds

GEN_L_RUN_TEMP.label: Run Temperature
GEN_L_RUN_TEMP.description: temperature required for generator to start producing power in deg celsius

GPS1_CAN_NODEID.label: Detected CAN Node ID for GPS
GPS1_CAN_NODEID.description: GPS Node id for GPS.  Detected node unless CAN_OVRIDE is set

GPS1_CAN_OVRIDE.label: DroneCAN GPS NODE ID
GPS1_CAN_OVRIDE.description: GPS Node id for GPS. If 0 the gps will be automatically selected on a first-come-first-GPS basis.

GPS1_COM_PORT.label: GPS physical COM port
GPS1_COM_PORT.description: The physical COM port on the connected device, currently only applies to SBF and GSOF GPS

GPS1_DELAY_MS.label: GPS delay in milliseconds
GPS1_DELAY_MS.description: Controls the amount of GPS  measurement delay that the autopilot compensates for. Set to zero to use the default delay for the detected GPS type.

GPS1_GNSS_MODE.label: GNSS system configuration
GPS1_GNSS_MODE.description: Bitmask for what GNSS system to use (all unchecked or zero to leave GPS as configured)

GPS1_MB_OFS_X.label: Base antenna X position offset
GPS1_MB_OFS_X.description: X position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive X is forward of the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_X.

GPS1_MB_OFS_Y.label: Base antenna Y position offset
GPS1_MB_OFS_Y.description: Y position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive Y is to the right of the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_Y.

GPS1_MB_OFS_Z.label: Base antenna Z position offset
GPS1_MB_OFS_Z.description: Z position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive Z is down from the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_Z.

GPS1_MB_TYPE.label: Moving base type
GPS1_MB_TYPE.description: Controls the type of moving base used if using moving base.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_TYPE.

GPS1_POS_X.label: Antenna X position offset
GPS1_POS_X.description: X position of the first GPS antenna in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.

GPS1_POS_Y.label: Antenna Y position offset
GPS1_POS_Y.description: Y position of the first GPS antenna in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.

GPS1_POS_Z.label: Antenna Z position offset
GPS1_POS_Z.description: Z position of the first GPS antenna in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.

GPS1_RATE_MS.label: GPS update rate in milliseconds
GPS1_RATE_MS.description: Controls how often the GPS should provide a position update. Lowering below 5Hz(default) is not allowed. Raising the rate above 5Hz usually provides little benefit and for some GPS (eg Ublox M9N) can severely impact performance.

GPS2_CAN_NODEID.label: Detected CAN Node ID for GPS
GPS2_CAN_NODEID.description: GPS Node id for GPS.  Detected node unless CAN_OVRIDE is set

GPS2_CAN_OVRIDE.label: DroneCAN GPS NODE ID
GPS2_CAN_OVRIDE.description: GPS Node id for GPS. If 0 the gps will be automatically selected on a first-come-first-GPS basis.

GPS2_COM_PORT.label: GPS physical COM port
GPS2_COM_PORT.description: The physical COM port on the connected device, currently only applies to SBF and GSOF GPS

GPS2_DELAY_MS.label: GPS delay in milliseconds
GPS2_DELAY_MS.description: Controls the amount of GPS  measurement delay that the autopilot compensates for. Set to zero to use the default delay for the detected GPS type.

GPS2_GNSS_MODE.label: GNSS system configuration
GPS2_GNSS_MODE.description: Bitmask for what GNSS system to use (all unchecked or zero to leave GPS as configured)

GPS2_MB_OFS_X.label: Base antenna X position offset
GPS2_MB_OFS_X.description: X position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive X is forward of the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_X.

GPS2_MB_OFS_Y.label: Base antenna Y position offset
GPS2_MB_OFS_Y.description: Y position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive Y is to the right of the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_Y.

GPS2_MB_OFS_Z.label: Base antenna Z position offset
GPS2_MB_OFS_Z.description: Z position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive Z is down from the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_Z.

GPS2_MB_TYPE.label: Moving base type
GPS2_MB_TYPE.description: Controls the type of moving base used if using moving base.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_TYPE.

GPS2_POS_X.label: Antenna X position offset
GPS2_POS_X.description: X position of the first GPS antenna in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.

GPS2_POS_Y.label: Antenna Y position offset
GPS2_POS_Y.description: Y position of the first GPS antenna in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.

GPS2_POS_Z.label: Antenna Z position offset
GPS2_POS_Z.description: Z position of the first GPS antenna in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.

GPS2_RATE_MS.label: GPS update rate in milliseconds
GPS2_RATE_MS.description: Controls how often the GPS should provide a position update. Lowering below 5Hz(default) is not allowed. Raising the rate above 5Hz usually provides little benefit and for some GPS (eg Ublox M9N) can severely impact performance.

GPS_AUTO_CONFIG.label: Automatic GPS configuration
GPS_AUTO_CONFIG.description: Controls if the autopilot should automatically configure the GPS based on the parameters and default settings

options.label: Enable automatic configuration for Serial GPSes only
Enable automatic configuration for DroneCAN as well
Clear all configurations not set by ardupilot (UBlox only)

GPS_AUTO_SWITCH.label: Automatic Switchover Setting
GPS_AUTO_SWITCH.description: Automatic switchover to GPS reporting best lock, 1:UseBest selects the GPS with highest status, if both are equal the GPS with highest satellite count is used 4:Use primary if 3D fix or better, will revert to 'UseBest' behaviour if 3D fix is lost on primary

options.label: Use primary if 3D fix or better

GPS_BLEND_MASK.label: Multi GPS Blending Mask
GPS_BLEND_MASK.description: Determines which of the accuracy measures Horizontal position, Vertical Position and Speed are used to calculate the weighting on each GPS receiver when soft switching has been selected by setting GPS_AUTO_SWITCH to 2(Blend)

GPS_CAN_NODEID1.label: GPS Node ID 1
GPS_CAN_NODEID1.description: GPS Node id for first-discovered GPS.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS1_CAN_NODEID.

GPS_CAN_NODEID2.label: GPS Node ID 2
GPS_CAN_NODEID2.description: GPS Node id for second-discovered GPS.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS2_CAN_NODEID.

GPS_COM_PORT.label: GPS physical COM port
GPS_COM_PORT.description: The physical COM port on the connected device, currently only applies to SBF and GSOF GPS,Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS1_COM_PORT.

GPS_COM_PORT2.label: GPS physical COM port
GPS_COM_PORT2.description: The physical COM port on the connected device, currently only applies to SBF and GSOF GPS.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS1_COM_PORT.

GPS_DELAY_MS.label: GPS delay in milliseconds
GPS_DELAY_MS.description: Controls the amount of GPS  measurement delay that the autopilot compensates for. Set to zero to use the default delay for the detected GPS type.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS1_DELAY_MS.

GPS_DELAY_MS2.label: GPS 2 delay in milliseconds
GPS_DELAY_MS2.description: Controls the amount of GPS  measurement delay that the autopilot compensates for. Set to zero to use the default delay for the detected GPS type.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS2_DELAY_MS.

options.label: Use UART2 for moving baseline on ublox
Use base station for GPS yaw on SBF
Use dedicated CAN port b/w GPSes for moving baseline
Use ellipsoid height instead of AMSL
Override GPS satellite health of L5 band from L1 health
Enable RTCM full parse even for a single channel
Disable automatic full RTCM parsing when RTCM seen on more than one channel
Force UBlox Config Get/Set for configuration then automatic configuration for Serial GPSes only

GPS_GNSS_MODE.label: GNSS system configuration
GPS_GNSS_MODE.description: Bitmask for what GNSS system to use on the first GPS (all unchecked or zero to leave GPS as configured).Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS1_GNSS_MODE.

GPS_GNSS_MODE2.label: GNSS system configuration.
GPS_GNSS_MODE2.description: Bitmask for what GNSS system to use on the second GPS (all unchecked or zero to leave GPS as configured). Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS2_GNSS_MODE

GPS_HDOP_GOOD.label: GPS Hdop Good
GPS_HDOP_GOOD.description: GPS Hdop value at or below this value represent a good position.  Used for pre-arm checks

GPS_INJECT_TO.label: Destination for GPS_INJECT_DATA MAVLink packets
GPS_INJECT_TO.description: The GGS can send raw serial packets to inject data to multiple GPSes.

GPS_MB1_OFS_X.label: Base antenna X position offset
GPS_MB1_OFS_X.description: X position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive X is forward of the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_X.

GPS_MB1_OFS_Y.label: Base antenna Y position offset
GPS_MB1_OFS_Y.description: Y position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive Y is to the right of the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_Y.

GPS_MB1_OFS_Z.label: Base antenna Z position offset
GPS_MB1_OFS_Z.description: Z position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive Z is down from the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_Z.

GPS_MB1_TYPE.label: Moving base type
GPS_MB1_TYPE.description: Controls the type of moving base used if using moving base.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_TYPE.

GPS_MB2_OFS_X.label: Base antenna X position offset
GPS_MB2_OFS_X.description: X position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive X is forward of the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_X.

GPS_MB2_OFS_Y.label: Base antenna Y position offset
GPS_MB2_OFS_Y.description: Y position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive Y is to the right of the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_Y.

GPS_MB2_OFS_Z.label: Base antenna Z position offset
GPS_MB2_OFS_Z.description: Z position of the base (primary) GPS antenna in body frame from the position of the 2nd antenna. Positive Z is down from the 2nd antenna. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_OFS_Z.

GPS_MB2_TYPE.label: Moving base type
GPS_MB2_TYPE.description: Controls the type of moving base used if using moving base.This is renamed in 4.6 and later to GPSx_MB_TYPE.

GPS_MIN_ELEV.label: Minimum elevation
GPS_MIN_ELEV.description: This sets the minimum elevation of satellites above the horizon for them to be used for navigation. Setting this to -100 leaves the minimum elevation set to the GPS modules default.

GPS_POS1_X.label: Antenna X position offset
GPS_POS1_X.description: X position of the first GPS antenna in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS1_POS_X.

GPS_POS1_Y.label: Antenna Y position offset
GPS_POS1_Y.description: Y position of the first GPS antenna in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS1_POS_Y.

GPS_POS1_Z.label: Antenna Z position offset
GPS_POS1_Z.description: Z position of the first GPS antenna in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS1_POS_Z.

GPS_POS2_X.label: Antenna X position offset
GPS_POS2_X.description: X position of the second GPS antenna in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS2_POS_X.

GPS_POS2_Y.label: Antenna Y position offset
GPS_POS2_Y.description: Y position of the second GPS antenna in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS2_POS_Y.

GPS_POS2_Z.label: Antenna Z position offset
GPS_POS2_Z.description: Z position of the second GPS antenna in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use antenna phase centroid location if provided by the manufacturer.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS2_POS_Z.

GPS_PRIMARY.label: Primary GPS
GPS_PRIMARY.description: This GPS will be used when GPS_AUTO_SWITCH is 0 and used preferentially with GPS_AUTO_SWITCH = 4.

GPS_RATE_MS.label: GPS update rate in milliseconds
GPS_RATE_MS.description: Controls how often the GPS should provide a position update. Lowering below 5Hz(default) is not allowed. Raising the rate above 5Hz usually provides little benefit and for some GPS (eg Ublox M9N) can severely impact performance.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS1_RATE_MS

GPS_RATE_MS2.label: GPS 2 update rate in milliseconds
GPS_RATE_MS2.description: Controls how often the GPS should provide a position update. Lowering below 5Hz(default) is not allowed. Raising the rate above 5Hz usually provides little benefit and for some GPS (eg Ublox M9N) can severely impact performance.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS2_RATE_MS

GPS_RAW_DATA.label: Raw data logging
GPS_RAW_DATA.description: Handles logging raw data; on uBlox chips that support raw data this will log RXM messages into logger; on Septentrio this will log on the equipment's SD card and when set to 2, the autopilot will try to stop logging after disarming and restart after arming

options.label: Only log every five samples (uBlox only)

GPS_SAVE_CFG.label: Save GPS configuration
GPS_SAVE_CFG.description: Determines whether the configuration for this GPS should be written to non-volatile memory on the GPS. Currently working for UBlox 6 series and above.

GPS_SBAS_MODE.label: SBAS Mode
GPS_SBAS_MODE.description: This sets the SBAS (satellite based augmentation system) mode if available on this GPS. If set to 2 then the SBAS mode is not changed in the GPS. Otherwise the GPS will be reconfigured to enable/disable SBAS. Disabling SBAS may be worthwhile in some parts of the world where an SBAS signal is available but the baseline is too long to be useful.

GPS_SBP_LOGMASK.label: Swift Binary Protocol Logging Mask
GPS_SBP_LOGMASK.description: Masked with the SBP msg_type field to determine whether SBR1/SBR2 data is logged

GPS_TYPE.label: 1st GPS type
GPS_TYPE.description: GPS type of 1st GPS.Renamed in 4.6 and later to GPS1_TYPE

GRIP_AUTOCLOSE.label: Gripper Autoclose time
GRIP_AUTOCLOSE.description: Time in seconds that gripper close the gripper after opening; 0 to disable

GRIP_GRAB.label: Gripper Grab PWM
GRIP_GRAB.description: PWM value in microseconds sent to Gripper to initiate grabbing the cargo

GRIP_NEUTRAL.label: Neutral PWM
GRIP_NEUTRAL.description: PWM value in microseconds sent to grabber when not grabbing or releasing

GRIP_REGRAB.label: EPM Gripper Regrab interval
GRIP_REGRAB.description: Time in seconds that EPM gripper will regrab the cargo to ensure grip has not weakened; 0 to disable

GRIP_RELEASE.label: Gripper Release PWM
GRIP_RELEASE.description: PWM value in microseconds sent to Gripper to release the cargo

GUID_OPTIONS.label: Guided mode options
GUID_OPTIONS.description: Options that can be applied to change guided mode behaviour

options.label: Waypoint navigation used for position targets

GUID_TIMEOUT.label: Guided mode timeout
GUID_TIMEOUT.description: Guided mode timeout after which vehicle will stop or return to level if no updates are received from caller. Only applicable during any combination of velocity, acceleration, angle control, and/or angular rate control

H_COL2YAW.label: Collective-Yaw Mixing
H_COL2YAW.description: Feed-forward compensation to automatically add rudder input when collective pitch is increased. Can be positive or negative depending on mechanics.

H_COL2_MAX.label: Swash 2 Maximum Collective Pitch
H_COL2_MAX.description: Highest possible servo position in PWM microseconds for swashplate 2

H_COL2_MIN.label: Swash 2 Minimum Collective Pitch
H_COL2_MIN.description: Lowest possible servo position in PWM microseconds for swashplate 2

H_COL_ANG_MAX.label: Collective Blade Pitch Angle Maximum
H_COL_ANG_MAX.description: Maximum collective blade pitch angle in deg that corresponds to the PWM set for maximum collective pitch (H_COL_MAX).

H_COL_ANG_MIN.label: Collective Blade Pitch Angle Minimum
H_COL_ANG_MIN.description: Minimum collective blade pitch angle in deg that corresponds to the PWM set for minimum collective pitch (H_COL_MIN).

H_COL_HOVER.label: Collective Hover Value
H_COL_HOVER.description: Collective needed to hover expressed as a number from 0 to 1 where 0 is H_COL_MIN and 1 is H_COL_MAX

H_COL_LAND_MIN.label: Collective Blade Pitch Minimum when Landed
H_COL_LAND_MIN.description: Minimum collective blade pitch angle when landed in degrees for non-manual collective modes (i.e. modes that use altitude hold).

H_COL_MAX.label: Maximum Collective Pitch
H_COL_MAX.description: Highest possible servo position in PWM microseconds for the swashplate

H_COL_MIN.label: Minimum Collective Pitch
H_COL_MIN.description: Lowest possible servo position in PWM microseconds for the swashplate

H_COL_ZERO_THRST.label: Collective Blade Pitch at Zero Thrust
H_COL_ZERO_THRST.description: Collective blade pitch angle at zero thrust in degrees. For symetric airfoil blades this value is zero deg. For chambered airfoil blades this value is typically negative.

H_CYC_MAX.label: Maximum Cyclic Pitch Angle
H_CYC_MAX.description: Maximum cyclic pitch angle of the swash plate.  There are no units to this parameter.  This should be adjusted to get the desired cyclic blade pitch for the pitch and roll axes.  Typically this should be 6-7 deg (measured blade pitch angle difference between stick centered and stick max deflection.

H_DCP_SCALER.label: Differential-Collective-Pitch Scaler
H_DCP_SCALER.description: Scaling factor applied to the differential-collective-pitch

H_DCP_TRIM.label: Differential Collective Pitch Trim
H_DCP_TRIM.description: Removes I term bias due to center of gravity offsets or discrepancies between rotors in swashplate setup. If DCP axis has I term bias while hovering in calm winds, use value of bias in DCP_TRIM to re-center I term.

H_DCP_YAW.label: Differential-Collective-Pitch Yaw Mixing
H_DCP_YAW.description: Feed-forward compensation to automatically add yaw input when differential collective pitch is applied.  Disabled for intermeshing mode.

H_DDFP_BAT_IDX.label: DDFP Tail Rotor Battery compensation index
H_DDFP_BAT_IDX.description: Which battery monitor should be used for doing compensation

H_DDFP_BAT_V_MAX.label: Battery voltage compensation maximum voltage
H_DDFP_BAT_V_MAX.description: Battery voltage compensation maximum voltage (voltage above this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 4.2 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

H_DDFP_BAT_V_MIN.label: Battery voltage compensation minimum voltage
H_DDFP_BAT_V_MIN.description: Battery voltage compensation minimum voltage (voltage below this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 3.3 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

H_DDFP_SPIN_MAX.label: DDFP Tail Rotor Motor Spin maximum
H_DDFP_SPIN_MAX.description: Point at which the thrust saturates expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range

H_DDFP_SPIN_MIN.label: DDFP Tail Rotor Motor Spin minimum
H_DDFP_SPIN_MIN.description: Point at which the thrust starts expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range.

H_DDFP_THST_EXPO.label: DDFP Tail Rotor Thrust Curve Expo
H_DDFP_THST_EXPO.description: Tail rotor DDFP motor thrust curve exponent (0.0 for linear to 1.0 for second order curve)

H_DUAL_MODE.label: Dual Mode
H_DUAL_MODE.description: Sets the dual mode of the heli, either as tandem, transverse, or intermeshing/coaxial.

H_HOVER_LEARN.label: Hover Value Learning
H_HOVER_LEARN.description: Enable/Disable automatic learning of hover collective

H_OPTIONS.label: Heli_Options
H_OPTIONS.description: Bitmask of heli options.  Bit 0 changes how the pitch, roll, and yaw axis integrator term is managed for low speed and takeoff/landing. In AC 4.0 and earlier, scheme uses a leaky integrator for ground speeds less than 5 m/s and won't let the steady state integrator build above ILMI. The integrator is allowed to build to the ILMI value when it is landed.  The other integrator management scheme bases integrator limiting on takeoff and landing.  Whenever the aircraft is landed the integrator is set to zero.  When the aicraft is airborne, the integrator is only limited by IMAX.

H_RSC_AROT_ENBL.label: Enable autorotation handling in RSC
H_RSC_AROT_ENBL.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the autorotation functionality within the Rotor Speed Controller.

H_RSC_AROT_IDLE.label: Idle throttle percentage during autorotation
H_RSC_AROT_IDLE.description: Idle throttle used for during autotoration. For external governors, this would be set to a value that is within the autorotation window of the governer/ESC to enable fast spool-up, when bailing out of an autorotation.  Set 0 to disable.

H_RSC_AROT_RAMP.label: Time for in-flight power re-engagement when exiting autorotations
H_RSC_AROT_RAMP.description: When exiting an autorotation in a bailout manoeuvre, this is the time in seconds for the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) to ramp from idle (H_RSC_AROT_IDLE) to flight throttle setting when motor interlock is re-enabled. When using an ESC with an autorotation bailout function, this parameter should be set to 0.1 (minimum value).

H_RSC_AROT_RUNUP.label: Time allowed for in-flight power re-engagement
H_RSC_AROT_RUNUP.description: When exiting an autorotation in a bailout manoeuvre, this is the expected time in seconds for the main rotor to reach full speed after motor interlock is enabled. Must be at least one second longer than the H_RSC_AROT_RAMP time that is set. This timer should be set for at least the amount of time it takes to get your helicopter to full flight power. Failure to heed this warning could result in early entry into autonomously controlled collective modes (e.g. alt hold, loiter, etc), whereby the collective could be raised before the engine has reached full power, with a subsequently dangerous slowing of head speed.

H_RSC_CLDWN_TIME.label: Cooldown Time
H_RSC_CLDWN_TIME.description: Will provide a fast idle for engine cooldown by raising the Ground Idle speed setting by 50% for the number of seconds the timer is set for. A setting of zero disables the fast idle. This feature will only apply after the runup complete has been declared. This will not extend the time before ground idle is declared, which triggers engine shutdown for autonomous landings.

H_RSC_CRITICAL.label: Critical Rotor Speed
H_RSC_CRITICAL.description: Percentage of normal rotor speed where flight is no longer possible. However currently the rotor runup/rundown is estimated using the RSC_RUNUP_TIME parameter.   Estimated rotor speed increases/decreases between 0 (rotor stopped) to 1 (rotor at normal speed) in the RSC_RUNUP_TIME in seconds. This parameter should be set so that the estimated rotor speed goes below critical in approximately 3 seconds.  So if you had a 10 second runup time then set RSC_CRITICAL to 70%.

H_RSC_GOV_COMP.label: Governor Torque Compensator
H_RSC_GOV_COMP.description: Adjusts the autothrottle governor torque compensator that determines how fast the governor will adjust the base torque reference to compensate for changes in density altitude. If RPM is low or high by more than 2-5 RPM, increase this setting by 1% at a time until the governor speed matches your RPM setting. Setting the compensator too high can result in surging and throttle "hunting". Do not make large adjustments at one time

H_RSC_GOV_DROOP.label: Governor Droop Compensator
H_RSC_GOV_DROOP.description: AutoThrottle governor droop response under load, normal settings of 0-50%. Higher value is quicker response to large speed changes due to load but may cause surging. Adjust this to be as aggressive as possible without getting surging or RPM over-run when the governor responds to large load changes on the rotor system

H_RSC_GOV_FF.label: Governor Feedforward
H_RSC_GOV_FF.description: Feedforward governor gain to throttle response during sudden loading/unloading of the rotor system. If RPM drops excessively during full collective climb with the droop response set correctly, increase the governor feedforward.

H_RSC_GOV_RANGE.label: Governor Operational Range
H_RSC_GOV_RANGE.description: RPM range +/- governor rpm reference setting where governor is operational. If speed sensor fails or rpm falls outside of this range, the governor will disengage and return to throttle curve. Recommended range is 100

H_RSC_GOV_RPM.label: Rotor RPM Setting
H_RSC_GOV_RPM.description: Main rotor RPM that governor maintains when engaged

H_RSC_GOV_TORQUE.label: Governor Torque Limiter
H_RSC_GOV_TORQUE.description: Adjusts the engine's percentage of torque rise on autothrottle during ramp-up to governor speed. The torque rise will determine how fast the rotor speed will ramp up when rotor speed reaches 50% of the rotor RPM setting. The sequence of events engaging the governor is as follows: Throttle ramp time will engage the clutch and start the main rotor turning. The collective should be at flat pitch and the throttle curve set to provide at least 50% of normal RPM at flat pitch. The autothrottle torque limiter will automatically activate and start accelerating the main rotor. If the autothrottle consistently fails to accelerate the main rotor during ramp-in due to engine tune or other factors, then increase the torque limiter setting. NOTE: throttle ramp time and throttle curve should be tuned using RSC_MODE Throttle Curve before using RSC_MODE AutoThrottle

H_RSC_IDLE.label: Throttle Output at Idle
H_RSC_IDLE.description: Throttle output (HeliRSC Servo) in percent while armed but motor interlock is disabled (throttle hold on). FOR COMBUSTION ENGINES. Sets the engine ground idle throttle percentage with clutch disengaged. This must be set to zero for electric helicopters under most situations. If the ESC has an autorotation window this can be set to keep the autorotation window open in the ESC. Consult the operating manual for your ESC to set it properly for this purpose

H_RSC_MODE.label: Rotor Speed Control Mode
H_RSC_MODE.description: Selects the type of rotor speed control used to determine throttle output to the HeliRSC servo channel when motor interlock is enabled (throttle hold off). RC Passthrough sends the input from the RC Motor Interlock channel as throttle output.  External Gov SetPoint sends the RSC SetPoint parameter value as throttle output.  Throttle Curve uses the 5 point throttle curve to determine throttle output based on the collective output.  AutoThrottle requires a rotor speed sensor, contains an advanced autothrottle governor and is primarily for piston and turbine engines. WARNING: Throttle ramp time and throttle curve MUST be tuned properly using Throttle Curve mode before using AutoThrottle

H_RSC_RAMP_TIME.label: Throttle Ramp Time
H_RSC_RAMP_TIME.description: Time in seconds for throttle output (HeliRSC servo) to ramp from ground idle (RSC_IDLE) to flight idle throttle setting when motor interlock is enabled (throttle hold off).

H_RSC_RUNUP_TIME.label: Rotor Runup Time
H_RSC_RUNUP_TIME.description: Actual time in seconds for the main rotor to reach full speed after motor interlock is enabled (throttle hold off). Must be at least one second longer than the Throttle Ramp Time that is set with RSC_RAMP_TIME. WARNING: For AutoThrottle users with piston and turbine engines it is VERY important to know how long it takes to warm up your engine and reach full rotor speed when throttle switch is turned ON. This timer should be set for at least the amount of time it takes to get your helicopter to full flight power, ready for takeoff. Failure to heed this warning could result in the auto-takeoff mode attempting to lift up into hover before the engine has reached full power, and subsequent loss of control

H_RSC_SETPOINT.label: External Motor Governor Setpoint
H_RSC_SETPOINT.description: Throttle (HeliRSC Servo) output in percent to the external motor governor when motor interlock enabled (throttle hold off).

H_RSC_SLEWRATE.label: Throttle Slew Rate
H_RSC_SLEWRATE.description: This controls the maximum rate at which the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) can change, as a percentage per second. A value of 100 means the throttle can change over its full range in one second. A value of zero gives unlimited slew rate.

H_RSC_THRCRV_0.label: Throttle Curve at 0% Coll
H_RSC_THRCRV_0.description: Sets the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) in percent for the throttle curve at the minimum collective pitch position. The 0 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MIN. Example: if the setup has -2 degree to +10 degree collective pitch setup, this setting would correspond to -2 degree of pitch.

H_RSC_THRCRV_100.label: Throttle Curve at 100% Coll
H_RSC_THRCRV_100.description: Sets the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) in percent for the throttle curve at the minimum collective pitch position. The 100 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MAX.  Example: if the setup has -2 degree to +10 degree collective pitch setup, this setting would correspond to +10 degree of pitch.

H_RSC_THRCRV_25.label: Throttle Curve at 25% Coll
H_RSC_THRCRV_25.description: Sets the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) in percent for the throttle curve at 25% of full collective travel where he 0 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MIN and 100 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MAX.  Example: if the setup has -2 degree to +10 degree collective pitch setup, the total range is 12 degrees. 25% of 12 degrees is 3 degrees, so this setting would correspond to +1 degree of pitch.

H_RSC_THRCRV_50.label: Throttle Curve at 50% Coll
H_RSC_THRCRV_50.description: Sets the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) in percent for the throttle curve at 50% of full collective travel where he 0 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MIN and 100 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MAX.  Example: if the setup has -2 degree to +10 degree collective pitch setup, the total range is 12 degrees. 50% of 12 degrees is 6 degrees, so this setting would correspond to +4 degree of pitch.

H_RSC_THRCRV_75.label: Throttle Curve at 75% Coll
H_RSC_THRCRV_75.description: Sets the throttle output (HeliRSC servo) in percent for the throttle curve at 75% of full collective travel where he 0 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MIN and 100 percent collective is defined by H_COL_MAX.  Example: if the setup has -2 degree to +10 degree collective pitch setup, the total range is 12 degrees. 75% of 12 degrees is 9 degrees, so this setting would correspond to +7 degree of pitch.

H_SV_MAN.label: Manual Servo Mode
H_SV_MAN.description: Manual servo override for swash set-up. Must be 0 (Disabled) for flight!

H_SV_TEST.label: Boot-up Servo Test Cycles
H_SV_TEST.description: Number of cycles to run servo test on boot-up

H_SW2_COL_DIR.label: Swash 2 Collective Direction
H_SW2_COL_DIR.description: Direction collective moves for positive pitch. 0 for Normal, 1 for Reversed

H_SW2_H3_ENABLE.label: Swash 2 H3 Generic Enable
H_SW2_H3_ENABLE.description: Automatically set when H3 generic swash type is selected for swashplate 2. Do not set manually.

H_SW2_H3_SV1_POS.label: Swash 2 H3 Generic Servo 1 Position
H_SW2_H3_SV1_POS.description: Azimuth position on swashplate for servo 1 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_SW2_H3_SV2_POS.label: Swash 2 H3 Generic Servo 2 Position
H_SW2_H3_SV2_POS.description: Azimuth position on swashplate 2 for servo 2 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_SW2_H3_SV3_POS.label: Swash 2 H3 Generic Servo 3 Position
H_SW2_H3_SV3_POS.description: Azimuth position on swashplate 2 for servo 3 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_SW2_LIN_SVO.label: Linearize Swash 2 Servos
H_SW2_LIN_SVO.description: This linearizes the swashplate 2 servo's mechanical output to account for nonlinear output due to arm rotation.  This requires a specific setup procedure to work properly.  The servo arm must be centered on the mechanical throw at the servo trim position and the servo trim position kept as close to 1500 as possible. Leveling the swashplate can only be done through the pitch links.  See the ardupilot wiki for more details on setup.

H_SW2_PHANG.label: Swash 2 Phase Angle Compensation
H_SW2_PHANG.description: Phase angle compensation can be used to correct control coupling issues.  If pitching the swash forward induces a roll, this can be correct the problem.

H_SW2_TYPE.label: Swash 2 Type
H_SW2_TYPE.description: H3 is generic, three-servo only. H3_120/H3_140 plates have Motor1 left side, Motor2 right side, Motor3 elevator in rear. HR3_120/HR3_140 have Motor1 right side, Motor2 left side, Motor3 elevator in front - use H3_120/H3_140 and reverse servo and collective directions as necessary. For all H3_90 swashplates use H4_90 and don't use servo output for the missing servo. For H4-90 Motors1&2 are left/right respectively, Motors3&4 are rear/front respectively. For H4-45 Motors1&2 are LF/RF, Motors3&4 are LR/RR

H_SW_COL_DIR.label: Swash 1 Collective Direction
H_SW_COL_DIR.description: Direction collective moves for positive pitch. 0 for Normal, 1 for Reversed

H_SW_H3_ENABLE.label: Swash 1 H3 Generic Enable
H_SW_H3_ENABLE.description: Automatically set when H3 generic swash type is selected for swashplate 1. Do not set manually.

H_SW_H3_SV1_POS.label: Swash 1 H3 Generic Servo 1 Position
H_SW_H3_SV1_POS.description: Azimuth position on swashplate for servo 1 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_SW_H3_SV2_POS.label: Swash 1 H3 Generic Servo 2 Position
H_SW_H3_SV2_POS.description: Azimuth position on swashplate 1 for servo 2 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_SW_H3_SV3_POS.label: Swash 1 H3 Generic Servo 3 Position
H_SW_H3_SV3_POS.description: Azimuth position on swashplate 1 for servo 3 with the front of the heli being 0 deg

H_SW_LIN_SVO.label: Linearize Swash 1 Servos
H_SW_LIN_SVO.description: This linearizes the swashplate 1 servo's mechanical output to account for nonlinear output due to arm rotation.  This requires a specific setup procedure to work properly.  The servo arm must be centered on the mechanical throw at the servo trim position and the servo trim position kept as close to 1500 as possible. Leveling the swashplate can only be done through the pitch links.  See the ardupilot wiki for more details on setup.

H_SW_PHANG.label: Swash 1 Phase Angle Compensation
H_SW_PHANG.description: Phase angle compensation can be used to correct control coupling issues.  If pitching the swash forward induces a roll, this can be correct the problem.

H_SW_TYPE.label: Swash 1 Type
H_SW_TYPE.description: H3 is generic, three-servo only. H3_120/H3_140 plates have Motor1 left side, Motor2 right side, Motor3 elevator in rear. HR3_120/HR3_140 have Motor1 right side, Motor2 left side, Motor3 elevator in front - use H3_120/H3_140 and reverse servo and collective directions as necessary. For all H3_90 swashplates use H4_90 and don't use servo output for the missing servo. For H4-90 Motors1&2 are left/right respectively, Motors3&4 are rear/front respectively. For H4-45 Motors1&2 are LF/RF, Motors3&4 are LR/RR

H_TAIL_SPEED.label: DDVP Tail ESC speed
H_TAIL_SPEED.description: Direct drive, variable pitch tail ESC speed in percent output to the tail motor esc (HeliTailRSC Servo) when motor interlock enabled (throttle hold off) and speed fully ramped up after spoolup.

H_TAIL_TYPE.label: Tail Type
H_TAIL_TYPE.description: Tail type selection. Servo Only uses tail rotor pitch to provide yaw control (including stabilization) via an output assigned to Motor4.  Servo with External Gyro uses an external gyro to control tail rotor pitch via a servo.  Yaw control without stabilization is passed to the external gyro via the output assigned to Motor4.  Direct Drive Variable Pitch(DDVP) is used for tails that have a motor whose ESC is connected to an output with function HeliTailRSC. Tail pitch is still accomplished with a servo on an output assigned to Motor4 function.  Direct Drive Fixed Pitch (DDFP) CW is used for helicopters with a rotor that spins clockwise when viewed from above with a motor whose ESC is controlled by an output whose function is Motor4. Direct Drive Fixed Pitch (DDFP) CCW is used for helicopters with a rotor that spins counter clockwise when viewed from above with a motor whose ESC is controlled by an output whose function is Motor4. In both DDFP cases, no servo is used for the tail and the tail motor esc on Motor4 output is used to control the yaw axis using motor speed.

H_YAW_REV_EXPO.label: Yaw reverser expo
H_YAW_REV_EXPO.description: For intermeshing mode only. Yaw revereser smoothing exponent, smoothen transition near zero collective region. Increase this parameter to shink smoothing range. Set to -1 to disable reverser.

H_YAW_SCALER.label: Scaler for yaw mixing
H_YAW_SCALER.description: Scaler for mixing yaw into roll or pitch.

H_YAW_TRIM.label: Tail Rotor Trim
H_YAW_TRIM.description: Fixed offset applied to yaw output to minimize yaw I-term contribution needed to counter rotor drag. Currently only works for DDFP tails (H_TAIL_TYPE = 3 or H_TAIL_TYPE = 4). If using the H_COL2YAW compensation this trim is used to compensate for the main rotor profile drag. If H_COL2YAW is not used, this value can be set to reduce the yaw I-term to zero when in a steady hover. In this case, move trim in the direction of the I-term found in PIDY.I log message (e.g. negative I means you want negative trim).

IM_ACRO_COL_EXP.label: Acro Mode Collective Expo
IM_ACRO_COL_EXP.description: Used to soften collective pitch inputs near center point in Acro mode.

IM_STB_COL_1.label: Stabilize Collective Low
IM_STB_COL_1.description: Helicopter's minimum collective pitch setting at zero collective stick input in Stabilize mode.  Set this as a percent of collective range given by H_COL_MAX minus H_COL_MIN.

IM_STB_COL_2.label: Stabilize Collective Mid-Low
IM_STB_COL_2.description: Helicopter's collective pitch setting at mid-low (40%) collective stick input in Stabilize mode. Set this as a percent of collective range given by H_COL_MAX minus H_COL_MIN.

IM_STB_COL_3.label: Stabilize Collective Mid-High
IM_STB_COL_3.description: Helicopter's collective pitch setting at mid-high (60%) collective stick input in Stabilize mode. Set this as a percent of collective range given by H_COL_MAX minus H_COL_MIN.

IM_STB_COL_4.label: Stabilize Collective High
IM_STB_COL_4.description: Helicopter's maximum collective pitch setting at full collective stick input in Stabilize mode. Set this as a percent of collective range given by H_COL_MAX minus H_COL_MIN.

INITIAL_MODE.label: Initial flight mode
INITIAL_MODE.description: This selects the mode to start in on boot. This is useful for when you want to start in AUTO mode on boot without a receiver.

INS4_ACCOFFS_X.label: Accelerometer offsets of X axis
INS4_ACCOFFS_X.description: Accelerometer offsets of X axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS4_ACCOFFS_Y.label: Accelerometer offsets of Y axis
INS4_ACCOFFS_Y.description: Accelerometer offsets of Y axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS4_ACCOFFS_Z.label: Accelerometer offsets of Z axis
INS4_ACCOFFS_Z.description: Accelerometer offsets of Z axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS4_ACCSCAL_X.label: Accelerometer scaling of X axis
INS4_ACCSCAL_X.description: Accelerometer scaling of X axis.  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS4_ACCSCAL_Y.label: Accelerometer scaling of Y axis
INS4_ACCSCAL_Y.description: Accelerometer scaling of Y axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS4_ACCSCAL_Z.label: Accelerometer scaling of Z axis
INS4_ACCSCAL_Z.description: Accelerometer scaling of Z axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS4_ACC_CALTEMP.label: Calibration temperature for accelerometer
INS4_ACC_CALTEMP.description: Temperature that the accelerometer was calibrated at

INS4_ACC_ID.label: Accelerometer ID
INS4_ACC_ID.description: Accelerometer sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS4_GYROFFS_X.label: Gyro offsets of X axis
INS4_GYROFFS_X.description: Gyro sensor offsets of X axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS4_GYROFFS_Y.label: Gyro offsets of Y axis
INS4_GYROFFS_Y.description: Gyro sensor offsets of Y axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS4_GYROFFS_Z.label: Gyro offsets of Z axis
INS4_GYROFFS_Z.description: Gyro sensor offsets of Z axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS4_GYR_CALTEMP.label: Calibration temperature for gyroscope
INS4_GYR_CALTEMP.description: Temperature that the gyroscope was calibrated at

INS4_GYR_ID.label: Gyro ID
INS4_GYR_ID.description: Gyro sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS4_POS_X.label: IMU accelerometer X position
INS4_POS_X.description: X position of the first IMU Accelerometer in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS4_POS_Y.label: IMU accelerometer Y position
INS4_POS_Y.description: Y position of the first IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS4_POS_Z.label: IMU accelerometer Z position
INS4_POS_Z.description: Z position of the first IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS4_TCAL_ACC1_X.label: Accelerometer 1st order temperature coefficient X axis
INS4_TCAL_ACC1_X.description: This is the 1st order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC1_Y.label: Accelerometer 1st order temperature coefficient Y axis
INS4_TCAL_ACC1_Y.description: This is the 1st order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC1_Z.label: Accelerometer 1st order temperature coefficient Z axis
INS4_TCAL_ACC1_Z.description: This is the 1st order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC2_X.label: Accelerometer 2nd order temperature coefficient X axis
INS4_TCAL_ACC2_X.description: This is the 2nd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC2_Y.label: Accelerometer 2nd order temperature coefficient Y axis
INS4_TCAL_ACC2_Y.description: This is the 2nd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC2_Z.label: Accelerometer 2nd order temperature coefficient Z axis
INS4_TCAL_ACC2_Z.description: This is the 2nd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC3_X.label: Accelerometer 3rd order temperature coefficient X axis
INS4_TCAL_ACC3_X.description: This is the 3rd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC3_Y.label: Accelerometer 3rd order temperature coefficient Y axis
INS4_TCAL_ACC3_Y.description: This is the 3rd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ACC3_Z.label: Accelerometer 3rd order temperature coefficient Z axis
INS4_TCAL_ACC3_Z.description: This is the 3rd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_ENABLE.label: Enable temperature calibration
INS4_TCAL_ENABLE.description: Enable the use of temperature calibration parameters for this IMU. For automatic learning set to 2 and also set the INS_TCALn_TMAX to the target temperature, then reboot

INS4_TCAL_GYR1_X.label: Gyroscope 1st order temperature coefficient X axis
INS4_TCAL_GYR1_X.description: This is the 1st order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_GYR1_Y.label: Gyroscope 1st order temperature coefficient Y axis
INS4_TCAL_GYR1_Y.description: This is the 1st order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_GYR1_Z.label: Gyroscope 1st order temperature coefficient Z axis
INS4_TCAL_GYR1_Z.description: This is the 1st order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_GYR2_X.label: Gyroscope 2nd order temperature coefficient X axis
INS4_TCAL_GYR2_X.description: This is the 2nd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_GYR2_Y.label: Gyroscope 2nd order temperature coefficient Y axis
INS4_TCAL_GYR2_Y.description: This is the 2nd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_GYR2_Z.label: Gyroscope 2nd order temperature coefficient Z axis
INS4_TCAL_GYR2_Z.description: This is the 2nd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_GYR3_X.label: Gyroscope 3rd order temperature coefficient X axis
INS4_TCAL_GYR3_X.description: This is the 3rd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_GYR3_Y.label: Gyroscope 3rd order temperature coefficient Y axis
INS4_TCAL_GYR3_Y.description: This is the 3rd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_GYR3_Z.label: Gyroscope 3rd order temperature coefficient Z axis
INS4_TCAL_GYR3_Z.description: This is the 3rd order temperature coefficient from a temperature calibration

INS4_TCAL_TMAX.label: Temperature calibration max
INS4_TCAL_TMAX.description: The maximum temperature that the calibration is valid for. This must be at least 10 degrees above TMIN for calibration

INS4_TCAL_TMIN.label: Temperature calibration min
INS4_TCAL_TMIN.description: The minimum temperature that the calibration is valid for

INS4_USE.label: Use first IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates
INS4_USE.description: Use first IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates

INS5_ACCOFFS_X.label: Accelerometer offsets of X axis
INS5_ACCOFFS_X.description: Accelerometer offsets of X axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS5_ACCOFFS_Y.label: Accelerometer offsets of Y axis
INS5_ACCOFFS_Y.description: Accelerometer offsets of Y axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS5_ACCOFFS_Z.label: Accelerometer offsets of Z axis
INS5_ACCOFFS_Z.description: Accelerometer offsets of Z axis. This is setup using the acceleration calibration or level operations

INS5_ACCSCAL_X.label: Accelerometer scaling of X axis
INS5_ACCSCAL_X.description: Accelerometer scaling of X axis.  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS5_ACCSCAL_Y.label: Accelerometer scaling of Y axis
INS5_ACCSCAL_Y.description: Accelerometer scaling of Y axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS5_ACCSCAL_Z.label: Accelerometer scaling of Z axis
INS5_ACCSCAL_Z.description: Accelerometer scaling of Z axis  Calculated during acceleration calibration routine

INS5_ACC_CALTEMP.label: Calibration temperature for accelerometer
INS5_ACC_CALTEMP.description: Temperature that the accelerometer was calibrated at

INS5_ACC_ID.label: Accelerometer ID
INS5_ACC_ID.description: Accelerometer sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS5_GYROFFS_X.label: Gyro offsets of X axis

INS5_GYR_CALTEMP.description: Temperature that the gyroscope was calibrated at

INS5_GYR_ID.label: Gyro ID
INS5_GYR_ID.description: Gyro sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS5_POS_X.label: IMU accelerometer X position
INS5_POS_X.description: X position of the first IMU Accelerometer in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS5_POS_Y.label: IMU accelerometer Y position
INS5_POS_Y.description: Y position of the first IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS5_POS_Z.label: IMU accelerometer Z position
INS5_POS_Z.description: Z position of the first IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_ACC_BODYFIX.description: The body-fixed accelerometer to be used for trim calculation

INS_ACC_ID.description: Accelerometer sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS_ENABLE_MASK.label: IMU enable mask
INS_ENABLE_MASK.description: Bitmask of IMUs to enable. It can be used to prevent startup of specific detected IMUs

INS_FAST_SAMPLE.label: Fast sampling mask
INS_FAST_SAMPLE.description: Mask of IMUs to enable fast sampling on, if available

INS_GYR3OFFS_Z.description: Gyro3 sensor offsets of Z axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYR3_CALTEMP.description: Temperature that the 3rd gyroscope was calibrated at

INS_GYR3_ID.label: Gyro3 ID
INS_GYR3_ID.description: Gyro3 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS_GYROFFS_X.label: Gyro offsets of X axis
INS_GYROFFS_X.description: Gyro sensor offsets of X axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYROFFS_Y.label: Gyro offsets of Y axis
INS_GYROFFS_Y.description: Gyro sensor offsets of Y axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYROFFS_Z.label: Gyro offsets of Z axis
INS_GYROFFS_Z.description: Gyro sensor offsets of Z axis. This is setup on each boot during gyro calibrations

INS_GYRO_FILTER.label: Gyro filter cutoff frequency
INS_GYRO_FILTER.description: Filter cutoff frequency for gyroscopes. This can be set to a lower value to try to cope with very high vibration levels in aircraft. A value of zero means no filtering (not recommended!)

INS_GYRO_RATE.label: Gyro rate for IMUs with Fast Sampling enabled
INS_GYRO_RATE.description: Gyro rate for IMUs with fast sampling enabled. The gyro rate is the sample rate at which the IMU filters operate and needs to be at least double the maximum filter frequency. If the sensor does not support the selected rate the next highest supported rate will be used. For IMUs which do not support fast sampling this setting is ignored and the default gyro rate of 1Khz is used.

INS_GYR_CAL.label: Gyro Calibration scheme
INS_GYR_CAL.description: Conrols when automatic gyro calibration is performed

INS_GYR_ID.label: Gyro ID
INS_GYR_ID.description: Gyro sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

INS_HNTC2_ATT.label: Harmonic Notch Filter attenuation
INS_HNTC2_ATT.description: Harmonic Notch Filter attenuation in dB. Values greater than 40dB will typically produce a hard notch rather than a modest attenuation of motor noise.

INS_HNTC2_BW.label: Harmonic Notch Filter bandwidth
INS_HNTC2_BW.description: Harmonic Notch Filter bandwidth in Hz. This is typically set to half the base frequency. The ratio of base frequency to bandwidth determines the notch quality factor and is fixed across harmonics.

INS_HNTC2_FM_RAT.label: Throttle notch min frequency ratio
INS_HNTC2_FM_RAT.description: The minimum ratio below the configured frequency to take throttle based notch filters when flying at a throttle level below the reference throttle. Note that lower frequency notch filters will have more phase lag. If you want throttle based notch filtering to be effective at a throttle up to 30% below the configured notch frequency then set this parameter to 0.7. The default of 1.0 means the notch will not go below the frequency in the FREQ parameter.

INS_HNTC2_FREQ.label: Harmonic Notch Filter base frequency
INS_HNTC2_FREQ.description: Harmonic Notch Filter base center frequency in Hz. This is the center frequency for static notches, the center frequency for Throttle based notches at the reference thrust value, and the minimum limit of center frequency variation for all other notch types. This should always be set lower than half the backend gyro rate (which is typically 1Khz).

INS_HNTC2_HMNCS.label: Harmonic Notch Filter harmonics
INS_HNTC2_HMNCS.description: Bitmask of harmonic frequencies to apply Harmonic Notch Filter to. This option takes effect on the next reboot. A value of 0 disables this filter. The first harmonic refers to the base frequency.

INS_HNTC2_MODE.label: Harmonic Notch Filter dynamic frequency tracking mode
INS_HNTC2_MODE.description: Harmonic Notch Filter dynamic frequency tracking mode. Dynamic updates can be throttle, RPM sensor, ESC telemetry or dynamic FFT based. Throttle-based harmonic notch cannot be used on fixed wing only planes. It can for Copters, QuadPlane(while in VTOL modes), and Rovers.

INS_HNTC2_OPTS.label: Harmonic Notch Filter options
INS_HNTC2_OPTS.description: Harmonic Notch Filter options. Triple and double-notches can provide deeper attenuation across a wider bandwidth with reduced latency than single notches and are suitable for larger aircraft. Multi-Source attaches a harmonic notch to each detected noise frequency instead of simply being multiples of the base frequency, in the case of FFT it will attach notches to each of three detected noise peaks, in the case of ESC it will attach notches to each of four motor RPM values. Loop rate update changes the notch center frequency at the scheduler loop rate rather than at the default of 200Hz. If both double and triple notches are specified only double notches will take effect.

options.label: Use min freq on RPM source failure

INS_HNTC2_REF.label: Harmonic Notch Filter reference value
INS_HNTC2_REF.description: A reference value of zero disables dynamic updates on the Harmonic Notch Filter and a positive value enables dynamic updates on the Harmonic Notch Filter.  For throttle-based scaling, this parameter is the reference value associated with the specified frequency to facilitate frequency scaling of the Harmonic Notch Filter. For RPM and ESC telemetry based tracking, this parameter is set to 1 to enable the Harmonic Notch Filter using the RPM sensor or ESC telemetry set to measure rotor speed.  The sensor data is converted to Hz automatically for use in the Harmonic Notch Filter.  This reference value may also be used to scale the sensor data, if required.  For example, rpm sensor data is required to measure heli motor RPM. Therefore the reference value can be used to scale the RPM sensor to the rotor RPM.

INS_LOG_BAT_CNT.label: sample count per batch
INS_LOG_BAT_CNT.description: Number of samples to take when logging streams of IMU sensor readings.  Will be rounded down to a multiple of 32. This option takes effect on the next reboot.

INS_LOG_BAT_LGCT.label: logging count
INS_LOG_BAT_LGCT.description: Number of samples to push to count every INS_LOG_BAT_LGIN

INS_LOG_BAT_LGIN.label: logging interval
INS_LOG_BAT_LGIN.description: Interval between pushing samples to the AP_Logger log

INS_LOG_BAT_MASK.label: Sensor Bitmask
INS_LOG_BAT_MASK.description: Bitmap of which IMUs to log batch data for. This option takes effect on the next reboot.

options.label: Sensor-Rate Logging (sample at full sensor rate seen by AP)

INS_POS1_X.label: IMU accelerometer X position
INS_POS1_X.description: X position of the first IMU Accelerometer in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS2_X.label: IMU accelerometer X position
INS_POS2_X.description: X position of the second IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS2_Y.label: IMU accelerometer Y position
INS_POS2_Y.description: Y position of the second IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS2_Z.label: IMU accelerometer Z position
INS_POS2_Z.description: Z position of the second IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS3_X.label: IMU accelerometer X position
INS_POS3_X.description: X position of the third IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS3_Y.label: IMU accelerometer Y position
INS_POS3_Y.description: Y position of the third IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_POS3_Z.label: IMU accelerometer Z position
INS_POS3_Z.description: Z position of the third IMU accelerometer in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Attention: The IMU should be located as close to the vehicle c.g. as practical so that the value of this parameter is minimised. Failure to do so can result in noisy navigation velocity measurements due to vibration and IMU gyro noise. If the IMU cannot be moved and velocity noise is a problem, a location closer to the IMU can be used as the body frame origin.

INS_STILL_THRESH.label: Stillness threshold for detecting if we are moving
INS_STILL_THRESH.description: Threshold to tolerate vibration to determine if vehicle is motionless. This depends on the frame type and if there is a constant vibration due to motors before launch or after landing. Total motionless is about 0.05. Suggested values: Planes/rover use 0.1, multirotors use 1, tradHeli uses 5
