INS_TCAL_OPTIONS.label: Options for temperature calibration
INS_TCAL_OPTIONS.description: This enables optional temperature calibration features. Setting of the Persist bits will save the temperature and/or accelerometer calibration parameters in the bootloader sector on the next update of the bootloader.

INS_TRIM_OPTION.label: Accel cal trim option
INS_TRIM_OPTION.description: Specifies how the accel cal routine determines the trims

options.label: Assume ACC_BODYFIX is perfectly aligned to the vehicle

INS_USE.label: Use first IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates
INS_USE.description: Use first IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates

INS_USE2.label: Use second IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates
INS_USE2.description: Use second IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates

INS_USE3.label: Use third IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates
INS_USE3.description: Use third IMU for attitude, velocity and position estimates

KDE_NPOLE.label: Number of motor poles
KDE_NPOLE.description: Sets the number of motor poles to calculate the correct RPM value

LAND_ALT_LOW_M.label: Land alt low
LAND_ALT_LOW_M.description: Altitude during Landing at which vehicle slows to LAND_SPD_MS

LAND_REPOSITION.label: Land repositioning
LAND_REPOSITION.description: Enables user input during LAND mode, the landing phase of RTL, and auto mode landings.

LAND_SPD_HIGH_MS.label: Land speed high
LAND_SPD_HIGH_MS.description: The descent speed for the first stage of landing in m/s. If this is zero then WP_SPD_DN is used

LAND_SPD_MS.label: Land speed
LAND_SPD_MS.description: The descent speed for the final stage of landing in m/s

LGR_DEPLOY_ALT.label: Landing gear deployment altitude
LGR_DEPLOY_ALT.description: Altitude where the landing gear will be deployed. This should be lower than the RETRACT_ALT. If zero then altitude is not used for deploying landing gear. Only applies when vehicle is armed.

LGR_DEPLOY_PIN.label: Chassis deployment feedback pin
LGR_DEPLOY_PIN.description: Pin number to use for detection of gear deployment. If set to -1 feedback is disabled. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

LGR_DEPLOY_POL.label: Chassis deployment feedback pin polarity
LGR_DEPLOY_POL.description: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the pin should be high when gear are deployed. If set to 0 then then deployed gear level is low.

LGR_OPTIONS.label: Landing gear auto retract/deploy options
LGR_OPTIONS.description: Options to retract or deploy landing gear in Auto or Guided mode

LGR_RETRACT_ALT.label: Landing gear retract altitude
LGR_RETRACT_ALT.description: Altitude where the landing gear will be retracted. This should be higher than the DEPLOY_ALT. If zero then altitude is not used for retracting landing gear. Only applies when vehicle is armed.

LGR_WOW_PIN.label: Weight on wheels feedback pin
LGR_WOW_PIN.description: Pin number to use for feedback of weight on wheels condition. If set to -1 feedback is disabled. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

LGR_WOW_POL.label: Weight on wheels feedback pin polarity
LGR_WOW_POL.description: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the pin should be high when there is weight on wheels. If set to 0 then then weight on wheels level is low.

LOG_BACKEND_TYPE.label: AP_Logger Backend Storage type
LOG_BACKEND_TYPE.description: Bitmap of what Logger backend types to enable. Block-based logging is available on SITL and boards with dataflash chips. Multiple backends can be selected.

LOG_BITMASK.label: Log bitmask
LOG_BITMASK.description: Bitmap of what on-board log types to enable. This value is made up of the sum of each of the log types you want to be saved. It is usually best just to enable all basiclog types by setting this to 65535. Note that if you want to reduce log sizes you should consider using LOG_FILE_RATEMAX instead of disabling logging items with this parameter.

LOG_BLK_RATEMAX.label: Maximum logging rate for block backend
LOG_BLK_RATEMAX.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the block backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

LOG_DARM_RATEMAX.label: Maximum logging rate when disarmed
LOG_DARM_RATEMAX.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to any backend when disarmed. A value of zero means that the normal backend rate limit is applied.

LOG_DISARMED.label: Enable logging while disarmed
LOG_DISARMED.description: If LOG_DISARMED is set to 1 then logging will be enabled at all times including when disarmed. Logging before arming can make for very large logfiles but can help a lot when tracking down startup issues and is necessary if logging of EKF replay data is selected via the LOG_REPLAY parameter. If LOG_DISARMED is set to 2, then logging will be enabled when disarmed, but not if a USB connection is detected. This can be used to prevent unwanted data logs being generated when the vehicle is connected via USB for log downloading or parameter changes. If LOG_DISARMED is set to 3 then logging will happen while disarmed, but if the vehicle never arms then the logs using the filesystem backend will be discarded on the next boot.

options.label: Discard log on reboot if never armed

LOG_FILE_BUFSIZE.label: Logging File and Block Backend buffer size max (in kibibytes)
LOG_FILE_BUFSIZE.description: The File and Block backends use a buffer to store data before writing to the block device.  Raising this value may reduce "gaps" in your SD card logging but increases memory usage.  This buffer size may be reduced to free up available memory

LOG_FILE_DSRMROT.label: Stop logging to current file on disarm
LOG_FILE_DSRMROT.description: When set, the current log file is closed when the vehicle is disarmed.  If LOG_DISARMED is set then a fresh log will be opened. Applies to the File and Block logging backends.

LOG_FILE_MB_FREE.label: Old logs on the SD card will be deleted to maintain this amount of free space
LOG_FILE_MB_FREE.description: Set this such that the free space is larger than your largest typical flight log

LOG_FILE_RATEMAX.label: Maximum logging rate for file backend
LOG_FILE_RATEMAX.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the file backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

LOG_FILE_TIMEOUT.label: Timeout before giving up on file writes
LOG_FILE_TIMEOUT.description: This controls the amount of time before failing writes to a log file cause the file to be closed and logging stopped.

LOG_MAV_BUFSIZE.label: Maximum AP_Logger MAVLink Backend buffer size
LOG_MAV_BUFSIZE.description: Maximum amount of memory to allocate to AP_Logger-over-mavlink

LOG_MAV_RATEMAX.label: Maximum logging rate for mavlink backend
LOG_MAV_RATEMAX.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the mavlink backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

LOG_MAX_FILES.label: Maximum number of log files
LOG_MAX_FILES.description: This sets the maximum number of log file that will be written on dataflash or sd card before starting to rotate log number. Limit is capped at 500 logs.

LOG_REPLAY.label: Enable logging of information needed for Replay
LOG_REPLAY.description: If LOG_REPLAY is set to 1 then the EKF2 and EKF3 state estimators will log detailed information needed for diagnosing problems with the Kalman filter. LOG_DISARMED must be set to 1 or 2 or else the log will not contain the pre-flight data required for replay testing of the EKF's. It is suggested that you also raise LOG_FILE_BUFSIZE to give more buffer space for logging and use a high quality microSD card to ensure no sensor data is lost.

LOIT_ACC_MAX_M.label: Loiter maximum correction acceleration
LOIT_ACC_MAX_M.description: Loiter maximum correction acceleration in m/s/s.  Higher values cause the copter to correct position errors more aggressively.

LOIT_ANG_MAX.label: Loiter pilot angle max
LOIT_ANG_MAX.description: Loiter maximum pilot requested lean angle. Set to zero for 2/3 of PSC_ANGLE_MAX/ATC_ANGLE_MAX. The maximum vehicle lean angle is still limited by PSC_ANGLE_MAX/ATC_ANGLE_MAX

LOIT_BRK_ACC_M.label: Loiter braking acceleration
LOIT_BRK_ACC_M.description: Loiter braking acceleration in m/s/s. Higher values stop the copter more quickly when the stick is centered.

LOIT_BRK_JRK_M.label: Loiter braking jerk
LOIT_BRK_JRK_M.description: Loiter braking jerk in m/s/s/s. Higher values will remove braking faster if the pilot moves the sticks during a braking maneuver.

LOIT_SPEED_MS.label: Loiter Horizontal Maximum Speed
LOIT_SPEED_MS.description: Defines the maximum speed in m/s which the aircraft will travel horizontally while in loiter mode

LTE_BAND.label: LTE band selection
LTE_BAND.description: This allows selection of LTE band. A value of -1 means no band setting change is made. A value of 0 sets all bands. Otherwise the specified band is set.

LTE_BAUD.label: Serial Baud Rate
LTE_BAUD.description: Baud rate for the serial port to the LTE modem when connected. Initial power on baudrate is in LTE_IBAUD

LTE_ENABLE.label: LTE Enable
LTE_ENABLE.description: Enable or disable the LTE modem driver

LTE_IBAUD.label: LTE initial baudrate
LTE_IBAUD.description: This is the initial baud rate on power on for the modem. This is set in the modem with the AT+IREX=baud command

LTE_MCCMNC.label: LTE operator selection
LTE_MCCMNC.description: This allows selection of network operator

LTE_OPTIONS.label: LTE options
LTE_OPTIONS.description: Options to control the LTE modem driver. If VerboseSignalInfoGCS is set then additional NAMED_VALUE_FLOAT values are sent with verbose signal information

LTE_PROTOCOL.label: LTE protocol
LTE_PROTOCOL.description: The protocol that we will use in communication with the LTE modem. If this is PPP then the LTE_SERVER parameters are not used and instead a PPP connection will be established and you should use the NET_ parameters to enable network ports. If this is MAVLink2 then the LTE_SERVER parameters are used to create a TCP or UDP connection to a single server.

LTE_SCRPORT.label: Scripting Serial Port
LTE_SCRPORT.description: Scripting Serial port to use for the LTE modem. This is the index of the SCR_SDEV ports that are set to 2 for "MAVLink2"

LTE_SERPORT.label: Serial Port
LTE_SERPORT.description: Serial port to use for the LTE modem. This is the index of the SERIALn_ ports that are set to 28 for "scripting"

LTE_SERVER_IP0.label: Server IP 0
LTE_SERVER_IP0.description: First octet of the server IP address to connect to

LTE_SERVER_IP1.label: Server IP 1
LTE_SERVER_IP1.description: Second octet of the server IP address to connect to

LTE_SERVER_IP2.label: Server IP 2
LTE_SERVER_IP2.description: Third octet of the server IP address to connect to

LTE_SERVER_IP3.label: Server IP 3
LTE_SERVER_IP3.description: Fourth octet of the server IP address to connect to

LTE_SERVER_PORT.label: Server Port
LTE_SERVER_PORT.description: IPv4 Port of the server to connect to

LTE_TIMEOUT.label: Timeout
LTE_TIMEOUT.description: Timeout in seconds for the LTE connection. If no data is received for this time, the connection will be reset. A value of zero disables the timeout

LTE_TX_RATE.label: Max transmit rate
LTE_TX_RATE.description: Maximum data transmit rate to the modem in bytes/second. Use zero for unlimited

MAV10_EXTRA1.label: Extra data type 1 stream rate
MAV10_EXTRA1.description: MAVLink Stream rate of ATTITUDE, SIMSTATE (SIM only), AHRS2, RPM, AOA_SSA, LANDING,ESC_TELEMETRY,EFI_STATUS, and PID_TUNING

MAV10_EXTRA3.label: Extra data type 3 stream rate
MAV10_EXTRA3.description: MAVLink Stream rate of AHRS, SYSTEM_TIME, WIND, RANGEFINDER, DISTANCE_SENSOR, TERRAIN_REQUEST, TERRAIN_REPORT, GIMBAL_DEVICE_ATTITUDE_STATUS, OPTICAL_FLOW, MAG_CAL_REPORT, MAG_CAL_PROGRESS, EKF_STATUS_REPORT, VIBRATION, and BATTERY_STATUS

MAV10_EXT_STAT.label: Extended status stream rate
MAV10_EXT_STAT.description: MAVLink Stream rate of SYS_STATUS, POWER_STATUS, MCU_STATUS, MEMINFO, CURRENT_WAYPOINT, GPS_RAW_INT, GPS_RTK (if available), GPS2_RAW_INT (if available), GPS2_RTK (if available), NAV_CONTROLLER_OUTPUT, FENCE_STATUS, and GLOBAL_TARGET_POS_INT

MAV10_OPTIONS.label: Bitmask for configuring this telemetry channel
MAV10_OPTIONS.description: Bitmask for configuring this telemetry channel. For having effect on all channels, set the relevant mask in all MAVx_OPTIONS parameters. Keep in mind that part of the flags may require a reboot to take action.

options.label: forward mavlink packets that don't pass CRC

MAV10_POSITION.label: Position stream rate
MAV10_POSITION.description: MAVLink Stream rate of GLOBAL_POSITION_INT and LOCAL_POSITION_NED

MAV10_RAW_CTRL.label: Raw Control stream rate
MAV10_RAW_CTRL.description: MAVLink Raw Control stream rate of SERVO_OUT

MAV10_RAW_SENS.label: Raw sensor stream rate
MAV10_RAW_SENS.description: MAVLink Stream rate of RAW_IMU, SCALED_IMU2, SCALED_IMU3, SCALED_PRESSURE, SCALED_PRESSURE2, SCALED_PRESSURE3 and AIRSPEED

MAV10_RC_CHAN.label: RC Channel stream rate

MAV_GCS_SYSID.label: My ground station number
MAV_GCS_SYSID.description: This sets what MAVLink source system IDs are accepted for GCS failsafe handling, RC overrides and manual control. When MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI is less than MAV_GCS_SYSID then only this value is considered to be a GCS. When MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI is greater than or equal to MAV_GCS_SYSID then the range of values between MAV_GCS_SYSID and MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI (inclusive) are all treated as valid GCS MAVLink system IDs

MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI.label: ground station system ID, maximum
MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI.description: Upper limit of MAVLink source system IDs considered to be from the GCS. When this is less than MAV_GCS_SYSID then only MAV_GCS_SYSID is used as GCS ID. When this is greater than or equal to MAV_GCS_SYSID then the range of values from MAV_GCS_SYSID to MAV_GCS_SYSID_HI (inclusive) is treated as a GCS ID.

MAV_OPTIONS.label: MAVLink Options
MAV_OPTIONS.description: Alters various behaviour of the MAVLink interface

options.label: Accept MAVLink only from system IDs given by MAV_SYSID_GCS and MAV_SYSID_GCS_HI

MAV_SYSID.label: MAVLink system ID of this vehicle
MAV_SYSID.description: Allows setting an individual MAVLink system id for this vehicle to distinguish it from others on the same network.

MAV_TELEM_DELAY.label: Telemetry startup delay
MAV_TELEM_DELAY.description: The amount of time (in seconds) to delay radio telemetry to prevent an Xbee bricking on power up

MIS_OPTIONS.label: Mission options bitmask
MIS_OPTIONS.description: Bitmask of what options to use in missions. If the DontZeroCounter counter option is set than on completion of a jump loop the counter is left at zero, so the jump will not happen again if the loop is re-entered.

MIS_RESTART.label: Mission Restart when entering Auto mode
MIS_RESTART.description: Controls mission starting point when entering Auto mode (either restart from beginning of mission or resume from last command run)

MIS_TOTAL.label: Total mission commands
MIS_TOTAL.description: The number of mission mission items that has been loaded by the ground station. Do not change this manually.

MNT1_DEFLT_MODE.label: Mount default operating mode
MNT1_DEFLT_MODE.description: Mount default operating mode on startup and after control is returned from autopilot

MNT1_DEVID.label: Mount Device ID
MNT1_DEVID.description: Mount device ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

MNT1_LEAD_PTCH.label: Mount Pitch stabilization lead time
MNT1_LEAD_PTCH.description: Servo mount pitch angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current pitch rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT1_LEAD_RLL.label: Mount Roll stabilization lead time
MNT1_LEAD_RLL.description: Servo mount roll angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current roll rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT1_NEUTRAL_X.label: Mount roll angle when in neutral position
MNT1_NEUTRAL_X.description: Mount roll angle when in neutral position

MNT1_NEUTRAL_Y.label: Mount pitch angle when in neutral position
MNT1_NEUTRAL_Y.description: Mount pitch angle when in neutral position

MNT1_NEUTRAL_Z.label: Mount yaw angle when in neutral position
MNT1_NEUTRAL_Z.description: Mount yaw angle when in neutral position

MNT1_OPTIONS.label: Mount options
MNT1_OPTIONS.description: Mount options bitmask, note bit 2 only impacts RC targetting mode

options.label: RC lock state from previous mode
Return to neutral angles on RC failsafe
Force FPV (bf) lock on roll and pitch

MNT1_RC_RATE.label: Mount RC Rate
MNT1_RC_RATE.description: Pilot rate control's maximum rate.  Set to zero to use angle control

MNT1_RETRACT_X.label: Mount roll angle when in retracted position
MNT1_RETRACT_X.description: Mount roll angle when in retracted position

MNT1_RETRACT_Y.label: Mount pitch angle when in retracted position
MNT1_RETRACT_Y.description: Mount pitch angle when in retracted position

MNT1_RETRACT_Z.label: Mount yaw angle when in retracted position
MNT1_RETRACT_Z.description: Mount yaw angle when in retracted position

MNT1_SYSID_DFLT.label: Mount Target sysID
MNT1_SYSID_DFLT.description: Default Target sysID for the mount to point to

MNT2_DEFLT_MODE.label: Mount default operating mode
MNT2_DEFLT_MODE.description: Mount default operating mode on startup and after control is returned from autopilot

MNT2_DEVID.label: Mount Device ID
MNT2_DEVID.description: Mount device ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

MNT2_LEAD_PTCH.label: Mount Pitch stabilization lead time
MNT2_LEAD_PTCH.description: Servo mount pitch angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current pitch rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT2_LEAD_RLL.label: Mount Roll stabilization lead time
MNT2_LEAD_RLL.description: Servo mount roll angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current roll rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT2_NEUTRAL_X.label: Mount roll angle when in neutral position
MNT2_NEUTRAL_X.description: Mount roll angle when in neutral position

MNT2_NEUTRAL_Y.label: Mount pitch angle when in neutral position
MNT2_NEUTRAL_Y.description: Mount pitch angle when in neutral position

MNT2_NEUTRAL_Z.label: Mount yaw angle when in neutral position
MNT2_NEUTRAL_Z.description: Mount yaw angle when in neutral position

MNT2_OPTIONS.label: Mount options
MNT2_OPTIONS.description: Mount options bitmask, note bit 2 only impacts RC targetting mode

options.label: RC lock state from previous mode
Return to neutral angles on RC failsafe
Force FPV (bf) lock on roll and pitch

MNT2_RC_RATE.label: Mount RC Rate
MNT2_RC_RATE.description: Pilot rate control's maximum rate.  Set to zero to use angle control

MNT2_RETRACT_X.label: Mount roll angle when in retracted position
MNT2_RETRACT_X.description: Mount roll angle when in retracted position

MNT2_RETRACT_Y.label: Mount pitch angle when in retracted position
MNT2_RETRACT_Y.description: Mount pitch angle when in retracted position

MNT2_RETRACT_Z.label: Mount yaw angle when in retracted position
MNT2_RETRACT_Z.description: Mount yaw angle when in retracted position

MNT2_SYSID_DFLT.label: Mount Target sysID
MNT2_SYSID_DFLT.description: Default Target sysID for the mount to point to

MODE.label: Sensor operating mode
MODE.description: 0: The sensor will save the fuel consumption across power resets. 1: The sensor will reset the power consumption.

MOT_BAT_CURR_MAX.label: Motor Current Max
MOT_BAT_CURR_MAX.description: Maximum current over which maximum throttle is limited (0 = Disabled)

MOT_BAT_CURR_TC.label: Motor Current Max Time Constant
MOT_BAT_CURR_TC.description: Time constant used to limit the maximum current

MOT_BAT_IDX.label: Battery compensation index
MOT_BAT_IDX.description: Which battery monitor should be used for doing compensation

MOT_BAT_VOLT_MAX.label: Battery voltage compensation maximum voltage
MOT_BAT_VOLT_MAX.description: Battery voltage compensation maximum voltage (voltage above this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 4.2 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

MOT_BAT_VOLT_MIN.label: Battery voltage compensation minimum voltage
MOT_BAT_VOLT_MIN.description: Battery voltage compensation minimum voltage (voltage below this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 3.3 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

MOT_BOOST_SCALE.label: Motor boost scale
MOT_BOOST_SCALE.description: Booster motor output scaling factor vs main throttle.  The output to the BoostThrottle servo will be the main throttle times this scaling factor. A higher scaling factor will put more of the load on the booster motor. A value of 1 will set the BoostThrottle equal to the main throttle.

MOT_HOVER_LEARN.label: Hover Value Learning
MOT_HOVER_LEARN.description: Enable/Disable automatic learning of hover throttle

MOT_IDLE_SEC.label: Idle time
MOT_IDLE_SEC.description: Delay after reaching ground idle when armed to allow ESC startup to complete.

MOT_PWM_MAX.label: PWM output maximum
MOT_PWM_MAX.description: This sets the max PWM value in microseconds that will ever be output to the motors

MOT_PWM_MIN.label: PWM output minimum
MOT_PWM_MIN.description: This sets the min PWM output value in microseconds that will ever be output to the motors

MOT_PWM_TYPE.label: Output PWM type
MOT_PWM_TYPE.description: This selects the output PWM type, allowing for normal PWM continuous output, OneShot, brushed or DShot motor output.PWMRange and PWMAngle are PWM special/rare cases for ESCs that dont calibrate normally (some Sub motors) or where each ESC must have its PWM range set individually using the Servo params instead of PWM_MIN/MAX parameters.

MOT_SAFE_DISARM.label: Motor PWM output disabled when disarmed
MOT_SAFE_DISARM.description: Disables motor PWM output when disarmed

MOT_SAFE_TIME.label: PWM re-enable delay after arming when disarmed pwm was disabled
MOT_SAFE_TIME.description: Delay after arming before spool transitions are allowed, used to let ESCs/servos complete startup when PWM was disabled while disarmed.

MOT_SLEW_DN_TIME.label: Output slew time for decreasing throttle
MOT_SLEW_DN_TIME.description: Time in seconds to slew output from full to zero. This is used to limit the rate at which output can change.  Range is constrained between 0 and 0.5.

MOT_SLEW_UP_TIME.label: Output slew time for increasing throttle
MOT_SLEW_UP_TIME.description: Time in seconds to slew output from zero to full. This is used to limit the rate at which output can change. Range is constrained between 0 and 0.5.

MOT_SPIN_ARM.label: Motor Spin armed
MOT_SPIN_ARM.description: Point at which the motors start to spin expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range.  Should be lower than MOT_SPIN_MIN.

MOT_SPIN_MAX.label: Motor Spin maximum
MOT_SPIN_MAX.description: Point at which the thrust saturates expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range

MOT_SPIN_MIN.label: Motor Spin minimum
MOT_SPIN_MIN.description: Point at which the thrust starts expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range.  Should be higher than MOT_SPIN_ARM.

MOT_SPOOL_TIME.label: Spool up time
MOT_SPOOL_TIME.description: Time in seconds to spool up the motors from zero to min throttle.

MOT_SPOOL_TIM_DN.label: Spool down time
MOT_SPOOL_TIM_DN.description: Time to ramp the throttle ceiling down toward zero. If 0, SPOOL_TIME is used.

MOT_THST_EXPO.label: Thrust Curve Expo
MOT_THST_EXPO.description: Motor thrust curve exponent (0.0 for linear to 1.0 for second order curve)

MOT_THST_HOVER.label: Thrust Hover Value
MOT_THST_HOVER.description: Motor thrust needed to hover expressed as a number from 0 to 1

MOT_YAW_HEADROOM.label: Matrix Yaw Min
MOT_YAW_HEADROOM.description: Yaw control is given at least this pwm in microseconds range

MOT_YAW_SV_ANGLE.label: Yaw Servo Max Lean Angle
MOT_YAW_SV_ANGLE.description: Yaw servo's maximum lean angle (Tricopter only)

MSP_OPTIONS.label: MSP OSD Options
MSP_OPTIONS.description: A bitmask to set some MSP specific options: EnableTelemetryMode-allows "push" mode telemetry when only rx line of OSD ic connected to autopilot,  EnableBTFLFonts-uses indexes corresponding to Betaflight fonts if OSD uses those instead of ArduPilot fonts. EnableINAVFonts uses INAV fonts and overrides EnableBTFLFonts if that option is enabled.

MSP_OSD_NCELLS.label: Cell count override
MSP_OSD_NCELLS.description: Used for average cell voltage calculation

NET_NETMASK.label: IP Subnet mask
NET_NETMASK.description: Allows setting static subnet mask. The value is a count of consecutive bits. Examples: 24 = 255.255.255.0, 16 = 255.255.0.0

NET_P1_TYPE.label: Port type
NET_P1_TYPE.description: Port type for network serial port. For the two client types a valid destination IP address must be set. For the two server types either 0.0.0.0 or a local address can be used. The UDP client type will use broadcast if the IP is set to 255.255.255.255 and will use UDP multicast if the IP is in the multicast address range.

NET_P2_TYPE.label: Port type
NET_P2_TYPE.description: Port type for network serial port. For the two client types a valid destination IP address must be set. For the two server types either 0.0.0.0 or a local address can be used. The UDP client type will use broadcast if the IP is set to 255.255.255.255 and will use UDP multicast if the IP is in the multicast address range.

NET_P3_TYPE.label: Port type
NET_P3_TYPE.description: Port type for network serial port. For the two client types a valid destination IP address must be set. For the two server types either 0.0.0.0 or a local address can be used. The UDP client type will use broadcast if the IP is set to 255.255.255.255 and will use UDP multicast if the IP is in the multicast address range.

NET_P4_TYPE.label: Port type
NET_P4_TYPE.description: Port type for network serial port. For the two client types a valid destination IP address must be set. For the two server types either 0.0.0.0 or a local address can be used. The UDP client type will use broadcast if the IP is set to 255.255.255.255 and will use UDP multicast if the IP is in the multicast address range.

NMEA_MSG_EN.label: Messages Enable bitmask
NMEA_MSG_EN.description: This is a bitmask of enabled NMEA messages. All messages will be sent consecutively at the same rate interval

NMEA_RATE_MS.label: NMEA Output rate
NMEA_RATE_MS.description: NMEA Output rate. This controls the interval at which all the enabled NMEA messages are sent. Most NMEA systems expect 100ms (10Hz) or slower.

NTF_BUZZ_ON_LVL.label: Buzzer-on pin logic level
NTF_BUZZ_ON_LVL.description: Specifies pin level that indicates buzzer should play

NTF_BUZZ_PIN.label: Buzzer pin
NTF_BUZZ_PIN.description: Enables to connect active buzzer to arbitrary pin. Requires 3-pin buzzer or additional MOSFET! Some the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

NTF_BUZZ_TYPES.label: Buzzer Driver Types
NTF_BUZZ_TYPES.description: Controls what types of Buzzer will be enabled

NTF_BUZZ_VOLUME.label: Buzzer volume
NTF_BUZZ_VOLUME.description: Control the volume of the buzzer

NTF_DISPLAY_TYPE.label: Type of on-board I2C display
NTF_DISPLAY_TYPE.description: This sets up the type of on-board I2C display. Disabled by default.

NTF_LED_BRIGHT.label: LED Brightness
NTF_LED_BRIGHT.description: Select the RGB LED brightness level. When USB is connected brightness will never be higher than low regardless of the setting.

NTF_LED_LEN.label: Serial LED String Length
NTF_LED_LEN.description: The number of Serial LED's to use for notifications (NeoPixel's and ProfiLED)

NTF_LED_OVERRIDE.label: Specifies colour source for the RGBLed
NTF_LED_OVERRIDE.description: Specifies the source for the colours and brightness for the LED.  OutbackChallenge conforms to the MedicalExpress (https://uavchallenge.org/medical-express/) rules, essentially "Green" is disarmed (safe-to-approach), "Red" is armed (not safe-to-approach). Traffic light is a simplified color set, red when armed, yellow when the safety switch is not surpressing outputs (but disarmed), and green when outputs are surpressed and disarmed, the LED will blink faster if disarmed and failing arming checks.

NTF_LED_TYPES.label: LED Driver Types
NTF_LED_TYPES.description: Controls what types of LEDs will be enabled

NTF_OREO_THEME.label: OreoLED Theme
NTF_OREO_THEME.description: Enable/Disable Solo Oreo LED driver, 0 to disable, 1 for Aircraft theme, 2 for Rover theme

OA_BR_CONT_ANGLE.label: BendyRuler's bearing change resistance threshold angle
OA_BR_CONT_ANGLE.description: BendyRuler will resist changing current bearing if the change in bearing is over this angle

OA_BR_CONT_RATIO.label: Obstacle Avoidance margin ratio for BendyRuler to change bearing significantly
OA_BR_CONT_RATIO.description: BendyRuler will avoid changing bearing unless ratio of previous margin from obstacle (or fence) to present calculated margin is atleast this much.

OA_BR_LOOKAHEAD.label: Object Avoidance look ahead distance maximum
OA_BR_LOOKAHEAD.description: Object Avoidance will look this many meters ahead of vehicle

OA_BR_TYPE.label: Type of BendyRuler
OA_BR_TYPE.description: BendyRuler will search for clear path along the direction defined by this parameter

OA_DB_ALT_MIN.label: OADatabase minimum altitude above home before storing obstacles
OA_DB_ALT_MIN.description: OADatabase will reject obstacles if vehicle's altitude above home is below this parameter, in a 3 meter radius around home. Set 0 to disable this feature.

OA_DB_BEAM_WIDTH.label: OADatabase beam width
OA_DB_BEAM_WIDTH.description: Beam width of incoming lidar data, used to calculate a object radius if none is provided by the data source.

OA_DB_DIST_MAX.label: OADatabase Distance Maximum
OA_DB_DIST_MAX.description: Maximum distance of objects held in database.  Set to zero to disable the limits

OA_DB_EXPIRE.label: OADatabase item timeout
OA_DB_EXPIRE.description: OADatabase item timeout. The time an item will linger without any updates before it expires. Zero means never expires which is useful for a sent-once static environment but terrible for dynamic ones.

OA_DB_OUTPUT.label: OADatabase output level
OA_DB_OUTPUT.description: OADatabase output level to configure which database objects are sent to the ground station. All data is always available internally for avoidance algorithms.

options.label: Send HIGH and NORMAL importance items

OA_DB_QUEUE_SIZE.label: OADatabase queue maximum number of points
OA_DB_QUEUE_SIZE.description: OADatabase queue maximum number of points. This in an input buffer size. Larger means it can handle larger bursts of incoming data points to filter into the database. No impact on cpu, only RAM. Recommend larger for faster datalinks or for sensors that generate a lot of data.

OA_DB_RADIUS_MIN.label: OADatabase Minimum  radius
OA_DB_RADIUS_MIN.description: Minimum radius of objects held in database

OA_DB_SIZE.label: OADatabase maximum number of points
OA_DB_SIZE.description: OADatabase maximum number of points. Set to 0 to disable the OA Database. Larger means more points but is more cpu intensive to process

OA_MARGIN_MAX.label: Object Avoidance wide margin distance
OA_MARGIN_MAX.description: Object Avoidance will ignore objects more than this many meters from vehicle

OA_OPTIONS.label: Options while recovering from Object Avoidance
OA_OPTIONS.description: Bitmask which will govern vehicles behaviour while recovering from Obstacle Avoidance (i.e Avoidance is turned off after the path ahead is clear).

OA_TYPE.label: Object Avoidance Path Planning algorithm to use

OSD4_FONT.label: Sets the font index for this screen (MSP DisplayPort only)
OSD4_FONT.description: Sets the font index for this screen (MSP DisplayPort only)

OSD4_HOMEDIR_EN.label: HOMEDIR_EN
OSD4_HOMEDIR_EN.description: Displays relative direction to HOME (MSP OSD only)

OSD4_HOMEDIST_EN.label: HOMEDIST_EN
OSD4_HOMEDIST_EN.description: Displays distance from HOME (MSP OSD only)

OSD4_HOMEDIST_X.label: HOMEDIST_X
OSD4_HOMEDIST_X.description: Horizontal position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD4_HOMEDIST_Y.label: HOMEDIST_Y
OSD4_HOMEDIST_Y.description: Vertical position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD4_HOME_EN.label: HOME_EN
OSD4_HOME_EN.description: Displays distance and relative direction to HOME

OSD4_PITCH_EN.label: PITCH_EN
OSD4_PITCH_EN.description: Displays degrees of pitch from level

OSD4_RC_ANT_EN.label: RC_ANT_EN
OSD4_RC_ANT_EN.description: Displays the current RC link active antenna

OSD4_RC_LQ_EN.label: RC_LQ_EN
OSD4_RC_LQ_EN.description: Displays the RC link quality (uplink, 0 to 100%) and also RF mode if bit 7 of OSD_OPTIONS is set

OSD4_RC_PWR_EN.label: RC_PWR_EN
OSD4_RC_PWR_EN.description: Displays the RC link transmit (TX) power in mW or W, depending on level

OSD4_RC_SNR_EN.label: RC_SNR_EN
OSD4_RC_SNR_EN.description: Displays RC link signal to noise ratio in dB

OSD4_RNGF_EN.label: RNGF_EN
OSD4_RNGF_EN.description: Displays a rangefinder's distance in cm

OSD4_ROLL_EN.label: ROLL_EN
OSD4_ROLL_EN.description: Displays degrees of roll from level

OSD4_RSSIDBM_EN.label: RSSIDBM_EN
OSD4_RSSIDBM_EN.description: Displays RC link signal strength in dBm

OSD4_TEMP_EN.label: TEMP_EN
OSD4_TEMP_EN.description: Displays temperature reported by primary barometer

OSD4_THROTTLE_EN.label: THROTTLE_EN
OSD4_THROTTLE_EN.description: Displays actual throttle percentage being sent to motor(s)

OSD4_TXT_RES.label: Sets the overlay text resolution (MSP DisplayPort only)
OSD4_TXT_RES.description: Sets the overlay text resolution for this screen to either SD 30x16 or HD 50x18/60x22 (MSP DisplayPort only)

OSD4_WAYPOINT_EN.label: WAYPOINT_EN
OSD4_WAYPOINT_EN.description: Displays bearing and distance to next waypoint

OSD4_WIND_EN.label: WIND_EN
OSD4_WIND_EN.description: Displays wind speed and relative direction, on Rover this is the apparent wind speed and direction from the windvane, if fitted

OSD5_CHAN_MIN.description: This sets the PWM lower limit for this screen

OSD6_SAVE_X.label: SAVE_X

OSD_ARM_SCR.label: Arm screen
OSD_ARM_SCR.description: Screen to be shown on Arm event. Zero to disable the feature.

OSD_BTN_DELAY.label: Button delay
OSD_BTN_DELAY.description: Debounce time in ms for stick commanded parameter navigation.

OSD_CELL_COUNT.label: Battery cell count
OSD_CELL_COUNT.description: Used for average cell voltage display. -1 disables, 0 uses cell count autodetection for well charged LIPO/LIION batteries at connection, other values manually select cell count used.

OSD_CHAN.label: Screen switch transmitter channel
OSD_CHAN.description: This sets the channel used to switch different OSD screens.

OSD_DSARM_SCR.label: Disarm screen
OSD_DSARM_SCR.description: Screen to be shown on disarm event. Zero to disable the feature.

OSD_FONT.label: OSD Font
OSD_FONT.description: This sets which OSD font to use. It is an integer from 0 to the number of fonts available

OSD_FS_SCR.label: Failsafe screen
OSD_FS_SCR.description: Screen to be shown on failsafe event. Zero to disable the feature.

OSD_H_OFFSET.label: OSD horizontal offset
OSD_H_OFFSET.description: Sets horizontal offset of the osd inside image

OSD_OPTIONS.label: OSD Options
OSD_OPTIONS.description: This sets options that change the display

options.label: Convert feet to miles at 5280ft instead of 10000ft

OSD_SB_H_OFS.label: Sidebar horizontal offset
OSD_SB_H_OFS.description: Extends the spacing between the sidebar elements by this amount of columns. Positive values increases the width to the right of the screen.

OSD_SB_V_EXT.label: Sidebar vertical extension
OSD_SB_V_EXT.description: Increase of vertical length of the sidebar itens by this amount of lines. Applied equally both above and below the default setting.

OSD_SW_METHOD.label: Screen switch method
OSD_SW_METHOD.description: This sets the method used to switch different OSD screens.

options.label: switch to next screen if channel value was changed
select screen based on pwm ranges specified for each screen
switch to next screen after low to high transition and every 1s while channel value is high

OSD_TYPE.label: OSD type
OSD_TYPE.description: OSD type. TXONLY makes the OSD parameter selection available to other modules even if there is no native OSD support on the board, for instance CRSF.

OSD_TYPE2.label: OSD type 2
OSD_TYPE2.description: OSD type 2. TXONLY makes the OSD parameter selection available to other modules even if there is no native OSD support on the board, for instance CRSF.

OSD_UNITS.label: Display Units
OSD_UNITS.description: Sets the units to use in displaying items

OSD_V_OFFSET.label: OSD vertical offset
OSD_V_OFFSET.description: Sets vertical offset of the osd inside image

OSD_W_ACRVOLT.label: Avg Cell Resting Volt warn level
OSD_W_ACRVOLT.description: Set level at which ACRVOLT item will flash

OSD_W_AVGCELLV.label: AVGCELLV warn level
OSD_W_AVGCELLV.description: Set level at which AVGCELLV item will flash

OSD_W_BATVOLT.label: BAT_VOLT warn level
OSD_W_BATVOLT.description: Set level at which BAT_VOLT item will flash

OSD_W_LQ.label: RC link quality warn level (in %)
OSD_W_LQ.description: Set level at which RC_LQ item will flash (%)

OSD_W_NSAT.label: NSAT warn level
OSD_W_NSAT.description: Set level at which NSAT item will flash

OSD_W_RESTVOLT.label: RESTVOLT warn level
OSD_W_RESTVOLT.description: Set level at which RESTVOLT item will flash

OSD_W_RSSI.label: RSSI warn level (in %)
OSD_W_RSSI.description: Set level at which RSSI item will flash (in positive % or negative dBm values as applicable). 30% or -100dBm are defaults.

OSD_W_SNR.label: RC link SNR warn level (in %)
OSD_W_SNR.description: Set level at which RC_SNR item will flash (in db)

OSD_W_TERR.label: Terrain warn level
OSD_W_TERR.description: Set level below which TER_HGT item will flash. -1 disables.

PHLD_BRK_ANGLE.label: PosHold braking angle max
PHLD_BRK_ANGLE.description: PosHold flight mode's max lean angle during braking in degrees

PHLD_BRK_RATE.label: PosHold braking rate
PHLD_BRK_RATE.description: PosHold flight mode's rotation rate during braking in deg/sec

PILOT_ACC_Z.label: Pilot vertical acceleration
PILOT_ACC_Z.description: The vertical acceleration used when pilot is controlling the altitude

PILOT_SPD_DN.label: Pilot maximum vertical speed descending
PILOT_SPD_DN.description: The maximum vertical descending velocity the pilot may request in m/s.  If 0 PILOT_SPD_UP value is used.

PILOT_SPD_UP.label: Pilot maximum vertical speed ascending
PILOT_SPD_UP.description: The maximum vertical ascending velocity the pilot may request in m/s

PILOT_THR_BHV.label: Throttle stick behavior
PILOT_THR_BHV.description: Bitmask containing various throttle stick options. TX with sprung throttle can set PILOT_THR_BHV to "1" so motor feedback when landed starts from mid-stick instead of bottom of stick.

PILOT_THR_FILT.label: Throttle filter cutoff
PILOT_THR_FILT.description: Throttle filter cutoff (Hz) - active whenever altitude control is inactive - 0 to disable

PILOT_TKO_ALT_M.label: Pilot takeoff altitude
PILOT_TKO_ALT_M.description: Altitude that altitude control modes will climb to when a takeoff is triggered with the throttle stick.

PILOT_Y_EXPO.label: Pilot controlled yaw expo
PILOT_Y_EXPO.description: Pilot controlled yaw expo to allow faster rotation when stick at edges

PILOT_Y_RATE.label: Pilot controlled yaw rate
PILOT_Y_RATE.description: Pilot controlled yaw rate max.  Used in all pilot controlled modes except Acro

PILOT_Y_RATE_TC.label: Pilot yaw rate control input time constant
PILOT_Y_RATE_TC.description: Pilot yaw rate control input time constant.  Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response

PLDP_DELAY.label: Payload Place climb delay
PLDP_DELAY.description: Delay after release, in seconds, before aircraft starts to climb back to starting altitude.

PLDP_RNG_MAX.label: Payload Place maximum range finder altitude
PLDP_RNG_MAX.description: Maximum range finder altitude in m to trigger payload touchdown, set to zero to disable.

PLDP_SPEED_DN.label: Payload Place decent speed
PLDP_SPEED_DN.description: The maximum vertical decent velocity in m/s. If 0 LAND_SPD_MS value is used.

PLDP_THRESH.label: Payload Place thrust ratio threshold
PLDP_THRESH.description: Ratio of vertical thrust during decent below which payload touchdown will trigger.

PLND_ACC_P_NSE.label: Kalman Filter Accelerometer Noise
PLND_ACC_P_NSE.description: Kalman Filter Accelerometer Noise, higher values weight the input from the camera more, accels less

PLND_ALT_CUTOFF.label: Precland altitude cutoff
PLND_ALT_CUTOFF.description: The altitude (rangefinder distance) below which we stop using the precision landing sensor and continue landing

PLND_ALT_MAX.label: PrecLand maximum alt for retry
PLND_ALT_MAX.description: Vehicle will continue landing vertically until this height if target is not found. Below this height if landing target is not found, landing retry/failsafe might be attempted. This needs a rangefinder to work. Set to zero to disable this.

PLND_ALT_MIN.label: PrecLand minimum alt for retry
PLND_ALT_MIN.description: Vehicle will continue landing vertically even if target is lost below this height. This needs a rangefinder to work. Set to zero to disable this.

PLND_BUS.label: Sensor Bus
PLND_BUS.description: Precland sensor bus for I2C sensors.

PLND_CAM_POS_X.label: Camera X position offset
PLND_CAM_POS_X.description: X position of the camera in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin.

PLND_CAM_POS_Y.label: Camera Y position offset
PLND_CAM_POS_Y.description: Y position of the camera in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin.

PLND_CAM_POS_Z.label: Camera Z position offset
PLND_CAM_POS_Z.description: Z position of the camera in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin.

PLND_EST_TYPE.label: Precision Land Estimator Type
PLND_EST_TYPE.description: Specifies the estimation method to be used

PLND_LAG.label: Precision Landing sensor lag
PLND_LAG.description: Precision Landing sensor lag, to cope with variable landing_target latency

PLND_LAND_OFS_X.label: Land offset forward
PLND_LAND_OFS_X.description: Desired landing position of the camera forward of the target in vehicle body frame

PLND_LAND_OFS_Y.label: Land offset right
PLND_LAND_OFS_Y.description: desired landing position of the camera right of the target in vehicle body frame

options.label: Allow Precision Landing after manual reposition
Maintain high speed in final descent

PLND_RET_BEHAVE.label: PrecLand retry behaviour
PLND_RET_BEHAVE.description: Prec Land will do the action selected by this parameter if a retry to a landing is needed

options.label: Go to the last location where landing target was detected
Go towards the approximate location of the detected landing target

PLND_RET_MAX.label: PrecLand Maximum number of retires for a failed landing
PLND_RET_MAX.description: PrecLand Maximum number of retires for a failed landing. Set to zero to disable landing retry.

PLND_STRICT.label: PrecLand strictness
PLND_STRICT.description: How strictly should the vehicle land on the target if target is lost

PLND_TIMEOUT.label: PrecLand retry timeout
PLND_TIMEOUT.description: Time for which vehicle continues descend even if target is lost. After this time period, vehicle will attempt a landing retry depending on PLND_STRICT parameter.

PLND_XY_DIST_MAX.label: Precision Landing maximum distance to target before descending
PLND_XY_DIST_MAX.description: The vehicle will not start descending if the landing target is detected and it is further than this many meters away. Set 0 to always descend.

PLND_YAW_ALIGN.label: Sensor yaw alignment
PLND_YAW_ALIGN.description: Yaw angle from body x-axis to sensor x-axis.

POI_DIST_MAX.label: Mount POI distance max
POI_DIST_MAX.description: POI's max distance (in meters) from the vehicle

PORT.label: Scripting serial port number
PORT.description: Which Scripting serial port the sensor is connected at.

PREV_RC_FUNC.label: param reversion RC function
PREV_RC_FUNC.description: RCn_OPTION number to used to trigger parameter reversion

PRX1_ADDR.label: Bus address of sensor
PRX1_ADDR.description: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX1_MAX.label: Proximity maximum range
PRX1_MAX.description: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX1_MIN.label: Proximity minimum range
PRX1_MIN.description: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX1_RECV_ID.label: CAN receive ID
PRX1_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PRX1_TYPE.label: Proximity type
PRX1_TYPE.description: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PRX2_ADDR.label: Bus address of sensor
PRX2_ADDR.description: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX2_MAX.label: Proximity maximum range
PRX2_MAX.description: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX2_MIN.label: Proximity minimum range
PRX2_MIN.description: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX2_RECV_ID.label: CAN receive ID
PRX2_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PRX2_TYPE.label: Proximity type
PRX2_TYPE.description: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PRX3_ADDR.label: Bus address of sensor
PRX3_ADDR.description: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX3_MAX.label: Proximity maximum range
PRX3_MAX.description: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX3_MIN.label: Proximity minimum range
PRX3_MIN.description: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX3_RECV_ID.label: CAN receive ID
PRX3_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PRX3_TYPE.label: Proximity type
PRX3_TYPE.description: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PRX4_ADDR.label: Bus address of sensor
PRX4_ADDR.description: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX4_MAX.label: Proximity maximum range
PRX4_MAX.description: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX4_MIN.label: Proximity minimum range
PRX4_MIN.description: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX4_RECV_ID.label: CAN receive ID
PRX4_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PRX4_TYPE.label: Proximity type
PRX4_TYPE.description: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PRX5_ADDR.label: Bus address of sensor
PRX5_ADDR.description: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX5_MAX.label: Proximity maximum range
PRX5_MAX.description: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX5_MIN.label: Proximity minimum range
PRX5_MIN.description: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX5_RECV_ID.label: CAN receive ID
PRX5_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PRX5_TYPE.label: Proximity type
PRX5_TYPE.description: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PRX_ALT_MIN.label: Proximity lowest altitude.
PRX_ALT_MIN.description: Minimum altitude below which proximity should not work.

PRX_FILT.label: Proximity filter cutoff frequency
PRX_FILT.description: Cutoff frequency for low pass filter applied to each face in the proximity boundary

PRX_IGN_GND.label: Proximity sensor land detection
PRX_IGN_GND.description: Ignore proximity data that is within 1 meter of the ground below the vehicle. This requires a downward facing rangefinder

PRX_LOG_RAW.label: Proximity raw distances log
PRX_LOG_RAW.description: Set this parameter to one if logging unfiltered(raw) distances from sensor should be enabled

PSC_ANGLE_MAX.label: Position Control Angle Max
PSC_ANGLE_MAX.description: Maximum lean angle autopilot can request. Set to zero to use ANGLE_MAX parameter value

PSC_D_ACC_D.label: Acceleration (vertical) controller D gain
PSC_D_ACC_D.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller D gain. Compensates for short-term change in desired vertical acceleration vs actual acceleration. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_D * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_D_FF.label: Accel (vertical) Derivative FeedForward Gain
PSC_D_ACC_D_FF.description: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_P * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_FF.label: Acceleration (vertical) controller feed forward
PSC_D_ACC_FF.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller feed forward. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_FF * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_FLTD.label: Acceleration (vertical) controller derivative frequency in Hz
PSC_D_ACC_FLTD.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller derivative frequency in Hz. Previously _ACCZ_FLTD.

PSC_D_ACC_FLTE.label: Acceleration (vertical) controller error frequency in Hz
PSC_D_ACC_FLTE.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller error frequency in Hz. Previously _ACCZ_FLTE.

PSC_D_ACC_FLTT.label: Acceleration (vertical) controller target frequency in Hz
PSC_D_ACC_FLTT.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller target frequency in Hz. Previously _ACCZ_FLTT.

PSC_D_ACC_I.label: Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain
PSC_D_ACC_I.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain. Corrects long-term difference in desired vertical acceleration and actual acceleration. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_I * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_IMAX.label: Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain maximum
PSC_D_ACC_IMAX.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain maximum. Constrains the maximum pwm that the I term will generate. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_IMAX * 0.001.

PSC_D_ACC_NEF.label: Accel (vertical) Error notch filter index
PSC_D_ACC_NEF.description: Accel (vertical) Error notch filter index. If upgrading from 4.6 this is Previously _ACCZ_NEF.

PSC_D_ACC_NTF.label: Accel (vertical) Target notch filter index
PSC_D_ACC_NTF.description: Accel (vertical) Target notch filter index. If upgrading from 4.6 this is Previously _ACCZ_NTF.

PSC_D_ACC_P.label: Acceleration (vertical) controller P gain
PSC_D_ACC_P.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller P gain. Converts the difference between desired vertical acceleration and actual acceleration into a motor output. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_P * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_PDMX.label: Acceleration (vertical) controller PD sum maximum
PSC_D_ACC_PDMX.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller PD sum maximum. The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _ACCZ_P * 0.1.

PSC_D_ACC_SMAX.label: Accel (vertical) slew rate limit
PSC_D_ACC_SMAX.description: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

PSC_D_POS_P.label: Position (vertical) controller P gain
PSC_D_POS_P.description: Position (vertical) controller P gain. Converts the difference between the desired altitude and actual altitude into a climb or descent rate which is passed to the throttle rate controller. Previously _POSZ_P.

PSC_D_VEL_D.label: Velocity (vertical) controller D gain
PSC_D_VEL_D.description: Velocity (vertical) controller D gain. Corrects short-term changes in velocity. Previously _VELZ_D.

PSC_D_VEL_FF.label: Velocity (vertical) controller Feed Forward gain
PSC_D_VEL_FF.description: Velocity (vertical) controller Feed Forward gain. Produces an output that is proportional to the magnitude of the target. Previously _VELZ_FF.

PSC_D_VEL_FLTD.label: Velocity (vertical) input filter for D term
PSC_D_VEL_FLTD.description: Velocity (vertical) input filter for D term. This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for D terms. Previously _VELZ_FLTD.

PSC_D_VEL_FLTE.label: Velocity (vertical) error filter
PSC_D_VEL_FLTE.description: Velocity (vertical) error filter. This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for P and I terms. Previously _VELZ_FLTE.

PSC_D_VEL_I.label: Velocity (vertical) controller I gain
PSC_D_VEL_I.description: Velocity (vertical) controller I gain. Corrects long-term difference in desired velocity to a target acceleration. Previously _VELZ_I.

PSC_D_VEL_IMAX.label: Velocity (vertical) controller I gain maximum
PSC_D_VEL_IMAX.description: Velocity (vertical) controller I gain maximum. Constrains the target acceleration that the I gain will output. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _VELZ_IMAX * 0.01.

PSC_D_VEL_P.label: Velocity (vertical) controller P gain
PSC_D_VEL_P.description: Velocity (vertical) controller P gain. Converts the difference between desired vertical speed and actual speed into a desired acceleration that is passed to the throttle acceleration controller. Previously _VELZ_P.

PSC_JERK_D.label: Jerk limit for the vertical kinematic input shaping
PSC_JERK_D.description: Jerk limit of the vertical kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the aircraft varies the acceleration target

PSC_JERK_NE.label: Jerk limit for the horizontal kinematic input shaping
PSC_JERK_NE.description: Jerk limit of the horizontal kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the aircraft varies the acceleration target

PSC_NE_POS_P.label: Position (horizontal) controller P gain
PSC_NE_POS_P.description: Position controller P gain. Converts the distance (in the latitude direction) to the target location into a desired speed which is then passed to the loiter latitude rate controller. Previously _POSXY_P.

PSC_NE_VEL_D.label: Velocity (horizontal) D gain
PSC_NE_VEL_D.description: Velocity (horizontal) D gain. Corrects short-term changes in velocity. Previously _VELXY_D.

PSC_NE_VEL_FF.label: Velocity (horizontal) feed forward gain
PSC_NE_VEL_FF.description: Velocity (horizontal) feed forward gain. Converts the difference between desired velocity to a target acceleration. Previously _VELXY_FF.

PSC_NE_VEL_FLTD.label: Velocity (horizontal) input filter
PSC_NE_VEL_FLTD.description: Velocity (horizontal) input filter. This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for D term. Previously _VELXY_FLTD.

PSC_NE_VEL_FLTE.label: Velocity (horizontal) input filter
PSC_NE_VEL_FLTE.description: Velocity (horizontal) input filter. This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for P and I terms. Previously _VELXY_FLTE.

PSC_NE_VEL_I.label: Velocity (horizontal) I gain
PSC_NE_VEL_I.description: Velocity (horizontal) I gain. Corrects long-term difference between desired and actual velocity to a target acceleration. Previously _VELXY_I.

PSC_NE_VEL_IMAX.label: Velocity (horizontal) integrator maximum
PSC_NE_VEL_IMAX.description: Velocity (horizontal) integrator maximum. Constrains the target acceleration that the I gain will output. If upgrading from 4.6 this is _VELXY_IMAX * 0.01.

PSC_NE_VEL_P.label: Velocity (horizontal) P gain
PSC_NE_VEL_P.description: Velocity (horizontal) P gain. Converts the difference between desired and actual velocity to a target acceleration. Previously _VELXY_P.

QUIK_ANGLE_MAX.label: maximum angle error for tune abort
QUIK_ANGLE_MAX.description: If while tuning the angle error goes over this limit then the tune will aborts to prevent a bad oscillation in the case of the tuning algorithm failing. If you get an error "Tuning: attitude error ABORTING" and you think it is a false positive then you can either raise this parameter or you can try increasing the QUIK_DOUBLE_TIME to do the tune more slowly. A value of zero disables this check.

QUIK_AUTO_FILTER.label: Quicktune auto filter enable
QUIK_AUTO_FILTER.description: When enabled the PID filter settings are automatically set based on INS_GYRO_FILTER

QUIK_AUTO_SAVE.label: Quicktune auto save
QUIK_AUTO_SAVE.description: Number of seconds after completion of tune to auto-save. This is useful when using a 2 position switch for quicktune

QUIK_DOUBLE_TIME.label: Quicktune doubling time
QUIK_DOUBLE_TIME.description: Time to double a tuning parameter. Raise this for a slower tune.

QUIK_GAIN_MARGIN.label: Quicktune gain margin
QUIK_GAIN_MARGIN.description: Reduction in gain after oscillation detected. Raise this number to get a more conservative tune

QUIK_MAX_REDUCE.label: Quicktune maximum gain reduction
QUIK_MAX_REDUCE.description: This controls how much quicktune is allowed to lower gains from the original gains. If the vehicle already has a reasonable tune and is not oscillating then you can set this to zero to prevent gain reductions. The default of 20% is reasonable for most vehicles. Using a maximum gain reduction lowers the chance of an angle P oscillation happening if quicktune gets a false positive oscillation at a low gain, which can result in very low rate gains and a dangerous angle P oscillation.

QUIK_OPTIONS.label: Quicktune options
QUIK_OPTIONS.description: Additional options. When the Two Position Switch option is enabled then a high switch position will start the tune, low will disable the tune. you should also set a QUIK_AUTO_SAVE time so that you will be able to save the tune.

QUIK_OSC_SMAX.label: Quicktune oscillation rate threshold
QUIK_OSC_SMAX.description: Threshold for oscillation detection. A lower value will lead to a more conservative tune.

QUIK_RC_FUNC.label: Quicktune RC function
QUIK_RC_FUNC.description: RCn_OPTION number to use to control tuning stop/start/save

QUIK_RP_PI_RATIO.label: Quicktune roll/pitch PI ratio
QUIK_RP_PI_RATIO.description: Ratio between P and I gains for roll and pitch. Raise this to get a lower I gain

QUIK_YAW_D_MAX.label: Quicktune Yaw D max
QUIK_YAW_D_MAX.description: Maximum value for yaw D gain

QUIK_YAW_P_MAX.label: Quicktune Yaw P max
QUIK_YAW_P_MAX.description: Maximum value for yaw P gain

QUIK_Y_PI_RATIO.label: Quicktune Yaw PI ratio
QUIK_Y_PI_RATIO.description: Ratio between P and I gains for yaw. Raise this to get a lower I gain

RALLY_INCL_HOME.label: Rally Include Home
RALLY_INCL_HOME.description: Controls if Home is included as a Rally point (i.e. as a safe landing place) for RTL

RALLY_LIMIT_KM.label: Rally Limit
RALLY_LIMIT_KM.description: Maximum distance to rally point. If the closest rally point is more than this number of kilometers from the current position and the home location is closer than any of the rally points from the current position then do RTL to home rather than to the closest rally point. This prevents a leftover rally point from a different airfield being used accidentally. If this is set to 0 then the closest rally point is always used.

RALLY_TOTAL.label: Rally Total
RALLY_TOTAL.description: Number of rally points currently loaded

RC10_DZ.label: RC dead-zone
RC10_DZ.description: PWM dead zone in microseconds around trim or bottom

RC10_MAX.label: RC max PWM
RC10_MAX.description: RC maximum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

RC10_MIN.label: RC min PWM
RC10_MIN.description: RC minimum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

RC10_OPTION.label: RC input option
RC10_OPTION.description: Function assigned to this RC channel

options.label: Test autotuned gains after tune is complete

RC10_REVERSED.label: RC reversed
RC10_REVERSED.description: Reverse channel input. Set to 0 for normal operation. Set to 1 to reverse this input channel.

RC10_TRIM.label: RC trim PWM
RC10_TRIM.description: RC trim (neutral) PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

RC11_DZ.description: PWM dead zone in microseconds around trim or bottom

RCK_ENABLE.label: Enable Message transmission
RCK_ENABLE.description: Enables the Rockblock sending and recieving

RCK_FORCEHL.label: Force enable High Latency mode
RCK_FORCEHL.description: Automatically enables High Latency mode if not already enabled

RCK_PERIOD.label: Update rate
RCK_PERIOD.description: When in High Latency mode, send Rockblock updates every N seconds

RCK_TIMEOUT.label: GCS timeout to start sendin Rockblock messages
RCK_TIMEOUT.description: If RCK_FORCEHL=2, this is the number of seconds of GCS timeout until High Latency mode is auto-enabled

RCMAP_PITCH.label: Pitch channel
RCMAP_PITCH.description: Pitch channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Pitch is normally on channel 2, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RCMAP_ROLL.label: Roll channel
RCMAP_ROLL.description: Roll channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Roll is normally on channel 1, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RCMAP_THROTTLE.label: Throttle channel
RCMAP_THROTTLE.description: Throttle channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Throttle is normally on channel 3, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter. Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RCMAP_YAW.label: Yaw channel
RCMAP_YAW.description: Yaw channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Yaw (also known as rudder) is normally on channel 4, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RC_FS_TIMEOUT.label: RC Failsafe timeout
RC_FS_TIMEOUT.description: RC failsafe will trigger this many seconds after loss of RC

options.label: Ignore Receiver Failsafe bit but allow other RC failsafes if setup
Skip the arming check for neutral Roll/Pitch/Yaw sticks
Suppress CRSF mode/rate message for ELRS systems
Use Link Quality for RSSI with CRSF
Annotate CRSF flight mode with * on disarm

RC_OVERRIDE_TIME.label: RC override timeout
RC_OVERRIDE_TIME.description: Timeout after which RC overrides will no longer be used, and RC input will resume, 0 will disable RC overrides, -1 will never timeout, and continue using overrides until they are disabled

RC_PROTOCOLS.label: RC protocols enabled
RC_PROTOCOLS.description: Bitmask of enabled RC protocols. Allows narrowing the protocol detection to only specific types of RC receivers which can avoid issues with incorrect detection. Set to 1 to enable all protocols.

RC_SPEED.label: ESC Update Speed
RC_SPEED.description: This is the speed in Hertz that your ESCs will receive updates

RELAY10_DEFAULT.label: Relay default state
RELAY10_DEFAULT.description: Should the relay default to on or off, this only applies to RELAYx_FUNC "Relay" (1). All other uses will pick the appropriate default output state from within the controlling function's parameters. Note that if INVERTED is set then the default is inverted.

RELAY10_FUNCTION.label: Relay function
RELAY10_FUNCTION.description: The function the relay channel is mapped to.

RELAY10_INVERTED.label: Relay invert output signal
RELAY10_INVERTED.description: Should the relay output signal be inverted. If enabled, relay on would be pin low and relay off would be pin high. NOTE: this impact's DEFAULT.

RELAY10_PIN.label: Relay pin
RELAY10_PIN.description: Digital pin number for relay control. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RK9_BOOTED.label: Rockblock booted feedback
RK9_BOOTED.description: SERVOn GPIO channel that reads the Rockblock booted state. This connects to I_BTD on the Rockblock header. Requires SERVON_FUNCTION=-1

RK9_DEBUG.label: Display Rockblock debugging text
RK9_DEBUG.description: Sends Rockblock debug text to GCS via statustexts

RK9_ENABLE.label: Enable Message transmission
RK9_ENABLE.description: Enables the Rockblock sending and recieving

RK9_FORCEHL.label: Force enable High Latency mode
RK9_FORCEHL.description: Automatically enables High Latency mode if not already enabled

RK9_PERIOD.label: Update rate
RK9_PERIOD.description: When in High Latency mode, send Rockblock updates every N seconds

RK9_RELAY.label: Rockblock Power Relay
RK9_RELAY.description: RELAYn output to control Rockblock power. This connects to I_EN on the Rockblock header.

RK9_SCRPORT.label: Rockblock Scripting Serial Port
RK9_SCRPORT.description: Scripting Serial port number to which the Rockblock is connected for HL2 messages This is the index of the SCR_SDEV ports that are set to 2 for "MavlinkHL"

RK9_SERPORT.label: Rockblock Serial Port
RK9_SERPORT.description: Serial port number to which the Rockblock is connected.This is the index of the SERIALn_ ports that are set to 28 for "scripting"

RK9_TIMEOUT.label: GCS timeout to start sendin Rockblock messages
RK9_TIMEOUT.description: If RK9_FORCEHL=2, this is the number of seconds of GCS timeout until High Latency mode is auto-enabled

RNGFND1_ADDR.label: Bus address of sensor
RNGFND1_ADDR.description: This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

RNGFND1_FUNCTION.label: Rangefinder function
RNGFND1_FUNCTION.description: Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

RNGFND1_GNDCLR.label: Distance from the range finder to the ground
RNGFND1_GNDCLR.description: This parameter sets the expected range measurement that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

RNGFND1_GRF_RATE.label: LightWare GRF Update Rate
RNGFND1_GRF_RATE.description: The update rate of the sensor in Hz.

RNGFND1_GRF_RET.label: LightWare GRF Distance Return Type
RNGFND1_GRF_RET.description: Selects which single return to use.

RNGFND1_GRF_ST.label: LightWare GRF Minimum Return Strength
RNGFND1_GRF_ST.description: Minimum acceptable return signal strength in dB. Returns weaker than this will be ignored. Set to 0 to disable filtering.

RNGFND1_MAX.label: Rangefinder maximum distance
RNGFND1_MAX.description: Maximum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND1_MIN.label: Rangefinder minimum distance
RNGFND1_MIN.description: Minimum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND1_OFFSET.label: rangefinder offset
RNGFND1_OFFSET.description: Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

RNGFND1_PIN.label: Rangefinder pin
RNGFND1_PIN.description: Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

RNGFND1_POS_X.label: X position offset
RNGFND1_POS_X.description: X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND1_POS_Y.label: Y position offset
RNGFND1_POS_Y.description: Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND1_POS_Z.label: Z position offset
RNGFND1_POS_Z.description: Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND1_PWRRNG.label: Powersave range
RNGFND1_PWRRNG.description: This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

RNGFND1_RECV_ID.label: RangeFinder CAN receive ID
RNGFND1_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

RNGFND1_RMETRIC.label: Ratiometric
RNGFND1_RMETRIC.description: This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

RNGFND1_SCALING.label: Rangefinder scaling
RNGFND1_SCALING.description: Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

RNGFND1_SNR_MIN.label: RangeFinder Minimum signal strength
RNGFND1_SNR_MIN.description: RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance

RNGFND1_STOP_PIN.label: Rangefinder stop pin
RNGFND1_STOP_PIN.description: Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RNGFND1_WSP_AVG.label: Multi-pulse averages
RNGFND1_WSP_AVG.description: Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement

RNGFND1_WSP_FRQ.label: Frequency
RNGFND1_WSP_FRQ.description: Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.

RNGFND1_WSP_MAVG.label: Moving Average Range
RNGFND1_WSP_MAVG.description: Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results

RNGFND1_WSP_MEDF.label: Moving Median Filter
RNGFND1_WSP_MEDF.description: Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active

RNGFND1_WSP_THR.label: Sensitivity threshold
RNGFND1_WSP_THR.description: Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments

RNGFND2_ADDR.label: Bus address of sensor
RNGFND2_ADDR.description: This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

RNGFND2_FUNCTION.label: Rangefinder function
RNGFND2_FUNCTION.description: Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

RNGFND2_GNDCLR.label: Distance from the range finder to the ground
RNGFND2_GNDCLR.description: This parameter sets the expected range measurement that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

RNGFND2_GRF_RATE.label: LightWare GRF Update Rate
RNGFND2_GRF_RATE.description: The update rate of the sensor in Hz.

RNGFND2_GRF_RET.label: LightWare GRF Distance Return Type
RNGFND2_GRF_RET.description: Selects which single return to use.

RNGFND2_GRF_ST.label: LightWare GRF Minimum Return Strength
RNGFND2_GRF_ST.description: Minimum acceptable return signal strength in dB. Returns weaker than this will be ignored. Set to 0 to disable filtering.

RNGFND2_MAX.label: Rangefinder maximum distance
RNGFND2_MAX.description: Maximum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND2_MIN.label: Rangefinder minimum distance
RNGFND2_MIN.description: Minimum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND2_OFFSET.label: rangefinder offset
RNGFND2_OFFSET.description: Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

RNGFND2_PIN.label: Rangefinder pin
RNGFND2_PIN.description: Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

RNGFND2_POS_X.label: X position offset
RNGFND2_POS_X.description: X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND2_POS_Y.label: Y position offset
RNGFND2_POS_Y.description: Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND2_POS_Z.label: Z position offset
RNGFND2_POS_Z.description: Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND2_PWRRNG.label: Powersave range
RNGFND2_PWRRNG.description: This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

RNGFND2_RECV_ID.label: RangeFinder CAN receive ID
RNGFND2_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

RNGFND2_RMETRIC.label: Ratiometric
RNGFND2_RMETRIC.description: This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

RNGFND2_SCALING.label: Rangefinder scaling
RNGFND2_SCALING.description: Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

RNGFND2_SNR_MIN.label: RangeFinder Minimum signal strength
RNGFND2_SNR_MIN.description: RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance

RNGFND2_STOP_PIN.label: Rangefinder stop pin
RNGFND2_STOP_PIN.description: Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RNGFND2_WSP_AVG.label: Multi-pulse averages
RNGFND2_WSP_AVG.description: Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement

RNGFND2_WSP_FRQ.label: Frequency
RNGFND2_WSP_FRQ.description: Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.

RNGFND2_WSP_MAVG.label: Moving Average Range
RNGFND2_WSP_MAVG.description: Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results

RNGFND2_WSP_MEDF.label: Moving Median Filter
RNGFND2_WSP_MEDF.description: Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active

RNGFND2_WSP_THR.label: Sensitivity threshold
RNGFND2_WSP_THR.description: Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments
