RNGFND7_FUNCTION.description: Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

RNGFND7_GNDCLR.label: Distance from the range finder to the ground
RNGFND7_GNDCLR.description: This parameter sets the expected range measurement that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

RNGFND7_GRF_RATE.label: LightWare GRF Update Rate
RNGFND7_GRF_RATE.description: The update rate of the sensor in Hz.

RNGFND7_GRF_RET.label: LightWare GRF Distance Return Type
RNGFND7_GRF_RET.description: Selects which single return to use.

RNGFND7_GRF_ST.label: LightWare GRF Minimum Return Strength
RNGFND7_GRF_ST.description: Minimum acceptable return signal strength in dB. Returns weaker than this will be ignored. Set to 0 to disable filtering.

RNGFND7_MAX.label: Rangefinder maximum distance
RNGFND7_MAX.description: Maximum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND7_MIN.label: Rangefinder minimum distance
RNGFND7_MIN.description: Minimum distance in metres that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND7_OFFSET.label: rangefinder offset
RNGFND7_OFFSET.description: Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

RNGFND7_PIN.label: Rangefinder pin
RNGFND7_PIN.description: Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

RNGFND7_POS_X.label: X position offset
RNGFND7_POS_X.description: X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND7_POS_Y.label: Y position offset
RNGFND7_POS_Y.description: Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND7_POS_Z.label: Z position offset
RNGFND7_POS_Z.description: Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND7_PWRRNG.label: Powersave range
RNGFND7_PWRRNG.description: This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

RNGFND7_RECV_ID.label: RangeFinder CAN receive ID
RNGFND7_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

RNGFND7_RMETRIC.label: Ratiometric
RNGFND7_RMETRIC.description: This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

RNGFND7_SCALING.label: Rangefinder scaling
RNGFND7_SCALING.description: Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

RNGFND7_SNR_MIN.label: RangeFinder Minimum signal strength
RNGFND7_SNR_MIN.description: RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance

RNGFND7_STOP_PIN.label: Rangefinder stop pin
RNGFND7_STOP_PIN.description: Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RNGFND7_WSP_AVG.label: Multi-pulse averages
RNGFND7_WSP_AVG.description: Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement

RNGFND7_WSP_FRQ.label: Frequency
RNGFND7_WSP_FRQ.description: Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.

RNGFND7_WSP_MAVG.label: Moving Average Range
RNGFND7_WSP_MAVG.description: Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results

RNGFND7_WSP_MEDF.label: Moving Median Filter
RNGFND7_WSP_MEDF.description: Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active

RNGFND7_WSP_THR.label: Sensitivity threshold
RNGFND7_WSP_THR.description: Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments

RNGFND8_ADDR.description: This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

RSSI_CHANNEL.description: The channel number where RSSI will be output by the radio receiver (5 and above).

RSSI_CHAN_HIGH.label: Receiver RSSI PWM high value
RSSI_CHAN_HIGH.description: PWM value that the radio receiver will put on the RSSI_CHANNEL or RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the strongest. Some radio receivers output inverted values so this value may be higher than RSSI_CHAN_LOW

RSSI_CHAN_LOW.label: RSSI PWM low value
RSSI_CHAN_LOW.description: PWM value that the radio receiver will put on the RSSI_CHANNEL or RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the weakest. Some radio receivers output inverted values so this value may be lower than RSSI_CHAN_HIGH

RSSI_PIN_HIGH.label: RSSI pin's highest voltage
RSSI_PIN_HIGH.description: RSSI pin's voltage received on the RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the strongest. Some radio receivers put out inverted values so this value may be lower than RSSI_PIN_LOW. When using pin 103, the maximum value of the parameter is 3.3V.

RSSI_PIN_LOW.label: RSSI pin's lowest voltage
RSSI_PIN_LOW.description: RSSI pin's voltage received on the RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the weakest. Some radio receivers put out inverted values so this value may be higher than RSSI_PIN_HIGH. When using pin 103, the maximum value of the parameter is 3.3V.

RSSI_TYPE.label: RSSI Type
RSSI_TYPE.description: Radio Receiver RSSI type. If your radio receiver supports RSSI of some kind, set it here, then set its associated RSSI_XXXXX parameters, if any.

RTL_ALT_FINAL_M.label: RTL Final Altitude
RTL_ALT_FINAL_M.description: Altitude the vehicle will move to as the final stage of Returning to Launch or after completing a mission. Set to zero to land.

RTL_ALT_M.label: RTL Altitude
RTL_ALT_M.description: The minimum alt above home the vehicle will climb to before returning. If the vehicle is flying higher than this value it will return at its current altitude.

RTL_ALT_TYPE.label: RTL mode altitude type
RTL_ALT_TYPE.description: RTL altitude type.  Set to 1 for Terrain following during RTL and then set WP_RFND_USE=1 to use rangefinder or WP_RFND_USE=0 to use Terrain database

RTL_CLIMB_MIN_M.label: RTL minimum climb
RTL_CLIMB_MIN_M.description: The vehicle will climb this many meters during the initial climb portion of the RTL

RTL_CONE_SLOPE.label: RTL cone slope
RTL_CONE_SLOPE.description: Defines a cone above home which determines maximum climb

RTL_LOIT_TIME.label: RTL loiter time
RTL_LOIT_TIME.description: Time (in milliseconds) to loiter above home before beginning final descent

RTL_OPTIONS.label: RTL mode options
RTL_OPTIONS.description: Options that can be applied to change RTL mode behaviour

RTL_SPEED_MS.label: RTL speed
RTL_SPEED_MS.description: The speed in m/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain horizontally while flying home. If this is set to zero, WP_SPD will be used instead.

RTUN_AUTO_FILTER.label: Rover Quicktune auto filter enable
RTUN_AUTO_FILTER.description: When enabled the PID filter settings are automatically set based on INS_GYRO_FILTER

RTUN_AUTO_SAVE.label: Rover Quicktune auto save
RTUN_AUTO_SAVE.description: Number of seconds after completion of tune to auto-save. This is useful when using a 2 position switch for quicktune

RTUN_RC_FUNC.label: Rover Quicktune RC function
RTUN_RC_FUNC.description: RCn_OPTION number to use to control tuning stop/start/save

RTUN_SPD_FFRATIO.label: Rover Quicktune Speed FeedForward (equivalent) ratio
RTUN_SPD_FFRATIO.description: Ratio between measured response and CRUISE_THROTTLE value. Raise this to get a higher CRUISE_THROTTLE value

RTUN_SPD_I_RATIO.label: Rover Quicktune Speed FF to I ratio
RTUN_SPD_I_RATIO.description: Ratio between speed FF and I gain. Raise this to get a higher I gain, 0 to leave I unchanged

RTUN_SPD_P_RATIO.label: Rover Quicktune Speed FF to P ratio
RTUN_SPD_P_RATIO.description: Ratio between speed FF and P gain. Raise this to get a higher P gain, 0 to leave P unchanged

RTUN_SPEED_MIN.label: Rover Quicktune minimum speed for tuning
RTUN_SPEED_MIN.description: The mimimum speed in m/s required for tuning to start

RTUN_STR_FFRATIO.label: Rover Quicktune Steering Rate FeedForward ratio
RTUN_STR_FFRATIO.description: Ratio between measured response and FF gain. Raise this to get a higher FF gain

RTUN_STR_I_RATIO.label: Rover Quicktune Steering FF to I ratio
RTUN_STR_I_RATIO.description: Ratio between steering FF and I gains. Raise this to get a higher I gain, 0 to leave I unchanged

RTUN_STR_P_RATIO.label: Rover Quicktune Steering FF to P ratio
RTUN_STR_P_RATIO.description: Ratio between steering FF and P gains. Raise this to get a higher P gain, 0 to leave P unchanged

SCHED_DEBUG.label: Scheduler debug level
SCHED_DEBUG.description: Set to non-zero to enable scheduler debug messages. When set to show "Slips" the scheduler will display a message whenever a scheduled task is delayed due to too much CPU load. When set to ShowOverruns the scheduled will display a message whenever a task takes longer than the limit promised in the task table.

SCHED_LOOP_RATE.label: Scheduling main loop rate
SCHED_LOOP_RATE.description: This controls the rate of the main control loop in Hz. This should only be changed by developers. This only takes effect on restart. Values over 400 are considered highly experimental.

SCHED_OPTIONS.label: Scheduling options
SCHED_OPTIONS.description: This controls optional aspects of the scheduler.

options.label: No Scripts to run message if all scripts have stopped
Runtime messages for memory usage and execution time
log runtime memory usage and execution time
Save CRC of current scripts to loaded and running checksum parameters enabling pre-arm
Disable heap expansion on allocation failure

SCR_DIR_DISABLE.label: Directory disable
SCR_DIR_DISABLE.description: This will stop scripts being loaded from the given locations

SCR_HEAP_SIZE.label: Scripting Heap Size
SCR_HEAP_SIZE.description: Amount of memory available for scripting

SCR_LD_CHECKSUM.label: Loaded script checksum
SCR_LD_CHECKSUM.description: Required XOR of CRC32 checksum of loaded scripts, vehicle will not arm with incorrect scripts loaded, -1 disables

SCR_RUN_CHECKSUM.label: Running script checksum
SCR_RUN_CHECKSUM.description: Required XOR of CRC32 checksum of running scripts, vehicle will not arm with incorrect scripts running, -1 disables

SCR_SDEV1_PROTO.label: Serial protocol of scripting serial device
SCR_SDEV1_PROTO.description: Serial protocol of scripting serial device

SCR_SDEV2_PROTO.label: Serial protocol of scripting serial device
SCR_SDEV2_PROTO.description: Serial protocol of scripting serial device

SCR_SDEV3_PROTO.label: Serial protocol of scripting serial device
SCR_SDEV3_PROTO.description: Serial protocol of scripting serial device

SCR_THD_PRIORITY.label: Scripting thread priority
SCR_THD_PRIORITY.description: This sets the priority of the scripting thread. This is normally set to a low priority to prevent scripts from interfering with other parts of the system. Advanced users can change this priority if scripting needs to be prioritised for realtime applications. WARNING: changing this parameter can impact the stability of your flight controller. The scipting thread priority in this parameter is chosen based on a set of system level priorities for other subsystems. It is strongly recommended that you use the lowest priority that is sufficient for your application. Note that all scripts run at the same priority, so if you raise this priority you must carefully audit all lua scripts for behaviour that does not interfere with the operation of the system.

SCR_USER1.label: Scripting User Parameter1
SCR_USER1.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER2.label: Scripting User Parameter2
SCR_USER2.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER3.label: Scripting User Parameter3
SCR_USER3.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER4.label: Scripting User Parameter4
SCR_USER4.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER5.label: Scripting User Parameter5
SCR_USER5.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER6.label: Scripting User Parameter6
SCR_USER6.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_VM_I_COUNT.label: Scripting Virtual Machine Instruction Count
SCR_VM_I_COUNT.description: The number virtual machine instructions that can be run before considering a script to have taken an excessive amount of time

SERIAL0_BAUD.label: Serial0 baud rate
SERIAL0_BAUD.description: The baud rate used on the USB console. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL0_PROTOCOL.label: Console protocol selection
SERIAL0_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol to use on the console.

SERIAL1_BAUD.label: Telem1 Baud Rate
SERIAL1_BAUD.description: The baud rate used on the Telem1 port. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL1_OPTIONS.label: Telem1 options
SERIAL1_OPTIONS.description: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.  NOTE that two bits have moved from this parameter into MAVn_OPTIONS!

options.label: Don't forward mavlink to/from (moved to MAVn_OPTIONS >4.7)

SERIAL1_PROTOCOL.label: Telem1 protocol selection
SERIAL1_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol to use on the Telem1 port. Note that the Frsky options require external converter hardware. See the wiki for details.

SERIAL2_BAUD.label: Telemetry 2 Baud Rate
SERIAL2_BAUD.description: The baud rate of the Telem2 port. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL_PASS1.label: Serial passthru first port
SERIAL_PASS1.description: This sets one side of pass-through between two serial ports. Once both sides are set then all data received on either port will be passed to the other port

SERIAL_PASS2.label: Serial passthru second port
SERIAL_PASS2.description: This sets one side of pass-through between two serial ports. Once both sides are set then all data received on either port will be passed to the other port. This parameter is normally reset to -1 on reboot, disabling passthrough. If SERIAL_PASSTIMO is set to -1 then it is not reset on reboot.

SERIAL_PASSTIMO.label: Serial passthru timeout
SERIAL_PASSTIMO.description: This sets a timeout for serial pass-through in seconds. When the pass-through is enabled by setting the SERIAL_PASS1 and SERIAL_PASS2 parameters then it remains in effect until no data comes from the first port for SERIAL_PASSTIMO seconds. This allows the port to revent to its normal usage (such as MAVLink connection to a GCS) when it is no longer needed. A value of 0 means no timeout. A value of -1 means no timeout and the SERIAL_PASS2 parameter is not reset on reboot.

SERVO10_FUNCTION.label: Servo output function
SERVO10_FUNCTION.description: Function assigned to this servo. Setting this to Disabled(0) will setup this output for control by auto missions or MAVLink servo set commands. any other value will enable the corresponding function

SERVO10_MAX.label: Maximum PWM
SERVO10_MAX.description: maximum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

SERVO10_MIN.label: Minimum PWM
SERVO10_MIN.description: minimum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

SERVO10_REVERSED.label: Servo reverse
SERVO10_REVERSED.description: Reverse servo operation. Set to 0 for normal operation. Set to 1 to reverse this output channel.

SERVO10_TRIM.label: Trim PWM
SERVO10_TRIM.description: Trim PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

SERVO_32_ENABLE.label: Enable outputs 17 to 31
SERVO_32_ENABLE.description: This allows for up to 32 outputs, enabling parameters for outputs above 16

SERVO_BLH_3DMASK.label: BLHeli bitmask of 3D channels
SERVO_BLH_3DMASK.description: Mask of channels which are dynamically reversible. This is used to configure ESCs in '3D' mode, allowing for the motor to spin in either direction. Note that setting an ESC as reversible with this option on AM32 will result in the forward direction of the ESC changing. You can combine with parameter with the SERVO_BLH_RVMASK parameter to maintain the same direction when the ESC is in 3D mode as it has in unidirectional (non-3D) mode.

SERVO_BLH_AUTO.label: BLHeli pass-thru auto-enable for multicopter motors
SERVO_BLH_AUTO.description: If set to 1 this auto-enables BLHeli pass-thru support for all multicopter motors

SERVO_BLH_BDMASK.label: BLHeli bitmask of bi-directional dshot channels
SERVO_BLH_BDMASK.description: Mask of channels which support bi-directional dshot telemetry. This is used for ESCs which have firmware that supports bi-directional dshot allowing fast rpm telemetry values to be returned for the harmonic notch.

SERVO_BLH_DEBUG.label: BLHeli debug level
SERVO_BLH_DEBUG.description: When set to 1 this enabled verbose debugging output over MAVLink when the blheli protocol is active. This can be used to diagnose failures.

SERVO_BLH_MASK.label: BLHeli Channel Bitmask
SERVO_BLH_MASK.description: Enable of BLHeli pass-thru servo protocol support to specific channels. This mask is in addition to motors enabled using SERVO_BLH_AUTO (if any)

SERVO_BLH_OTYPE.label: BLHeli output type override
SERVO_BLH_OTYPE.description: When set to a non-zero value this overrides the output type for the output channels given by SERVO_BLH_MASK. This can be used to enable DShot on outputs that are not part of the multicopter motors group.

SERVO_BLH_POLES.label: BLHeli Motor Poles
SERVO_BLH_POLES.description: This allows calculation of true RPM from ESC's eRPM. The default is 14.

SERVO_BLH_PORT.label: Control port
SERVO_BLH_PORT.description: This sets the mavlink channel to use for blheli pass-thru. The channel number is determined by the number of serial ports configured to use mavlink. So 0 is always the console, 1 is the next serial port using mavlink, 2 the next after that and so on.

SERVO_BLH_RVMASK.label: BLHeli bitmask of reversed channels
SERVO_BLH_RVMASK.description: Mask of channels which are reversed. This is used to configure ESCs to reverse motor direction. Note that when combined with SERVO_BLH_3DMASK this will change what direction is considered to be forward.

SERVO_BLH_TEST.label: BLHeli internal interface test
SERVO_BLH_TEST.description: Setting SERVO_BLH_TEST to a motor number enables an internal test of the BLHeli ESC protocol to the corresponding ESC. The debug output is displayed on the USB console.

SERVO_BLH_TMOUT.label: BLHeli protocol timeout
SERVO_BLH_TMOUT.description: This sets the inactivity timeout for the BLHeli protocol in seconds. If no packets are received in this time normal MAVLink operations are resumed. A value of 0 means no timeout

SERVO_BLH_TRATE.label: BLHeli telemetry rate
SERVO_BLH_TRATE.description: This sets the rate in Hz for requesting telemetry from ESCs. It is the rate per ESC. Setting to zero disables telemetry requests

SERVO_DSHOT_ESC.label: Servo DShot ESC type
SERVO_DSHOT_ESC.description: DShot ESC type for all outputs. The ESC type affects the range of DShot commands available and the bit widths used. None means that no dshot commands will be executed. Some ESC types support Extended DShot Telemetry (EDT) which allows telemetry other than RPM data to be returned when using bi-directional dshot. If you enable EDT you must install EDT capable firmware for correct operation.

SERVO_DSHOT_RATE.label: Servo DShot output rate
SERVO_DSHOT_RATE.description: DShot output rate for all outputs as a multiple of the loop rate. 0 sets the output rate to be fixed at 1Khz for low loop rates. This value should never be set below 500Hz.

SERVO_FTW_MASK.label: Servo channel output bitmask
SERVO_FTW_MASK.description: Servo channel mask specifying FETtec ESC output.

SERVO_FTW_RVMASK.label: Servo channel reverse rotation bitmask
SERVO_FTW_RVMASK.description: Servo channel mask to reverse rotation of FETtec ESC outputs.

SERVO_GPIO_MASK.label: Servo GPIO mask
SERVO_GPIO_MASK.description: Bitmask of outputs which will be available as GPIOs. Any output with either the function set to -1 or with the corresponding bit set in this mask will be available for use as a GPIO pin

SERVO_RATE.label: Servo default output rate
SERVO_RATE.description: Default output rate in Hz for all PWM outputs.

SERVO_RC_FS_MSK.label: Servo RC Failsafe Mask
SERVO_RC_FS_MSK.description: Bitmask of scaled passthru output channels which will be set to their trim value during rc failsafe instead of holding their last position before failsafe.

SERVO_ROB_POSMAX.label: Robotis servo position max
SERVO_ROB_POSMAX.description: Position maximum at servo max value. This should be within the position control range of the servos, normally 0 to 4095

SERVO_ROB_POSMIN.label: Robotis servo position min
SERVO_ROB_POSMIN.description: Position minimum at servo min value. This should be within the position control range of the servos, normally 0 to 4095

SERVO_SBUS_RATE.label: SBUS default output rate
SERVO_SBUS_RATE.description: This sets the SBUS output frame rate in Hz.

SERVO_VOLZ_MASK.label: Channel Bitmask
SERVO_VOLZ_MASK.description: Enable of volz servo protocol to specific channels

SERVO_VOLZ_RANGE.label: Range of travel
SERVO_VOLZ_RANGE.description: Range to map between 1000 and 2000 PWM. Default value of 200 gives full +-100 deg range of extended position command. This results in 0.2 deg movement per US change in PWM. If the full range is not needed it can be reduced to increase resolution. 40 deg range gives 0.04 deg movement per US change in PWM, this is higher resolution than possible with the VOLZ protocol so further reduction in range will not improve resolution. Reduced range does allow PWMs outside the 1000 to 2000 range, with 40 deg range 750 PWM results in a angle of -30 deg, 2250 would be +30 deg. This is still limited by the 200 deg maximum range of the actuator.

SHIP_AUTO_OFS.label: Ship automatic offset trigger
SHIP_AUTO_OFS.description: Settings this parameter to one triggers an automatic follow offset calculation based on current position of the vehicle and the landing target. NOTE: This parameter will auto-reset to zero once the offset has been calculated.

SHIP_LAND_ANGLE.label: Ship landing angle
SHIP_LAND_ANGLE.description: Angle from the stern of the ship for landing approach. Use this to ensure that on a go-around that ship superstructure and cables are avoided. A value of zero means to approach from the rear of the ship. A value of 90 means the landing will approach from the port (left) side of the ship. A value of -90 will mean approaching from the starboard (right) side of the ship. A value of 180 will approach from the bow of the ship. This parameter is combined with the sign of the RTL_RADIUS parameter to determine the holdoff pattern. If RTL_RADIUS is positive then a clockwise loiter is performed, if RTL_RADIUS is negative then a counter-clockwise loiter is used.

SID_AXIS.label: System identification axis
SID_AXIS.description: Controls which axis are being excited.  Set to non-zero to see more parameters

SID_F_START_HZ.label: System identification Start Frequency
SID_F_START_HZ.description: Frequency at the start of the sweep

SID_F_STOP_HZ.label: System identification Stop Frequency
SID_F_STOP_HZ.description: Frequency at the end of the sweep

SID_MAGNITUDE.label: System identification Chirp Magnitude
SID_MAGNITUDE.description: Magnitude of sweep in deg, deg/s and 0-1 for mixer outputs.

SID_T_FADE_IN.label: System identification Fade in time
SID_T_FADE_IN.description: Time to reach maximum amplitude of sweep

SID_T_FADE_OUT.label: System identification Fade out time
SID_T_FADE_OUT.description: Time to reach zero amplitude at the end of the sweep

SID_T_REC.label: System identification Total Sweep length
SID_T_REC.description: Time taken to complete the sweep

SIMPLE.label: Simple mode bitmask
SIMPLE.description: Bitmask which holds which flight modes use simple heading mode (eg bit 0 = 1 means Flight Mode 0 uses simple mode). The bitmask is for flightmode switch positions.

SIM_ACC1_RND.label: Accel 1 motor noise factor
SIM_ACC1_RND.description: scaling factor for simulated vibration from motors

SIM_ACC1_SCAL_X.label: Accel 1 scaling factor
SIM_ACC1_SCAL_X.description: scaling factors applied to simulated accelerometer (X-axis)

SIM_ACC1_SCAL_Y.label: Accel 1 scaling factor
SIM_ACC1_SCAL_Y.description: scaling factors applied to simulated accelerometer (Y-axis)

SIM_BATT_VOLTAGE.description: Simulated battery resting voltage (no load sag). Defaults to and clipped to the battery model's maximum voltage. Changes re-initialize the state of charge, and values below the maximum indicate a partially-charged battery. For batteries with unlimited capacity, see `SIM_BATT_CAP_AH`. Value ignored when receiving battery state updates from an external source.

SIM_MAG3_ODI_Z.description: ODI_Z in the magnetometer soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

SIM_MAG3_OFS_X.label: Magnetometer offset applied to SITL
SIM_MAG3_OFS_X.description: Magnetometer offset injected into the simulation (X-axis)

SIM_MAG3_OFS_Y.label: Magnetometer offset applied to SITL
SIM_MAG3_OFS_Y.description: Magnetometer offset injected into the simulation (Y-axis)

SIM_MAG3_OFS_Z.label: Magnetometer offset applied to SITL
SIM_MAG3_OFS_Z.description: Magnetometer offset injected into the simulation (Z-axis)

SIM_MAG3_SCALING.label: MAG3 Scaling factor
SIM_MAG3_SCALING.description: Scale the compass 3 to simulate sensor scale factor errors

SIM_MAG_ALY_HGT.label: Magnetic anomaly height
SIM_MAG_ALY_HGT.description: Height above ground where anomally strength has decayed to 1/8 of the ground level value

SIM_MAG_ALY_X.label: NED anomaly vector at ground level
SIM_MAG_ALY_X.description: Simulates localized magnetic field distortions at ground level that decays with altitude. (X-axis)

SIM_MAG_ALY_Y.label: NED anomaly vector at ground level
SIM_MAG_ALY_Y.description: Simulates localized magnetic field distortions at ground level that decays with altitude. (Y-axis)

SIM_MAG_ALY_Z.label: NED anomaly vector at ground level
SIM_MAG_ALY_Z.description: Simulates localized magnetic field distortions at ground level that decays with altitude. (Z-axis)

SIM_MAG_MOT_X.label: Motor magnetic interference
SIM_MAG_MOT_X.description: Simulates distortion of magnetometer readings caused by motor current (X-axis)

SIM_MAG_MOT_Y.label: Motor magnetic interference
SIM_MAG_MOT_Y.description: Simulates distortion of magnetometer readings caused by motor current (Y-axis)

SIM_MAG_MOT_Z.label: Motor magnetic interference
SIM_MAG_MOT_Z.description: Simulates distortion of magnetometer readings caused by motor current (Z-axis)

SIM_MAG_RND.label: Mag motor noise factor
SIM_MAG_RND.description: Scaling factor for simulated vibration from motors

SIM_MAG_SAVE_IDS.label: Save MAG devids on startup
SIM_MAG_SAVE_IDS.description: This forces saving of compass devids on startup so that simulated compasses start as calibrated

SIM_OH_MASK.label: SIM-on_hardware Output Enable Mask
SIM_OH_MASK.description: channels which are passed through to actual hardware when running sim on actual hardware

SIM_OH_RELAY_MSK.label: SIM-on_hardware Relay Enable Mask
SIM_OH_RELAY_MSK.description: Allow relay output operation when running SIM-on-hardware

SIM_OSD_COLUMNS.label: Simulated OSD number of text columns
SIM_OSD_COLUMNS.description: Simulated OSD number of text columns

SIM_OSD_ROWS.label: Simulated OSD number of text rows
SIM_OSD_ROWS.description: Simulated OSD number of text rows

SIM_PARA_ENABLE.label: Parachute Sim enable/disable
SIM_PARA_ENABLE.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the Parachute simulation

SIM_PARA_PIN.label: Parachute pin
SIM_PARA_PIN.description: The pin number that the Parachute pyrotechnics are connected to. (start at 1)

SIM_PLD_ENABLE.label: Preland device Sim enable/disable
SIM_PLD_ENABLE.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the Preland simulation

SIM_PLD_HEIGHT.label: Precland device center's height SITL origin
SIM_PLD_HEIGHT.description: Precland device center's height above SITL origin. Assumes a 2x2m square as station base

SIM_PLD_RATE.label: Precland device update rate
SIM_PLD_RATE.description: Precland device rate. e.g led patter refresh rate, RF message rate, etc.

SIM_PLD_SHIP.label: SIM_Precland follow ship
SIM_PLD_SHIP.description: This makes the position of the landing beacon follow the simulated ship from SIM_SHIP. The ship movement is controlled with the SIM_SHIP parameters

SIM_PLD_TYPE.label: Precland device radiance type
SIM_PLD_TYPE.description: Precland device radiance type: it can be a cylinder, a cone, or a sphere.

SIM_PLD_YAW.label: Precland device systems rotation from north
SIM_PLD_YAW.description: Precland device systems rotation from north

SIM_RC_FAIL.label: Simulated RC signal failure
SIM_RC_FAIL.description: Allows you to emulate rc failures in sim

options.label: All Channels neutral except Throttle is 950us
Swap first 4 and last 4 servos (for quadplane testing)
Demix heli servos and send roll/pitch/collective/yaw

SIM_RICH_CTRL.label: Pin RichenPower is connectred to
SIM_RICH_CTRL.description: The pin number that the RichenPower spinner servo is connected to. (start at 1)

SIM_RICH_ENABLE.label: RichenPower Generator sim enable/disable
SIM_RICH_ENABLE.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the RichenPower simulator

SIM_SB_ARM_LEN.label: arm length
SIM_SB_ARM_LEN.description: distance from center of mass to one motor

SIM_SB_COL.label: center of lift
SIM_SB_COL.description: center of lift position above CoG

SIM_SB_FLR.label: free lift rate
SIM_SB_FLR.description: amount of additional lift generated by the helper balloon (for the purpose of ascent), as a proportion of the 'neutral buoyancy' lift

SIM_SB_MASS.label: mass
SIM_SB_MASS.description: mass of blimp not including lifting gas

SIM_SB_MOT_THST.label: motor thrust
SIM_SB_MOT_THST.description: thrust at max throttle for one motor

SIM_SB_YAW_RT.label: yaw rate
SIM_SB_YAW_RT.description: maximum yaw rate with full left throttle at target altitude

SIM_SERVO_SPEED.label: servo speed
SIM_SERVO_SPEED.description: servo speed (time for 60 degree deflection). If DELAY and FILTER are not set then this is converted to a 1p lowpass filter. If DELAY or FILTER are set then this is treated as a rate of change limit

SIM_SHIP_OFS_X.label: Ship landing pad offset
SIM_SHIP_OFS_X.description: Defines the offset of the ship's landing pad w.r.t. the ship's origin, i.e. where the beacon is placed on the ship (X-axis)

SIM_SHIP_OFS_Y.label: Ship landing pad offset
SIM_SHIP_OFS_Y.description: Defines the offset of the ship's landing pad w.r.t. the ship's origin, i.e. where the beacon is placed on the ship (Y-axis)

SIM_SHIP_OFS_Z.label: Ship landing pad offset
SIM_SHIP_OFS_Z.description: Defines the offset of the ship's landing pad w.r.t. the ship's origin, i.e. where the beacon is placed on the ship (Z-axis)

SIM_SHIP_PSIZE.label: Path Size
SIM_SHIP_PSIZE.description: Diameter of the circle the ship is traveling on

SIM_SHOVE_TIME.label: Time length for shove
SIM_SHOVE_TIME.description: Force to the vehicle over a period of time

SIM_SHOVE_X.label: Acceleration of shove x
SIM_SHOVE_X.description: Acceleration of shove to vehicle in x axis

SIM_SHOVE_Y.label: Acceleration of shove y
SIM_SHOVE_Y.description: Acceleration of shove to vehicle in y axis

SIM_SHOVE_Z.label: Acceleration of shove z
SIM_SHOVE_Z.description: Acceleration of shove to vehicle in z axis

SIM_SLUP_DRAG.label: Slung Payload drag coefficient
SIM_SLUP_DRAG.description: Slung Payload drag coefficient.  Higher values increase drag and slow the payload more quickly

SIM_SLUP_LINELEN.label: Slung Payload line length
SIM_SLUP_LINELEN.description: Slung Payload line length in meters

SIM_SLUP_SYSID.label: Slung Payload MAVLink system ID
SIM_SLUP_SYSID.description: Slung Payload MAVLink system id to distinguish it from others on the same network

SIM_SONAR_GLITCH.label: Sonar glitch probablility
SIM_SONAR_GLITCH.description: Probablility a sonar glitch would happen

SIM_SONAR_POS_X.label: Sonar Offsets
SIM_SONAR_POS_X.description: XYZ position of the sonar relative to the body frame origin (X-axis)

SIM_SONAR_POS_Y.label: Sonar Offsets
SIM_SONAR_POS_Y.description: XYZ position of the sonar relative to the body frame origin (Y-axis)

SIM_SONAR_POS_Z.label: Sonar Offsets
SIM_SONAR_POS_Z.description: XYZ position of the sonar relative to the body frame origin (Z-axis)

SIM_SONAR_RND.label: Sonar noise factor
SIM_SONAR_RND.description: Scaling factor for simulated sonar noise

SIM_SONAR_SCALE.label: Sonar conversion scale
SIM_SONAR_SCALE.description: Sonar conversion scale from distance to voltage

SIM_SPEEDUP.label: Sim Speedup
SIM_SPEEDUP.description: Runs the simulation at multiples of normal speed. Do not use if realtime physics, like RealFlight, is being used

SIM_SPR_ENABLE.label: Sprayer Sim enable/disable
SIM_SPR_ENABLE.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the Sprayer simulation

SIM_SPR_PUMP.label: Sprayer pump pin
SIM_SPR_PUMP.description: The pin number that the Sprayer pump is connected to. (start at 1)

SIM_SPR_SPIN.label: Sprayer spinner servo pin
SIM_SPR_SPIN.description: The pin number that the Sprayer spinner servo is connected to. (start at 1)

SIM_TA_ENABLE.label: ToneAlarm enable/disable
SIM_TA_ENABLE.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the simulated tonealarm

SIM_TEMP_BFACTOR.label: Baro temperature factor
SIM_TEMP_BFACTOR.description: A pressure change with temperature that closely matches what has been observed with a ICM-20789

SIM_TEMP_BRD_OFF.label: Baro temperature offset
SIM_TEMP_BRD_OFF.description: Barometer board temperature offset from atmospheric temperature

SIM_TETH_DENSITY.label: Tether Wire Density
SIM_TETH_DENSITY.description: Linear mass density of the tether wire

SIM_TETH_DMPCNST.label: Tether Damping Constant
SIM_TETH_DMPCNST.description: Damping constant for the tether to simulate resistance based on change in stretch

SIM_TETH_ENABLE.label: Tether Simulation Enable/Disable
SIM_TETH_ENABLE.description: Enable or disable the tether simulation

SIM_TETH_LINELEN.label: Tether Maximum Line Length
SIM_TETH_LINELEN.description: Maximum length of the tether line in meters

SIM_TETH_SPGCNST.label: Tether Spring Constant
SIM_TETH_SPGCNST.description: Spring constant for the tether to simulate elastic forces when stretched beyond its maximum length

SIM_TETH_STUCK.label: Tether Stuck Enable/Disable
SIM_TETH_STUCK.description: Enable or disable a stuck tether simulation

SIM_TETH_SYSID.label: Tether Simulation MAVLink System ID
SIM_TETH_SYSID.description: MAVLink system ID for the tether simulation, used to distinguish it from other systems on the network

SIM_THML_SCENARI.label: Thermal scenarios
SIM_THML_SCENARI.description: Scenario for thermalling simulation, for soaring

SIM_TIDE_DIR.label: Tide direction
SIM_TIDE_DIR.description: Tide direction wave is coming from

SIM_TIME_JITTER.label: Loop time jitter
SIM_TIME_JITTER.description: Upper limit of random jitter in loop time

SIM_TWIST_TIME.label: Twist time
SIM_TWIST_TIME.description: Time that twist is applied on the vehicle

SIM_TWIST_X.label: Twist x
SIM_TWIST_X.description: Rotational acceleration of twist x axis

SIM_TWIST_Y.label: Twist y
SIM_TWIST_Y.description: Rotational acceleration of twist y axis

SIM_TWIST_Z.label: Twist z
SIM_TWIST_Z.description: Rotational acceleration of twist z axis

SIM_UART_LOSS.label: UART byte loss percentage
SIM_UART_LOSS.description: Sets percentage of outgoing byte loss on UARTs

SIM_VIB_FREQ_X.label: Vibration frequency
SIM_VIB_FREQ_X.description: Frequency of vibration applied to IMU readings in SITL (X-axis)

SIM_VIB_FREQ_Y.label: Vibration frequency
SIM_VIB_FREQ_Y.description: Frequency of vibration applied to IMU readings in SITL (Y-axis)

SIM_VIB_FREQ_Z.label: Vibration frequency
SIM_VIB_FREQ_Z.description: Frequency of vibration applied to IMU readings in SITL (Z-axis)

SIM_VIB_MOT_MASK.label: Motor mask
SIM_VIB_MOT_MASK.description: Motor mask, allowing external simulators to mark motors

SIM_VIB_MOT_MAX.label: Max motor vibration frequency
SIM_VIB_MOT_MAX.description: Max frequency to use as baseline for adding motor noise for the gyros and accels

SIM_VIB_MOT_MULT.label: Vibration motor scale
SIM_VIB_MOT_MULT.description: Amplitude scaling of motor noise relative to gyro/accel noise

SIM_VICON_POS_X.label: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Forward direction
SIM_VICON_POS_X.description: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Forward direction

SIM_VICON_POS_Y.label: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Right direction
SIM_VICON_POS_Y.description: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Right direction

SIM_VICON_POS_Z.label: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Down direction
SIM_VICON_POS_Z.description: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Down direction

SIM_VICON_P_SD.label: SITL vicon position standard deviation for gaussian noise
SIM_VICON_P_SD.description: SITL vicon position standard deviation for gaussian noise

SIM_VICON_QUAL.label: SITL vicon odometry quality
SIM_VICON_QUAL.description: SITL vicon odometry quality field sent in MAVLink ODOMETRY message (-1=failure, 0=unknown, 1-100=quality)

SIM_VICON_V_SD.label: SITL vicon velocity standard deviation for gaussian noise
SIM_VICON_V_SD.description: SITL vicon velocity standard deviation for gaussian noise

SIM_VICON_YAW.label: SITL vicon yaw angle in earth frame
SIM_VICON_YAW.description: SITL vicon yaw angle in earth frame

SIM_VICON_YAWERR.label: SITL vicon yaw error
SIM_VICON_YAWERR.description: SITL vicon yaw added to reported yaw sent to vehicle

SIM_VOLZ_ENA.label: Volz simulator enable/disable
SIM_VOLZ_ENA.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the Volz simulator

SIM_VOLZ_FMASK.label: Volz fail mask
SIM_VOLZ_FMASK.description: fail servo at current position.  Channel 1 is bit 0.

SIM_VOLZ_MASK.label: Volz override mask
SIM_VOLZ_MASK.description: mask of servo output channels to override with values from Volz protocol.  Note these are indexed from 0 - so channel 3 (usually throttle) has value 4 in this bitmask (1<<2).

SIM_WIND_DIR.label: Direction simulated wind is coming from
SIM_WIND_DIR.description: Allows you to set wind direction (true deg) in sim

SIM_WIND_DIR_Z.label: Simulated wind vertical direction
SIM_WIND_DIR_Z.description: Allows you to set vertical wind direction (true deg) in sim. 0 means pure horizontal wind. 90 means pure updraft.

SIM_WIND_SPD.label: Simulated Wind speed
SIM_WIND_SPD.description: Allows you to emulate wind in sim

SIM_WIND_T.label: Wind Profile Type
SIM_WIND_T.description: Selects how wind varies from surface to WIND_T_ALT

SIM_WIND_TC.label: Wind variation time constant
SIM_WIND_TC.description: this controls the time over which wind changes take effect

SIM_WIND_TURB.label: Simulated Wind variation
SIM_WIND_TURB.description: Allows you to emulate random wind variations in sim

SIM_WIND_T_ALT.label: Full Wind Altitude
SIM_WIND_T_ALT.description: Altitude at which wind reaches full strength, decaying from full strength as altitude lowers to ground level

SIM_WIND_T_COEF.label: Linear Wind Curve Coeff
SIM_WIND_T_COEF.description: For linear wind profile,wind is reduced by (Altitude-WIND_T_ALT) x this value

SIM_WOW_PIN.label: Weight on Wheels Pin
SIM_WOW_PIN.description: SITL set this simulated pin to true if vehicle is on ground

SLUP_DEBUG.label: Slung Payload debug output
SLUP_DEBUG.description: Slung payload debug output, set to 1 to enable debug

SLUP_DIST_MAX.label: Slung Payload horizontal distance max
SLUP_DIST_MAX.description: Oscillation is suppressed when vehicle and payload are no more than this distance horizontally.  Set to 0 to always suppress

SLUP_RESTOFS_TC.label: Slung Payload resting offset estimate filter time constant
SLUP_RESTOFS_TC.description: payload's position estimator's time constant used to compensate for GPS errors and wind.  Higher values result in smoother estimate but slower response

SLUP_SYSID.label: Slung Payload mavlink system id
SLUP_SYSID.description: Slung Payload mavlink system id.  0 to use any/all system ids

SLUP_VEL_P.label: Slung Payload Velocity P gain
SLUP_VEL_P.description: Slung Payload Velocity P gain, higher values will result in faster movements in sync with payload

SLUP_WP_POS_P.label: Slung Payload return to WP position P gain
SLUP_WP_POS_P.description: WP position P gain. higher values will result in vehicle moving more quickly back to the original waypoint

SPRAY_ENABLE.label: Sprayer enable/disable
SPRAY_ENABLE.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the sprayer

SPRAY_PUMP_MIN.label: Pump speed minimum
SPRAY_PUMP_MIN.description: Minimum pump speed expressed as a percentage

SPRAY_PUMP_RATE.label: Pump speed
SPRAY_PUMP_RATE.description: Desired pump speed when travelling 1m/s expressed as a percentage

SPRAY_SPEED_MIN.label: Speed minimum
SPRAY_SPEED_MIN.description: Speed minimum at which we will begin spraying

SPRAY_SPINNER.label: Spinner rotation speed
SPRAY_SPINNER.description: Spinner's rotation speed in PWM (a higher rate will disperse the spray over a wider area horizontally)

SRTL_ACCURACY.label: SmartRTL accuracy
SRTL_ACCURACY.description: SmartRTL accuracy. The minimum distance between points.

SRTL_POINTS.label: SmartRTL maximum number of points on path
SRTL_POINTS.description: SmartRTL maximum number of points on path. Set to 0 to disable SmartRTL.  100 points consumes about 3k of memory.

STAT_BOOTCNT.label: Boot Count
STAT_BOOTCNT.description: Number of times board has been booted

STAT_DISTFLWN.label: Total Distance Flown
STAT_DISTFLWN.description: Estimate of total distance flown since statistics reset

STAT_RESET.label: Statistics Reset Time
STAT_RESET.description: Seconds since January 1st 2016 (Unix epoch+1451606400) since statistics reset (set to 0 to reset statistics, other set values will be ignored)

SUPER_SIMPLE.label: Super Simple Mode
SUPER_SIMPLE.description: Bitmask to enable Super Simple mode for some flight modes. Setting this to Disabled(0) will disable Super Simple Mode. The bitmask is for flight mode switch positions

SURFTRAK_MODE.label: Surface Tracking Mode
SURFTRAK_MODE.description: set which surface to track in surface tracking

SURFTRAK_TC.label: Surface Tracking Filter Time Constant
SURFTRAK_TC.description: Time to achieve 63.2% of the surface altitude measurement change.  If 0 filtering is disabled

TA_ACT_FN.label: Activation Function for Terrain Avoidance
TA_ACT_FN.description: Setting an RC channel's _OPTION to this value will use it for Terrain Avoidance enable/disable

TA_ALT_MAX.label: ceiling for pitching/quading
TA_ALT_MAX.description: This is a limit on how high the terrain avoidane will take the vehicle. It acts a failsafe to prevent vertical flyaways.

TA_CMTC_ENABLE.label: CMTC Enable
TA_CMTC_ENABLE.description: Whether to enable Can't Make That Climb while running Terrain Avoidance

TA_CMTC_HGT.label: CMTC Height
TA_CMTC_HGT.description: The minimum Height above terrain to maintain when following an AUTO mission or RTL. If zero(0) use TA_PTCH_DOW_MIN.

TA_CMTC_RAD.label: CMTC loiter radius
TA_CMTC_RAD.description: Use this radius for the loiter when trying to gain altitude. If not set or <=0 use WP_LOITER_RAD

TA_GSP_AIRBRAKE.label: Groudspeed Airbrake limt
TA_GSP_AIRBRAKE.description: This is the limit for triggering airbrake to slow groundspeed as a difference between the airspeed and groundspeed. -1 for disabled.

TA_GSP_MAX.label: Maximum Groundspeed
TA_GSP_MAX.description: This is a limit on how fast in groundspeeed terrain avoidance will take the vehicle. This is to allow for reliable sensor readings. -1 for disabled.

TA_HOME_DIST.label: safe distance around home
TA_HOME_DIST.description: Terrain avoidance will not be applied if the vehicle is less than this distance from home

TA_PTCH_DWN_MIN.label: down distance minimum for Pitching
TA_PTCH_DWN_MIN.description: If the downward distance is less than this value then start Pitching up to gain altitude.

TA_PTCH_FWD_MIN.label: forward distance minimum for Pitching
TA_PTCH_FWD_MIN.description: If the farwardward distance is less than this value then start Pitching up to gain altitude.

TA_PTCH_GSP_MIN.label: minimum ground speed for Pitching
TA_PTCH_GSP_MIN.description: Minimum Groundspeed (not airspeed) to be flying for Pitching to be used.

TA_PTCH_TIMEOUT.label: timeout Pitching
TA_PTCH_TIMEOUT.description: Minimum down or forward distance must be triggered for more than this many seconds to start Pitching

TA_QUAD_DWN_MIN.label: Downward distance minimum Quading
TA_QUAD_DWN_MIN.description: If the downward distance is less than this value then start Quading up to gain altitude.

TA_QUAD_FWD_MIN.label: minimum forward distance for Quading
TA_QUAD_FWD_MIN.description: If the farwardward distance is less than this value then start Quading up to gain altitude.

TCAL_BARO_EXP.label: Temperature Calibration barometer exponent
TCAL_BARO_EXP.description: Learned exponent for barometer temperature correction

TCAL_ENABLED.label: Temperature calibration enable
TCAL_ENABLED.description: Enable temperature calibration. Set to 0 to disable. Set to 1 to use learned values. Set to 2 to learn new values and use the values

TCAL_TEMP_MAX.label: Temperature calibration max learned temperature
TCAL_TEMP_MAX.description: Maximum learned temperature. This is automatically set by the learning process

TCAL_TEMP_MIN.label: Temperature calibration min learned temperature
TCAL_TEMP_MIN.description: Minimum learned temperature. This is automatically set by the learning process

TEMP10_A0.label: Temperature sensor analog 0th polynomial coefficient
TEMP10_A0.description: a0 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP10_A1.label: Temperature sensor analog 1st polynomial coefficient
TEMP10_A1.description: a1 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP10_A2.label: Temperature sensor analog 2nd polynomial coefficient
TEMP10_A2.description: a2 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP10_A3.label: Temperature sensor analog 3rd polynomial coefficient
TEMP10_A3.description: a3 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP10_A4.label: Temperature sensor analog 4th polynomial coefficient
TEMP10_A4.description: a4 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP10_A5.label: Temperature sensor analog 5th polynomial coefficient
TEMP10_A5.description: a5 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP10_ADDR.label: Temperature sensor address
TEMP10_ADDR.description: Temperature sensor address, typically used for I2C address

TEMP10_BUS.label: Temperature sensor bus
TEMP10_BUS.description: Temperature sensor bus number, typically used to select from multiple I2C buses

TEMP10_MSG_ID.label: Temperature sensor DroneCAN message ID
TEMP10_MSG_ID.description: Sets the message device ID this backend listens for

TEMP10_PIN.label: Temperature sensor analog voltage sensing pin
TEMP10_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for temprature monitoring. Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

TEMP10_RTD_NOM.label: Nominal RTD resistance
TEMP10_RTD_NOM.description: Nominal RTD resistance used to calculate temperature, typically 100 or 1000 ohms.

TEMP10_RTD_REF.label: RTD reference resistance
TEMP10_RTD_REF.description: Reference resistance used to calculate temperature, in ohms

TEMP10_SRC.label: Sensor Source
TEMP10_SRC.description: Sensor Source is used to designate which device's temperature report will be replaced by this temperature sensor's data. If 0 (None) then the data is only available via log. In the future a new Motor temperature report will be created for returning data directly.

TEMP10_SRC_ID.label: Sensor Source Identification
TEMP10_SRC_ID.description: Sensor Source Identification is used to replace a specific instance of a system component's temperature report with the temp sensor's. Examples: TEMP_SRC = 1 (ESC), TEMP_SRC_ID = 1 will set the temp of ESC1. TEMP_SRC = 3 (BatteryIndex),TEMP_SRC_ID = 2 will set the temp of BATT2. TEMP_SRC = 4 (BatteryId/SerialNum),TEMP_SRC_ID=42 will set the temp of all batteries that have param BATTn_SERIAL = 42.

TEMP11_A0.label: Temperature sensor analog 0th polynomial coefficient
TEMP11_A0.description: a0 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP11_A1.label: Temperature sensor analog 1st polynomial coefficient
TEMP11_A1.description: a1 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP11_A2.label: Temperature sensor analog 2nd polynomial coefficient
TEMP11_A2.description: a2 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP11_A3.label: Temperature sensor analog 3rd polynomial coefficient
TEMP11_A3.description: a3 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP11_A4.label: Temperature sensor analog 4th polynomial coefficient
TEMP11_A4.description: a4 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP11_A5.label: Temperature sensor analog 5th polynomial coefficient
TEMP11_A5.description: a5 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP11_ADDR.label: Temperature sensor address
TEMP11_ADDR.description: Temperature sensor address, typically used for I2C address

TEMP11_BUS.label: Temperature sensor bus
TEMP11_BUS.description: Temperature sensor bus number, typically used to select from multiple I2C buses

TEMP11_MSG_ID.label: Temperature sensor DroneCAN message ID
TEMP11_MSG_ID.description: Sets the message device ID this backend listens for

TEMP11_PIN.label: Temperature sensor analog voltage sensing pin
TEMP11_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for temprature monitoring. Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

TEMP11_RTD_NOM.label: Nominal RTD resistance
TEMP11_RTD_NOM.description: Nominal RTD resistance used to calculate temperature, typically 100 or 1000 ohms.

TEMP11_SRC.label: Sensor Source
TEMP11_SRC.description: Sensor Source is used to designate which device's temperature report will be replaced by this temperature sensor's data. If 0 (None) then the data is only available via log. In the future a new Motor temperature report will be created for returning data directly.

TEMP11_SRC_ID.label: Sensor Source Identification
TEMP11_SRC_ID.description: Sensor Source Identification is used to replace a specific instance of a system component's temperature report with the temp sensor's. Examples: TEMP_SRC = 1 (ESC), TEMP_SRC_ID = 1 will set the temp of ESC1. TEMP_SRC = 3 (BatteryIndex),TEMP_SRC_ID = 2 will set the temp of BATT2. TEMP_SRC = 4 (BatteryId/SerialNum),TEMP_SRC_ID=42 will set the temp of all batteries that have param BATTn_SERIAL = 42.

TEMP5_A5.label: Temperature sensor analog 5th polynomial coefficient
TEMP5_A5.description: a5 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP5_ADDR.label: Temperature sensor address
TEMP5_ADDR.description: Temperature sensor address, typically used for I2C address

TEMP5_BUS.label: Temperature sensor bus
TEMP5_BUS.description: Temperature sensor bus number, typically used to select from multiple I2C buses

TEMP5_MSG_ID.label: Temperature sensor DroneCAN message ID
TEMP5_MSG_ID.description: Sets the message device ID this backend listens for

TEMP5_PIN.label: Temperature sensor analog voltage sensing pin
TEMP5_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for temprature monitoring. Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

TEMP5_RTD_NOM.label: Nominal RTD resistance
TEMP5_RTD_NOM.description: Nominal RTD resistance used to calculate temperature, typically 100 or 1000 ohms.

TEMP5_RTD_REF.label: RTD reference resistance
TEMP5_RTD_REF.description: Reference resistance used to calculate temperature, in ohms

TEMP5_SRC.label: Sensor Source
TEMP5_SRC.description: Sensor Source is used to designate which device's temperature report will be replaced by this temperature sensor's data. If 0 (None) then the data is only available via log. In the future a new Motor temperature report will be created for returning data directly.

TEMP5_SRC_ID.label: Sensor Source Identification
TEMP5_SRC_ID.description: Sensor Source Identification is used to replace a specific instance of a system component's temperature report with the temp sensor's. Examples: TEMP_SRC = 1 (ESC), TEMP_SRC_ID = 1 will set the temp of ESC1. TEMP_SRC = 3 (BatteryIndex),TEMP_SRC_ID = 2 will set the temp of BATT2. TEMP_SRC = 4 (BatteryId/SerialNum),TEMP_SRC_ID=42 will set the temp of all batteries that have param BATTn_SERIAL = 42.
